We didn’t make use of the bowling alley but that looked like a lot of fun.
Great stay! Would recommend. 👌🏼

Really disappointed.

google_reviews.author: Andrej Galoić
google_reviews.text: It’s a good place to drink coffee but damn, it’s so much people there and impossible to find a table. We needed to wait some minutes for a table to be freed up. And the tables are very close to each other so it doesn’t give a feeling of privacy/intimacy.

google_reviews.author: Stephen Ellwood
google_reviews.text: Very trendy, bustling Italian restaurant. We were lucky to get a table. Food is really good, Prices are a little on the high side but ok.

google_reviews.author: Jean-Philippe Andreu
google_reviews.text:
We ordered a Heringsschmaus for 2 (as take away because of the COVID restrictions).
We got:
- 1x Maki (and I mean 1 single Maki, not a set of Makis)
- 1x Nigiri (schrimp) (again 1 single Nigiri)
- 1x sashimi (Tuna) (again 1 single sashimi))
- 3 different herring salads (~200ml each)
- 1x octopus salad (~200ml)
- 1x shrimps cocktail (~200ml)
- 1x ceviche with 1 finger sized piece of salmon, sword fish and sea bass
- Some focaccia bread
- Tiramisu for 2
For 78€ I was expecting a treat. The overall quantity was barely OK and the quality pretty average.
There was not a lot of fish all together and the shrimps cocktail was seriously lacking of shrimps.
Guys, I understand the situation with the COVID is not an easy one for restaurants but I felt completely ripped off.
Half of the price would have been roughly OK but for nearly 80€ I could have had 4x sashimi moriawase (big portions) at the yamamoto sushibar (one of the best sushi restaurants in Graz)!
So to conclude: I never ever will again order something at this place.

restaurants.name: Philipp

google_reviews.author: Anna Antoniou
google_reviews.text: I came here as a tourist and I’m honestly so glad I did. I tried the ice cream and a couple of desserts, and they were so good and super traditional. The interior inside the shop is beautiful too, and the vibe in the shop is just really nice and heartwarming. I left really happy and I’m 100% planning to come back

Went to have a look at the cafe and get a takeaway cake and krapfen. The interior looks quite aged and kitschy. Similar to an old American diner. Many colours of the cakes look quite bright and unnatural. The service was very friendly though.
I had a slice of the Steirer cake which was supposed to have alcohol in it but unfortunately didn’t taste of much. The chocolate mousse was dense and dry. Generally it just tasted of sugar but not much else. I’d say the whole place could really do with a rebrand.

google_reviews.author: Yazan M Rihan

A wide collection of Gluten Free (glutenfrei) options are available too, as it is in the picture.

we had ice cream and it test like sugar water .

restaurants.name: Gösser Bräu
restaurants.summary: Casual, enduring eatery with a beer garden serving comfort food, plus salads, soups & desserts.

google_reviews.author: Boris Bardak
google_reviews.text: Perfect food, house beer excelent, staff also perfect! Smoked ribs are something that you will remember. They are bit pricey but they are worth every euro!

google_reviews.author: Brian B
google_reviews.text:
A nice restaurant with a pleasant atmosphere. The food itself was good and came out as expected, no complaints there.

One odd part of the experience: when we were seated, there was an old, stale pretzel already sitting on the table. On a little wooden hanger. At the end of the meal, they tried to charge us for it, even though we hadn’t ordered or touched it. The charge was removed once we pointed it out, but definitely odd.

Overall, decent food and a nice setting, but the pretzel incident was unusual.

google_reviews.author: Dror Alon Digital
google_reviews.text:
I’ve been to countless pubs and breweries across Europe, but few capture the soul of local tradition like Gösser Bräu in Graz. As a seasoned tourist and amateur historian, I’m always drawn to places that preserve their roots while staying vibrant — and this one does it effortlessly. The wooden interiors, the cheerful noise of locals, and the slow rhythm of a long afternoon beer all feel authentically Styrian.

I ordered the Gösser Bräu Schnitzel — crisp, golden perfection — and paired it with a dark Gösser beer that reminded me why Austrian brewing is a craft of balance and patience. The portions are generous, the flavors honest, and the service friendly without pretense.

google_reviews.author: Martha Milch
google_reviews.text: As a world-traveling culinary enthusiast, stepping into Gösser Bräu feels like entering the heart of Graz’s brewing heritage—especially with its charming rooftop beer garden nestled under a chestnut tree canopy. The interior blends sturdy brewery tables with warm lighting, perfect for a comforting evening. The menu, freshly updated for August 2025, features gems like the historic Gösser Bräu Gulasch since 1902 with potatoes, Frankfurter, pickles and a sunny fried egg; creamy Käsespätzle crowned with mountain cheese and crunchy onions; and the ever-cozy Frittatensuppe and Apfelstrudel with whipped cream. The beer selection is impressive—eight taps showcasing local brews that pair effortlessly with each dish. Service stands out—warm, attentive, and genuine—adding to the relaxed, convivial atmosphere. Whether it’s enjoying a sumptuous schnitzel in the rooftop garden or sipping a crisp lager indoors, Gösser Bräu delivers an authentic, hearty taste of Styrian hospitality. A must-visit for those who want tradition, flavor, and atmosphere all in one memorable outing.

google_reviews.author: Sapir
google_reviews.text:
At first we came str8 for the beer, and it was incredible!
But we were surprised that the menu felt good and the food was very tasty (the mushroom's soup) and the bagel with the typical cheese spread - it went really good with the beer.
Also the waiters were really nice, and honest.
The only missing thing was that the food felt a bit 'tired' cuz we arrived kind of late.

google_reviews.author: Iztok Žgalin
google_reviews.text:
Very nice open terrace on the Tower Hill. Limited selection but good food. Recommend.
LEO♥️

google_reviews.author: Jasmin Hussain
google_reviews.text: Beautiful view with a relaxing atmosphere. It's great for a short break. We got a Apfelstrudel which was great but be aware that you have to order vanilla ice cream or sauce extra and pay additionally for it.

google_reviews.author: Kimberly G James
google_reviews.text: Great place to spend a few hours (you can reserve for 2 hours), enjoy the view,  and people watch. Very good service. I recommend paying to take the lift up to the restaurant unless you want a short, intense workout on the way to your meal.

google_reviews.author: Harper Maci
google_reviews.text: Modern restaurant perched on a hill overlooking the city. The food is elegant yet approachable, and the view makes the whole experience feel special. Perfect for a slow dinner.

google_reviews.author: Josh C
google_reviews.text: Best asset is the view of the city. Being a tourist trap, the service is far from optimal. Be ready to be treated not with the highest welcoming spirit and also be ready for anything you want to take long time (10 minutes to even be acknowledged, 22 minutes to get someone to take your order, rather having to stand up and say walk to the exit to pay because no one will bring you the check).  Food is standard so don’t expect anything amazing.

google_reviews.author: Mateja Košak
google_reviews.text: Ambience in a wonderful retro style. Friendly staff, good atmosphere and excellent coffee. Payment by cash only. I haven't tried the food, so I can't rate it.

google_reviews.text: What a place !! 🥰 the owner is amazing!! The food is delicious. If you are looking for a calm place to chill that’s your base. 💛

google_reviews.text: Very nice atmosphere, cozy, beautiful interior with character. The staff was friendly, there's a variety of drinks and they even have some food during the lunch time. Great to sit outside on the warmer days.

google_reviews.author: Lorenz Leitner
google_reviews.text: Very casual and cool place. You can even order in food from other places if you’re there outside their lunch times. I guess that’s why it’s called “living room”.

google_reviews.text: Since it turned to non-smoking, I'm there very often because it's a super awesome place if you like rock music.

google_reviews.text: Very nice service staff, courteous, helpful, polite, pleasant guests and LGBTQ friendly!

google_reviews.author: Rafael Paiotti
google_reviews.text: Good beer, nice music (metal), fair prices and non smoking place; I strongly recommend it!

google_reviews.author: R Lin
google_reviews.text:
This is my dear friend’s favorite coffee shop in Graz, and I totally understand why. Not only is their coffee strong and smooth, and their food delicious, but it’s such a nice place to hang out.

There’s indoor and outdoor seating, and we sat near the window to be able to look outside and people watch. It was so pleasant.

I also loved the baristas made a little smile face art on the latte I ordered. It was so good, smooth and tasty. They had a little bar where you could add sugar and some flavorings to the coffee (my American self very appreciated this). They even had chocolate powders.

Last, the bagel sandwich was YUM. A poppyseed bagel with pesto, tomato, spinach and egg, so fresh and delicious. They had a lot of tempting baked goods and sweets as well.

google_reviews.author: Sonia Sortan
google_reviews.text: The tastiest cheesecake (we’ve eaten it three days in a row haha), super attentive service, great variety of bagels, water with apple/carrot/lemon/mint at your discretion, perfect location, nothing else needed 🤍

google_reviews.author: Edward Rodrigues
google_reviews.text: Went for breakfast here this morning. It's where the locals hangout for good wholesome Jewish type bagels and coffee.  They do have sweet desserts as well. Value for money and inexpensive.  I strongly recommend this place if you are not a big eater and searching for a quick bite.

google_reviews.author: Michael Walsh
google_reviews.text: Cappuccino and a bagel were very good. Service was friendly. Atmosphere great for relaxing, chatting or working.

google_reviews.author: Floor
google_reviews.text: Great coffee and lovely spot to enjoy the winter sun. Really liked their bagels, I thought the one with Weisswurst was super creative and tasted great!

google_reviews.author: ひうら
google_reviews.text: The drink I was served looked similar to what I ordered, but it turned out to be something different. Without realizing it, I drank it, and only when I went to pay did I find out that I had been charged for a more expensive item. When I mentioned it to a staff, he simply said that since I had drunk it, I had to pay. I paid reluctantly. The price difference wasn’t large, but I was very disappointed by the dishonest and unprofessional way the situation was handled. It was especially disappointing because the drink itself was good.

google_reviews.author: Aigul H
google_reviews.text: Boss himself made an omelet, that I will never forget, with ham and cheese, very tasty. Highly recommend to try it. I have never eaten such omelet in my life. Thank you

google_reviews.author: MotoTouring World Wide
google_reviews.text: I wish the owner would remove the graffiti off the Wall especially the new big one. The small graffiti halfway up the wall has been on the wall for 5 years and it is annoying. Please remove and don't let the graffiti thugs win.

google_reviews.author: Erika Csuportová
google_reviews.text: It was really wholesome experience. The waiter was really kind to us with lovely smile. Great coffee and delicious cakes. I will recommend this place to my friends ❤️

google_reviews.author: Sez it Reght
google_reviews.text: Great cafe in the centre time to sit recharge and have a tea or a beer or cake. Great service and price

google_reviews.author: Bernhard Lang
google_reviews.text:
The food and service was excellent, we celebrated our daughter’s birthday and couldn’t have picked a better place. Our waiter Laszlo went above and beyond to make this a special evening, and we greatly appreciate his attention.
The food was really tasty, and one of the best meals we had in Graz and Austria on our trip. We definitely can recommend this restaurant and will return for sure in the future.

google_reviews.author: Gosia Rudnik
google_reviews.text: It was our second visit in this restaurant. The first one was in the summer and it was a pleasant experience. Unfortunately, this time we were really disappointed. We arrived at the restaurant quite late and already asked at the door if we can still be served drinks. We were told that we can take the seats and will be served shortly. We sat at the table that the staff directed us to. Table was still dirty with dirty glasses after the previous guests. There were clean and unoccupied tables available however we were directed to the dirty one. We waited for someone to clean and take our drink order for over 15’. Since nobody was interested in serving us, my partner approached the bar manager (who showed us the table) and politely asked if we for sure can still be served since he overheard that the manager told one of the staff that nobody is to be served anymore. After the manager heard the question, he replied with in a very angry manner that they are busy and in any case there is only 30’ left to finish the drinks. (Drinks that haven’t even been ordered yet). My partner got back to the table. We decided to leave since it seemed to be such a big deal to have a glass of wine served in this place. When the manager realized we were about to leave he just shouted toward us „what should I do, we are stressed”. I would like to mention that the place wasn’t even half full. Our first visit was very nice and the food was really tasty that is why we decided to go back and have a drink in a nice place. Sadly, we won’t be coming back anytime soon.

google_reviews.author: frederic vicentini
google_reviews.text: Nice traditional austrian cuisine with a gastronomy twist. Took goose fillet with la chasse vegetable, don t miss the local dessert

google_reviews.author: Rosi K
google_reviews.text: Ingeneral good place but I am not sure why they serve half of Burata on the Burata salad.

google_reviews.author: Sandra Johnston
google_reviews.text: Delicious food and huge portions!! The server was very kind and accomodating with our walk in group of 6. Despite language barriers we managed to place our orders and the server was very kind and patient. The food took some time to arrive but was completely worth the wait. Fresh, delicious and generous portions. The prices were reasonable for what you get and for the quiet relaxed atmosphere for a family gathering to catch up. Thank you!

restaurants.name: Frankowitsch
restaurants.summary: Historic, family-run establishment offering fancy pastries, sandwiches with cured meats & more.

google_reviews.author: Vanessa L.
google_reviews.text:
Excellent service and great food. We came here because the place was recommended to us as tourists visiting Graz.
We cannot eat egg and garlic and the lady behind the counter was super helpful. Thanks a lot we really appreciated it

google_reviews.author: Joann Mistica
google_reviews.text: The bread is great. I can probably eat it as is and still enjoy it. With the toppings though, it's just perfect! If we lived closer to this place, we would buy here regularly. Simple, pretty, and delicious.

google_reviews.author: bluebardot
google_reviews.text: This is probably the most high end place in Graz. It doesn't get better than that. Food and drinks are excellent. Staff is friendly, efficient and go all the way to make your visit a memorable one. I am regular customer.

google_reviews.author: M.M Marie
google_reviews.text: This place was recommended by an acquaintance and it must have been my most favorite place to eat in Graz! The open faced sandwiches were delicious and I regret that we only ate there once. If I'm ever in Graz I would definitely return. Highly recommended!!!

google_reviews.author: Matthias Gutmann
google_reviews.text: Street life at it’s best, humming atmosphere ‘n’ all

google_reviews.author: Pawel

The food itself was absolutely superb, perfectly complemented by a delightful organic Red Bull cola. Highly recommended.

google_reviews.author: Silja Reitzer
google_reviews.text: Food is amazing and price point is good. Highly recommend the shrimp soup, had to go back another time just to have it again - perfect balance of spice and flavour. Vegan spicy mango dish was sadly lacking in sauce so ended up being quite dry. Cute place overall with a big menu. Highly recommend!

google_reviews.author: Mike Szarmach
google_reviews.text: This one was one of the worst food we had. A group of 6 people today… at 17:00 out of beef on a Saturday. Our food did not taste like Thai food. I asked gut spiced fresh chilly and they don’t have fresh chilly. So how can you cook Thai food and don’t have chilly. Service was bad as well… going tithe bathroom and looking in the kitchen said it all.

google_reviews.author: HOP HIP
google_reviews.text:
We've ordered 3 cacao's and 1 cappuccino XL. Capuccino was good, very strong but good.

Cacao's were awful, they were too light. They tasted like a hot milk with some taste of cacao, I've got a feeling that I got a tea spoon of nesquik with hot milk. I was so disappointed, do not order cacao here.

google_reviews.author: Rodney Torres-Mabe
google_reviews.text: Awesome patio with good drinks. My wallet almost fell out of my pocket and one of the staff alerted me

google_reviews.author: Jánosné Rocskai

google_reviews.author: Manuel Hödl
google_reviews.text: It was wonderful

restaurants.name: Murinsel Café
restaurants.summary: Contemporary floating structure featuring a café, theater & striking nighttime illumination.

google_reviews.author: Gyorgy Rakoczy
google_reviews.text: A little pearl in the middle of the city and the river. There's a nice cafe bar indoor and outdoor as well. Check out murinselgraz homepage for concerts and events available in the outdoor part. The island is accessible from both sides of the river, it's worth to just cross it.

google_reviews.author: Mia Parovic
google_reviews.text: Murinsel Café is a great place with a cozy and relaxed atmosphere. The staff is friendly, the prices are reasonable, and the architecture is truly unique — a floating structure in the middle of the Mur river that makes the whole experience special. Whether you're just grabbing a coffee or taking a break while exploring Graz, it's definitely worth a visit!

google_reviews.author: Oksana Sukhenko
google_reviews.text: It's so beautiful and not typical architecture.  However,  it was empty the day I visited it

google_reviews.text: Just walked thru, perfect for nice pictures. Brilliant architecture in the middle of the river. You can have a coffee inside, we didn’t but it seems nice and they have nice looking desserts.

google_reviews.author: Platinum Guide
google_reviews.text: The exterior is nice. There is a coffee shop inside. The sound of the river is magnificent. It is such a pleasant building.

google_reviews.author: Mario Mitterer
google_reviews.text:
The worst experience with foodora! Ordered food, received confirmation, waited, and then after 40 minutes received a cancellation notice!!!!

Opening hours are different on every platform!

Nowhere to be found with a phone number!

We were very hungry and disappointed!!!!!!!

Send me an email if you (the staff) have any ideas!!!!

google_reviews.author: Stojan Cvitković
google_reviews.text: Loved the atmosphere on saturday night. If you come in a bit later, it might be hard to find a table. Definitely the kind of spot I’d visit all the time if I lived in Graz

google_reviews.author: Lucas Pereira
google_reviews.text: We experienced the best service in Graz in this place. The food was delicious, but the service was totally out of ordinary. We were served by two very professional and friendly ladies. Always smiling and attention to details even when the placed was fully crowded. Highly recommended.

google_reviews.author: Elena Torres
google_reviews.text: I spent 5 months here in Graz, and flanns quickly became a fan favorite!! We spent multiple birthdays here and the service was always top tier. Specifically Leo, the kindest waiter I have ever had. They made us feel like we were always at home and welcome no matter how busy they were. If in Graz, go to flanns and ask for Leo!! You will not be disappointed.

google_reviews.author: Micki Thorson
google_reviews.text: A delicious Irish pub with a relaxing atmosphere. I ordered the Guinness Stew which was the best I think I've ever tried. I also had their cider & shandy. The shandy wasn't my favorite, it tasted floral like which I'm typically not a fan of but my partner liked it. The cider I got was whatever one they had on tap & it was very good. Very typical European dining experience here so you will have to wave your server to come over but I like that experience over being checked up on constantly like in the US.

google_reviews.author: Justin Lees
google_reviews.text: İnterior is best "Irish pub" design/branding I have seen. But apart from that this is a great eatery and pub  with lovely staff, food and drink selection. Been 3 times so far and every visit is friendly fun. Obviously you have to be up for the "craic" , but the staff seem to be in the vibe, even if none have ever been to Clonmel Tipperary, the centre of the Irish universe! Visiting again tonight!

google_reviews.author: Jyothish Atheendran
google_reviews.text: Conveniently located near the University and Tram stop. They accept card payments. I tried the Chicken kebab from here and it was good. They offer both Dine-In and takeout.

google_reviews.author: Arcas Novák
google_reviews.text: The food was great, the price was good and there was no extra charge for the takeaway box.

google_reviews.author: Florin Zamfir
google_reviews.text: Excellent food, great service, good prices. I would definitely recommend!

google_reviews.author: Nazariy Shkarlat
google_reviews.text: very good pizza and nice service!

google_reviews.author: Raka Prasetya
google_reviews.text: The service was fast. the food was good. The language barrier did not bother me that much

google_reviews.author: Maryam Mehdi
google_reviews.text: It’s was delicious, big salad portions, fresh meat and very friendly waiters. We were positively surprised from this restaurant. The only downside is that this restaurant is a bit far from city center.

google_reviews.author: Paweł G
google_reviews.text: This place is worth to visit, if you looking for something to eat or drink, staying for a while, rest in the middle of your way. This little restaurant with garden run by Greek family gives you exactly what you need: cold beer, glace of wine, good coffee and tasty food: souvlaki, gyros, schnitzel, veggie pasta or toast. Lady who's a head of this place is absolutely fantastic, helpfull and lovely person ❤️ Thank You for your kindness.

google_reviews.author: Madeleine Marshall
google_reviews.text: Food was excellent and the owners were lovely! Definitely recommend 😊

google_reviews.author: David Procházka
google_reviews.text: The staff was friendly, the food was really good. We had mix of seafood and grilled meat. Both without any issues. Just the serving times were quite slow.

google_reviews.author: none noname
google_reviews.text: Great staff, lovely ambience, delicious food - perfect price performance ratio. No haute cuisine but a down-to-earth native host with a great sense for her guests needs and expectations...

google_reviews.author: Ma Burro
google_reviews.text: Great people, great place and great food. I'm missing more vegetarian/vegan options though, they have a feta burger but having an alternative for a vegetable patty would be great. Also salads!!!

google_reviews.author: Patricia Brocker
google_reviews.text: Looks a good address but still have not been must try it out sometime.

google_reviews.author: Florentina
google_reviews.text: I was very impressed with the food. You really get the feeling that the kitchen staff is highly motivated and qualified. I was particularly taken with the crepe cake. I've never eaten such an original dessert anywhere else.

google_reviews.author: Stefanie Angerbauer
google_reviews.text: I recently celebrated my birthday at Jössas and would choose it again in a heartbeat! Both the owners and the staff are super friendly and accommodating. We had our own private room where we could play music and celebrate. The food was really good, and they have a wide selection of drinks. The outdoor area is also great and invites you to sit outside when the weather's nice. I'll definitely be back!☺️

google_reviews.author: Mathias Glatz
google_reviews.text: It was good; they have a large selection of draft beers and bottled beers. The food was also good, even though we had to wait a bit. It's certainly not cheap, but the prices are generous (beer) and not massively overpriced.

google_reviews.author: Mauricio Azevedo
google_reviews.text: Amazing experience! It was my mother’s birthday and, without request, they decorated the table and even brought a candle for a happy birthday song. The staff were incredibly nice, very kind, and lovely. The Hungarian lady is just adorable. The food is also great, we had the polenta, and a chocolate mousse. Unfortunately, the kitchen was already closed, so we could not order anything else. The environment and decoration were charming and cozy. Total worthy going!

google_reviews.author: Jonathan Staggs
google_reviews.text: Fantastic food and wine! I had the risotto nero which was simply delicious and this was matched with some handy Italian white and red wine. This restaurant has an amazing range of grappa also so that was a nice way to finish the evening.

google_reviews.author: Marko Mijatovic
google_reviews.text: Great Italian food. Not the common menu of pizza and pasta, but really traditional stuff.

google_reviews.author: Julian Konrath

google_reviews.author: Edward Bennett

google_reviews.author: L A
google_reviews.text: This is the best breakfast experience I’ve had; the bread and muesli are absolutely delicious👌 and I had the option to have everything vegan 😁

google_reviews.author: Pedro Santos
google_reviews.text: Place to expensive for what produces. Breath does not contain gold. This places together with Martin Hauer need to stop charging absenes amount of money for breath. There are bolangeries in France with better quality costing less. If someone wants a good opportunity of bussiness is simple, open a small backery in the building areas were people leave produce quality breath open at Sunday and et voila Martin Hauer and this places start closing down.

google_reviews.author: Natascha Eibl
google_reviews.text: Bakery is okay, I prefer Auer though. The food and lunch menus are really good. Also vegetarian options.

google_reviews.author: Ilana Sanveto
google_reviews.text: Everything is delicious

google_reviews.author: Irina Fishchuk
google_reviews.text: Good atmosphere and amazing Coffee!

google_reviews.author: ChunkyCherry
google_reviews.text:
Big queue and many people outside the store on a Sunday. Heard it was among the best kebabs in Graz. Thought it was decent but nothing more than that.

The bread is made to order so that’s really nice, and the bread IS also super delicious, but the fillings are a bit lackluster. I for one think that shredded carrot doesn’t belong in a kebab, but that’s just me.

Prices are amazing, super cheap and also very fast service. But definitely not the best in Graz

google_reviews.author: N. Yeoh
google_reviews.text: Small shop with only a few tables and chairs but the kebab was exceptional! This is by far the best kebab I've ever had in my entire life! The ingredients especially the vegetables are very fresh; great selection of options and really affordable too. Quality of the food is just top of the tops! Highly recommend.

google_reviews.author: Guillermo Bermejo
google_reviews.text: One of the best kebabs I've ever eaten. You can really see the meat, it isn't a meat paste like in most kebabs or like in most southern Europe. Really worth the visit: tasty and a very clean place.

google_reviews.author: Jyothish Atheendran
google_reviews.text: Tried the Falafel 🧆 Wrap 🌯 from here. It was okay. There is a small area for dining in.

google_reviews.author: Sanjin Banić
google_reviews.text:
It's already 5 years since we took our 1st kebab at Welat and....simply delicious and amazing! There was not even 1 single time that the kebab was not good,really. Vegetables always fresh and the meat perfect. The workers and service excellent, always friendly and positive.
Simply great and amazing.
It's a pity that we can't give 6 stars instead of 5.

restaurants.name: Buschenschank Sattler
restaurants.summary: Low-key hotel with a working vineyard, casual dining, a terrace with picnic tables & a bar.

google_reviews.author: Anil Tilanthe
google_reviews.text:
Good wine and nice Austrian food.

Easily accessible by bus no 64 from city.

We went on the day of full blue moon. The view from bus stop was mesmerizing. With the Sattler and the moon coming out from the mountain.

It's also affordable. Per person price came upto only 12.20€ including wine, food and desserts.

google_reviews.author: Breakcaft
google_reviews.text: Incredibly friendly hosts. The meal was great and not too pricey. They really deserved this review, what topped it of was the apple tiramisu I definitely recommend it.

google_reviews.author: Jesús Pestana Puerta
google_reviews.text: This restaurant sells self-made foods (charcuterie, cheese,...) and drinks (fruit juices, white wine and apple cider). The restaurante is in a somewhat elevated position with greenerie and forests around it, and the possibility to seat outside enjoying the landscape. It is also reachable by bike from Graz (no separate bike lane though).

google_reviews.author: Michael Klug
google_reviews.text: Haven't been here for many years sadly. This changed today and I have to say: still good food and friendly people. Combined with a great view a nice place to visit.

google_reviews.author: Janel Zechner-Leonor
google_reviews.text: Is a nice place to eat a traditional Styrian Brettljause (charcuterie board), wine and apple cider. You can also eat other traditional warm dishes.

google_reviews.author: Tom Maier
google_reviews.text: We found this place by surprise during a walk on Sunday afternoon and were lucky enough there was still a small table for us inside. It's a very nice place, with options for sitting outside almost in/between the vineyard and a nice panoramic view ... Excellent fresh food  (Schweinebraten 5*) + good beer, vine of the house and some cake for dessert. Friendly talking with the guests and owner.

google_reviews.author: Laura Kulman
google_reviews.text: Enjoy a great view of Graz from the outdoor patio, and a quick hike either before or after lunch. The fried chicken was so crispy and juicy! Really a special treat. Get there early or order ahead if you’re planning to have the fried chicken.

google_reviews.author: Ahmet Yüksek
google_reviews.text: Food is nice the view is amazing. But the portions are rather small and thus normally nice-looking prices become expensive. I would not go there only for the restaurant. But if you are in the premises and feeling thirsty/hungry, it satisfies the need.

google_reviews.author: Lee Novak
google_reviews.text: Great atmosphere, with delicious food made local ingredients. The owners Franz & Martina are very friendly as is the team, especially Manuela. We love coming to Orthacker as we always have lots of fun with the team and are always made welcome no matter how busy the restaurant is. Highly recommend this very special place and the team speak excellent English!

google_reviews.author: Tamás Kovács
google_reviews.text: Really good view for the City, nice atmosphere, on the summer the terasse is really recommend. In our time the place was so busy but we had time. The staff was overloaded a little bit I think. But the restaurant is really authentic and I recommend it during hiking or anytime.

google_reviews.author: Manisha Choudhary
google_reviews.text: Great food and super friendly staff. We have been to this cafe several times and every time, the food especially pizza and pastas were amazing. Not to forget they also make amazing soup especially the kurbiscreme soup during the pumpkin season. Their dessert are also great.

Cash only 15/10/23

google_reviews.text:
Schillerhof - a former popular bar - is Martin Auer's latest addition to his growing bakery empire. A trendy location part bakery part café with a very cool, laid-back vibe.
Food and coffee tastes great but not as good as in other locations of the same chain. I cannot quite put my finger on it but something is missing.
Staff is at times a  bit too casual  for my taste. The outdoor seating area comes highly recommended!

google_reviews.author: Joann Mistica

The butter croissant is delicious - big and nice texture. I didn't like the filled croissants so much. I think there's too much filling and it becomes too sweet and messy. The pancakes with nougat, cream, and berries are so good! Too bad the pancakes are quite small.

The coffee is always good and the cookie that comes with it. I wish I could buy a jar of those cookies! The breakfast sets especially the ones with prosciutto are worth the price. The breads that comes with it are fresh and "crispy".

The servers are nice.

google_reviews.text: We had such great cocktails (favorite: Mojito) and food here that it feels (almost) like you are in the middle of  Cuba right now – fabulous atmosphere, sunshine music + friendly, open-minded people around – Best place to meet, drink & relax!

google_reviews.author: Kristjan Jekimov
google_reviews.text: Not a lot of places manage to transport you to Carribean, South or Central America as effortlessly as Cohibar. Without any extra bells or whistles but just with a welcoming vibe, warm service authentic interior, great rum and coctail menu and a variety of cigars and cigarillos, it is all done here. A pearl, a treasure, a must visit when in Graz, especially during a cold night. Muchas gracias y buena suerte!

google_reviews.author: AndRob Filipinas
google_reviews.text: It is amazing that Thomas and his team can maintain this high standard of quality and sympathy over so many years... Just to recommend

We had cocktails, and the options were surprising. There is a large selection, and if you want something that’s not on the menu, you can probably ask and get it. The prices were fair for austrian standards the quality was also fine. However, the cocktails appearance could be more creative

google_reviews.author: Elke Tomašič
google_reviews.text: great variety of tasty cocktails and very cosy, can definitely recommend.

Nice & helpful staff, local wine and great prices and they are also dog-friendly, they bring a bowl od water to my dog automatically without being asked to do so. I cannot judge food because we didn't try.

When I came for the first time there was great service from a girl working there. But when the boss came he killed the vibe by playing classical music and then when I wanted to pay he got upset and started screaming.

The second time I came I saw him screaming at the waitress and starting an arguement infront of the customers and I left inmediatly.

google_reviews.author: Татьяна Анатольевна
google_reviews.text: A very unpleasant place. If you don’t speak German, you’re treated as a second-class guest – the staff is rude, unhelpful, and acts like they don’t understand you. The atmosphere is noisy and uncomfortable, with no sense of hospitality. If you’re a tourist or not fluent in German, be prepared for unfriendly treatment. The experience was entirely negative, and I definitely wouldn’t recommend this place.

google_reviews.author: Igor Weidlich
google_reviews.text: Nice, cosy, laid back and welcoming atmosphere. Best espresso! Friendly owner who had no problem comunicating with us in English.

google_reviews.author: jakob moser
google_reviews.text: Claire is a great bartender. She needs a raise

google_reviews.author: Tatiana Boura
google_reviews.text: We tried the lunch buffet and everything was fresh and delicious. I am particularly impressed with the quality of the desserts; Typically in such places the main focus is on the food.

google_reviews.author: Dr. Dr. Immanuel Fruhmann MSc
google_reviews.text:
The Asia Wok Royal is a great Chinese Restaurant, located in the west side of Graz in the busiest part of the Eggenberg district of Graz, close to Schloss Eggenberg aka Eggenberg Palace, this fancy palais with a huge garden and part of the UNESCO World Heritage. In the summer there are many peacocks out there roaming around garden. But back to the Asia Wok Royal…this Chinese Restaurant has a good atmosphere as well as a nice interior and it is easy to reach since it is located next to the tram station. The seating is comfy and the food is very delicious. When ordering you can simply say to the waiter that you want to have your food without MSG (Monsodium Glutamate aka Mononatriumglutamat, or simply Glutamat, E 621) and as long as you order à la carte the staff can arrange it and therefore your wish will be respected. Just keep in mind that like in many Chinese Restaurants the already prepared buffet is with MSG aka Glutamat. But if you want to have it spicy or tasty there are the common hot and tasty sauces at the table to modify your meal at pleasure. So the dishes are delicious, the portions are big and the staff is very friendly and willing to help, and of course they are Chinese as one would expect in a Chinese Restaurant.

From Graz with Love!
Dr. Dr. Immanuel Fruhmann

google_reviews.author: Jakob Wiener
google_reviews.text: Top! Super clean (except for sticky floor), Godly Price (11,50€ for buffet with premium Sushis), very tasty, nice waiters and waitresses and not busy restaurant. It's not loud and many starters, main courses and deserts. Many fresh fruits and good baked bananas. I am really looking forward to coming to this restaurant again and I really recommend this restaurant, even if you are not eating anything and just come for a coffee! Thanks!

google_reviews.author: Snehal Shekatkar
google_reviews.text: We had a surprisingly good unlimited food for a very cheap price. They charge only 12 Euros for an entire buffet that includes 25 main courses, 6 starters, and 6 deserts. Not for vegetarians though. Highly recommended otherwise!

google_reviews.author: Fabian

restaurants.name: Bokan
restaurants.summary: Low-key rooms in a casual property offering a restaurant & a cozy bar, plus a spa area with a sauna.

google_reviews.author: Vee J [Vi Gì]
google_reviews.text:
This is a practical, well-organized spot that has quietly outgrown its roots as a modest guesthouse without losing its local charm. In fact, it still embraces its dual identity - with the original old-school, mansion-style guesthouse serving as backup when the modern block fills up, and the traditional, handy, quick-to-order restaurant next door still dishing out hearty home-style Austrian meals and oversized cake slices.

Located just outside the pedestrian and tram zones, Bokan offers plenty of free parking - which, in Graz, is a luxury perk. That alone makes it a top pick for business travelers, touring teams, or anyone who’s had enough of downtown boutique hotels with three-room limits and no space for a suitcase, let alone a van or a bus.

The newer building is tailored for groups - whether you're here for sports, a business trip, or a team retreat. The rooms are spacious and functional, and from what I could tell, many on the first floor and above have direct access to a shared balcony, while the ground-floor rooms enjoy a bit of private green space out front. Great design if you like a breath of fresh air and the sight of swaying green leaves with your morning coffee or tea.

(Although, a small note: a water boiler in the room would make that morning moment perfect -  or at least the option to request one. Tea and coffee lovers don’t always operate on lobby hours!)

The breakfast is surprisingly good. While served from compact containers (likely to keep everything fresh), they’re refilled with lightning speed, and the variety is solid. Try the egg or ham salads - a quirky but tasty twist. The bread selection and presentation are excellent: always warm, crusty, self-serve, and good enough to make you consider a third roll... or fourth. Whether you lean Italian, continental, or protein-heavy in the morning, you’ll leave happy and fueled.

Service is efficient and helpful, and the overall atmosphere strikes a nice balance between relaxed, business-oriented, and well-run.

As for the older guesthouse, it offers character-filled, no-two-rooms-alike accommodations - perfect for student groups, sports teams, families, or anyone headed to the fantastic swimming and diving facility nearby. Old-school charm with a splash of practicality.

Bokan is where traditional hospitality meets modern utility - with good food, lots of space, and room for your luggage and your teammates.

google_reviews.author: Blanka Kobayashi
google_reviews.text: I think the property has two wings, newer and older building. We booked very late and got rooms in the old wing. The old wing is just that - old, outdated, no wifi either. But we came by 11 pm and just needed place to sleep over night, so we were happy they waited for us. Very big parking. Good breakfast. In general good value for a quick sleep.

google_reviews.author: Nikola
google_reviews.text: Very courteous staff. The rooms are very clean although mine was missing the temperature dial setting. Underground parking is available. During evenings internet speeds can get a bit slower but adequate for video calls. In the morning you'll be satisfied with the breakfast spread - the staff constantly makes sure the food is fresh, plentiful and that the tables are clean. Great for short term business travellers or outdoor goers.

google_reviews.author: Marko Kosanovic
google_reviews.text:
Great value for money in a quiet neighbourhood, fantastic breakfast, old furniture:
Liked: The location is excellent, provided you arrive by car, I'm not sure about the public transit options. It's only a 10-minute drive from the center of Graz, but very quiet and peaceful. The room and bathroom was very clean, and the breakfast was fantastic. All the staff we met was super helpful.
Plentiful free parking around the hotel, and also a free underground parking garage available (which we didn't use).
Disliked: The furniture is really old and it shows - the tops of the tables and nightstands are well worn, the bed was extra creaky, and one of the matresses was quite uneven. Please note that this applies only to the older Gasthaus (***) part of the establishment. Things are probably different in the much newer Hotel (****) part.

google_reviews.author: Habibur Rahman
google_reviews.text: Great design if you like a breath of fresh air and the sight of swaying green leaves with your morning coffee or tea.

google_reviews.author: Kee Leong Wang
google_reviews.text:
This place serves amazing bread and breakfast. Service was good and staff spoke English. We were there daily for pur breakfast during our holiday in Graz.

The Guten Morgen can be shared. The Grünes Frühstück has really good dips. The matcha latte and coffee is good. Brioche are good too.

google_reviews.author: Frank Gelling
google_reviews.text: The wife told me to go find my morning coffee and enjoy.  This bakery became my everyday coffee stop..   Excellent coffee and many treats.  Very comfortable seating and I usually stayed to read a couple of chapters in my book.  I highly recommend...stay and relax.

google_reviews.text: A well located bakery, where you can get a cup of good coffee and all kinds of bakery things. Highly recommend their fried triangles with jam, dont remember its name) but tastes always great. Also recently tried breakfast, took Guten Morgen - good breakfast for a good and fair price.

google_reviews.author: Taylla de Paula Fotografia
google_reviews.text: Very delicious. I loved the coffee and the food :)

google_reviews.author: Лъчезар Арсов
google_reviews.text: We had breakfast here, everything was great

restaurants.name: eat Asia

google_reviews.author: Yele
google_reviews.text:
We had a wonderful dining experience. The staff, especially the lady attending to us, was exceptionally kind and accommodating, creating a warm and welcoming atmosphere. Their genuine hospitality made our meal even more enjoyable.

Now, onto the food – it was fantastic!All shrimps dish stood out with its exquisite flavor and perfectly cooked texture. Each bite was a good experience, and it's clear that attention to detail goes into their culinary creations.

The overall atmosphere of the restaurant was cozy and inviting, contributing to a memorable evening. We left with satisfied appetites.

google_reviews.author: Stefi
google_reviews.text: Absolutely amazing service, diverse and tasty food, hot or cold, salty or sweet. 17.9 euro per person 2h all you can eat in Sept. 2025. Would go again anytime! The staff is super friendly.

google_reviews.author: Johan Bonn
google_reviews.text: Large selection of both cold and warm dishes. Also many deserts. Staff very friendly.

google_reviews.author: Anh Thu Bui
google_reviews.text: Decent Asian restaurant! Very nice and helpful waiters and waitresses 😊 Just next time please don’t cut my noodles into small pieces 🙏

google_reviews.author: Ben Gombor
google_reviews.text: Best piccante soup in town, sushi is running very fresh, they like to experiment with new ingredients and styles, very enjoyable. The warm food section is a bit reduced compared to the competition but you can find many great dishes. We like coming here.

restaurants.google_name: Eat Asia Food - Running Sushi & all you can eat in Graz

google_reviews.author: Midori-Nico Mavric
google_reviews.text:
Regardless of the disgust I felt in the cafe, I was refused permission to park in the customer parking space! I was just about to sit down and order a coffee when she told me I wasn't allowed to park there. I then asked the manager why, it's her parking space, and she said she doesn't need any new customers here 😅😂😂😂😂

So if Cafe Tscharri goes, here's the answer why 😅😂

google_reviews.author: Ursula Kapp
google_reviews.text: Great café with lunch. Home-style cooking at a reasonable price. Large portions and really tasty. Highly recommended.

google_reviews.author: sumsi sum
google_reviews.text:
An absolutely inhumane experience.
I stood in the parking lot for exactly seven minutes, immediately apologized, and even left €3 in the café as compensation – but I still received a letter from my lawyer demanding €180. No understanding, no discussion – just harshness. That's not how you treat people.

google_reviews.author: Karl Zako
google_reviews.text: The best spaghetti bolognese ragu for miles around. 🍝🥗🍺And it's absolutely ridiculous how well the chef cooks. 👌

google_reviews.author: J. K.
google_reviews.text: Small, cozy bar. Right in the middle, yet quiet.

restaurants.name: Der Steirer
restaurants.summary: Laid-back destination with a wine shop & a trendy ambiance, specializing in traditional fare.

google_reviews.author: Luis Teixeira
google_reviews.text: Thank you very, very  much to the outstanding Chef who kindly proposed to bring the food to our hotel following the cancellation of our reservation due to sudden illness. Absolutely spotless and kind initiative. The food was delicious. God bless you Sir.

google_reviews.author: TARIQ FAISAL
google_reviews.text:
Der Steirer delivered a beautifully curated dining experience with an ambiance that blended rustic Styrian charm with a contemporary, soulful warmth. We enjoyed the sweet potato gnocchi in saffron ginger coconut cream, a dish that felt both inventive and comforting.
The truffled celery cream soup with puff pastry sticks was refined and deeply aromatic, and the Wiener Schnitzel from veal stood out with its classic authenticity.
A thoughtfully crafted atmosphere paired with memorable flavors Der Steirer truly captured Graz’s creative culinary spirit.

google_reviews.author: Tom LaRocque
google_reviews.text: Excellent food!  Made a reservation through Google (very convenient), and had a great meal!  Wiener Schnitzel (veal), a very good hausbier and a wonderful apple strudel- all so very tasty!  I’m glad I was staying next door so I could enjoy this restaurant!

google_reviews.author: Mishel Beygelman
google_reviews.text: Great spot, thought it would be super busy, but it had many free spots. Recommend coming before six for a guaranteed spot. Their fried chicken was very juicy, we took the mushroom soup too, it was very different from the classical cream soups, it had pieces of potatoes and mushrooms. It was very comforting and cozy.

google_reviews.author: Ryan Hall
google_reviews.text: Food was amazing and the staff were even better! Traditional cuisine and a good wine selection too. Great, relaxed atmosphere and the puddings were next level! I had a perfect creme Brulee! 10/10

google_reviews.author: David Champaign
google_reviews.text: This was a fantastic experience. The food and atmosphere were excellent. I had the goulash, and I highly recommend it. This is really worth coming to, you have to try it.

google_reviews.author: Zsófia Maróti
google_reviews.text: The prettiest, most amazin Café in Graz. Loved the vibes, the staff and the food. Highly recommend it for breakfast for lunch, we loved it here!

google_reviews.author: TheHipsterCher
google_reviews.text:
I came here on two separate days since I was just staying in Hotel Das Weitzer. They have English and German menu to accommodate to the local and tourists. Not all waiters/waitress are fluent in English though. A tall male waiter spoke fluent English and gave quite a good service. Nevertheless, they still try their best to provide good service.
Their cappuccino actually costs 3.50 euro, it is smaller cup than the one serves inside the hotel lobby, which cost 3.90 euro. Their decaf espresso double is more worthy of the price tag.
I tried their Apple Strudel, it didn’t make me feel well for the next following hours, it could just be me with the bad digestive system.
Their Topfentorte is an interesting desert for me, definitely better than the Apple Strudel.

google_reviews.author: Patricia O'Brien
google_reviews.text: We went in for a drink but most customers were going for their coffee with cake offerings! Open on a very cold Sunday, warm inside and very popular, with people trying to squeeze in any available space. Nice friendly café.

google_reviews.author: Vika
google_reviews.text:
A cozy cafe in the center, very good coffee, very tasty and not sweet strudel

Дуже смачно!

google_reviews.author: Martin Oisterschek
google_reviews.text: Best vegetarian restaurant in Graz. Always fresh food buffet with a big variety of salads, warm meals and sweets. Great coffee too and a nice little kids area. Some areas look a bit dated, but all on all a great place to have lunch and bring your kids.

google_reviews.author: Izzy Carter
google_reviews.text: Really tasty and fresh food with lots of variety and choice, with hot and cold options. Good value as you pay for exactly how much you take.

google_reviews.author: MICHEL VEILLEUX
google_reviews.text:
As a visiting Canadian finding this jewel was great. Not speaking German is a challenge reading menus (despite the translation app)
So it was nice to see what can fancy your taste… no matter. The variety of hot and cold selection was just great.
So good food, great variety and great value.
Hard to beat.

google_reviews.author: Hi Wa
google_reviews.text:
It's a nice place. Large with offering a place from for coffee and drinks and another place for food. They also have stuff for purchasing and a place for kids to play.
Atmosphere is nice and pleasant
Taste of the food was also good. It's buffet and based on weighing you pay.
I find it costly
Also they have limited time open
Staff are nice and welcoming
It's also located central and a nice place nearby the river

google_reviews.author: berke ayrancioglu
google_reviews.text: We visited this  nice vegan/vegeterian restaurant for the lunch. Prices were in a reasonable range but the quality was great. You take anything from the buffet and pay according to your plate's weight. The entrance of the restaurant is small but inside is pretty big.

google_reviews.author: Marcin Butler
google_reviews.text:
Excellent food!
Very nice staff.
Really worth visiting!

google_reviews.author: Victoria Chitic
google_reviews.text: Great people. Delicious food. Tip: try the onion soup.

google_reviews.author: Pietro Cipriano
google_reviews.text: Old but characteristics, old style Austrian dishes.

google_reviews.author: Patricia Brocker
google_reviews.text: Good food

google_reviews.author: Gabriele Siegl
google_reviews.text: Top !!!

google_reviews.author: Aoife Caige
google_reviews.text: I love the decor and the food. Snoopy from Minnesota roots was there so I felt right at home.

google_reviews.author: Revioli Baer
google_reviews.text: I wanted to try some vietnamese food and i loved it. It reminds me of home. We were the only ones there to be honest, the staff were very friendly and the food is soo good. But all that is missing is the music. The atmosphere was like an echo. No music at all. Should of added music for a good atmosphere. Of course, we were only the ones there and it was quite dead. I think it is busy in the evening nights. I would recommend the food, it is really the best.

google_reviews.author: Ernst-Georg Lorinser
google_reviews.text: Really good food and great staff that offer a perfect eating experience - well done 👍

google_reviews.author: Igor Iles
google_reviews.text:
We started with the summer rolls, which were rather small and filled mostly with shredded salad. The flavor was quite plain, and the sauce on the side was almost tasteless. There was also no chili available to add some spice.

The pho (we tried both chicken and beef) was good but nothing special — decent flavor, though it lacked bean sprouts, which usually bring freshness and balance to the dish. The Aperol Spritz wasn’t good either.

The service was friendly, which we appreciated, but overall the experience didn’t quite meet expectations.

google_reviews.author: Adrijana Camnasio
google_reviews.text:
Seems to be very liked restaurant, we have booked the table for 3 and we had to wait 40 min to get it. But in the mean time we got small table and great drinks, it was worth to wait.
The food was great, the Service was great. We didn't know what to order, so they made mixed apetizer and mixed main courses with 5 dishes each. It was really good, for sure we will come back.

google_reviews.author: Shadia Tibi
google_reviews.text: Great food and big portions. You can make your own combination of food. The staff is very patient and friendly. The beer is varied and excellent. The atmosphere is wonderful, neither too calm nor too crowded, You won't go wrong if you decide to spend your time here. I really recommend it.

google_reviews.author: Jakob
google_reviews.text:
This pub delivers everything you’d expect and more! The food is classic, hearty, and simply bausatz-great – the kind of dishes that make you feel at home. Their burgers and fries are top-notch, and the portions are generous without compromising on taste. Pair that with a perfectly chilled pint of Guinness or a smooth Irish whiskey, and you’re in for a treat.

The staff, especially the waitresses, are super friendly and attentive, even when the place gets busy – and trust me, it does get busy! The atmosphere is a mix of lively chatter, clinking glasses, and upbeat music, which creates this perfect pub vibe. It’s a bit crowded at times, but honestly, that’s part of the charm – it feels like a place where everyone is just having a good time.

If you’re looking for a spot with great food, friendly faces, and an authentic Irish pub vibe, this is it. I’d recommend coming early if you want a quieter corner, but either way, it’s worth every visit!

google_reviews.author: Sez it Reght
google_reviews.text: Good pub in the centre, nice pint of guinness and good to see they also have a zero alcohol option too. Definitely be back

google_reviews.author: Virág Palotai
google_reviews.text: Very friendly staff,nice vibe and tasty food. I like the music as well. Thank you guys 🤘😁

google_reviews.author: Robin (老外 . tw)
google_reviews.text: Good atmosphere, friendly service.

google_reviews.author: Sai Pavan Kumar Veeranki
google_reviews.text:
What a disgrace! I was violently dragged out of Nachtexpress for absolutely no reason. I’ve lived in Graz for 8 years, and I used to think this place was a haven for rock music lovers and foreigners. Not anymore as today was a classic example of racism.

While simply enjoying a beer in the corner, I was approached by a bald-headed security guard who asked me to leave. I refused, as I was just minding my own business and paid already for my drink. To my shock, he physically removed me from the club and I had to leave my bier worth of € 5,10.

I even called the police, but they couldn’t assist me in this situation. It’s disappointing because I’ve always promoted Nachtexpress to my friends as a great place for foreigners, but given the recent developments in Austrian politics and the behavior of the bald-headed security staff, I can no longer recommend it.

While it still has a cool atmosphere, I advise any foreigner to think twice before visiting Nachtexpress unless you have a white skin and blond hair.

PS: I couldn't reply to your response, thus updating the review.

Thank you for your response.

Let me make this crystal clear: the ridiculous allegation that I was smoking in the club is completely false. I’m a non-smoker, I wasn’t drunk, and I know and respect Austrian regulations. I was explaining we are not doing anything and asking he could speak to me nicely. Your security cannot grab a bottle which I have bought at your premises from my hand, It's unacceptable. I have been living in this country for the last 2 decades, I have never encountered such an experience.

This is the story he came up with that I kept smoking? You may come up with any further bullshit stories.

If you have respect for "Menschen", first you don't behave like this and second you do not put fault on other for a review. Saying “everyone is welcome” means nothing if you don’t back it up with real integrity.

google_reviews.author: Markus
google_reviews.text: You like rock and heavy metal music, that this might be the right place for you in graz. The people are totally mixed fro.the real heavy metal fan to the guys in suits all are welcome and there and have just a great party with hard rock and havy metal music. Drinks are affordable and crew is friendly

google_reviews.author: Djordje Skakun
google_reviews.text: Great place for listening rock music, unique atmosphere and layout, its a little bit neglected in the maintenance department, but i think it adds to the whole experience.    Really the only such place in Graz, if you like rock and metal, you must visit it.

google_reviews.author: Mohammadreza Kariman
google_reviews.text:
Visited here for the first time with my colleagues.
Enjoyed the music a lot. In general, the atmosphere was quite nice.

google_reviews.author: Sarah Haas
google_reviews.text: Well, it's a little rundown, the floor is fortunately black and always sticky, it's very dark and loud. The perfect metal club if you ask me. I love it

google_reviews.author: solo
google_reviews.text:
Our family of 2 adults and kids ate dinner at Restaurant Athen in Graz.
We had four different dishes, and all were well made and and very tasty. The service was perfect : friendly and informative.
All in all we had a very nice experience!

google_reviews.author: EMMANOUIL VICHOS
google_reviews.text: Very friendly place! The food is simple and good as it is supposed to be in a Greek tavern. I visited the place twice with my Austrian girlfriend and will do it again in the future!

google_reviews.author: Teodor Pop
google_reviews.text: We visited the restaurant while on a short trip in Graz. The food and the service were absolutely wonderful. Everything we ordered was very very tasty. Thank you! Next time in Graz we'll eat here again... I recommend this restaurant to anyone. Please make sure you try the home-made baklava!

google_reviews.author: Gaia Alfinito
google_reviews.text: Me and my friend came here for dinner, we really appreciated the food but also the service who was ready with kindness to offer suggestions on the menu. Unfortunately I don't have pictures 'cause we ate all very fast😂 We'll definitely come back!

google_reviews.author: Mariia Beliaeva
google_reviews.text: Amazing food and great stuff!

google_reviews.author: Mladen Popovic
google_reviews.text: Nice doner in Graz - we wanted to have a quick bite and a friend recommended this place. Nothing much to add - the service was friendly, good value for money and tasty food.

google_reviews.author: Jyothish Atheendran
google_reviews.text:
I tried the steak 🥩 Kebab 🥙 from here. The service was very nice and the person at the counter was very friendly. However I don't have too much of an opinion about the food I tried.

It was a bit dry for my taste and the salads in the kebab overshadowed the kebab meat filling itself. I'm yet to try any of their other offerings.

They don't accept payments with card 💳❌

google_reviews.author: ChunkyCherry
google_reviews.text: Honestly a really nice place. They serve yaprak Döner with very good prices and a nice variety of fillings. The sauces are nice, the dressings are nice and I would say the meat quality is better than many other places. Nothing to complain about here.

google_reviews.author: Mátyás Szatmári
google_reviews.text: Cheap, tasty, big portions

google_reviews.author: Florian Wimberger
google_reviews.text: Best Kebab in Graz!

google_reviews.author: Oliver Hutchison
google_reviews.text: Just what we were looking for on our quick trip through Austria: a nice easy pub-style schnitzel dinner with a beer in a place with lots of wood and what to me feels like a traditional ambiance. We weren't disappointed, and on a Saturday night at 8pm there was plenty of space for us and our dog, who was promptly given a bowl of water. It's just a short, pleasant walk past the Folk Life Museum on the edge of the old town.

google_reviews.author: Richard Healy
google_reviews.text: Good beer,shit food €14.50 for a €2 salad served with overcooked fried chicken. Even better they gave me two and one half. When I asked for the explanation my meal disappeared. Unfortunately I chose wrong.

google_reviews.author: Jessica Lopez
google_reviews.text:
Easy walk from the castle and the tourist sites. The service was excellent and the food was delicious. Definitely recommended the short walk to enjoy this place. Plenty of space inside the restaurant and a nice patio to enjoy the outdoor.
The menu is pretty extensive so there are options for every taste. Vegetarian options as well.

google_reviews.author: Ivan Hontić
google_reviews.text: Service is excellent and friendly, but too many guests for one waitresses. She was running from table to table running like crazy and despite her best attempts we still had to wait 10-15 minutes before we could order. I’ve had Wiener schnitzel with fries for lunch. I got ketchup instead of what traditionally I would expect Preislbeeren. Meal was fine but nothing special. Prices are way to expensive for the quality, amount and waiting time.

google_reviews.author: Conni Covington
google_reviews.text: I got the Käsespätzle and they were able to make it without Speck, which was nice.

google_reviews.author: SACHIT VARMA
google_reviews.text: One of the best places for delicious food, drinks accompanied by a very happening and jolly atmosphere. There are more than 100 types of beers available here. There's a big hall with many pool tables and electronic darts which are almost always flocking with people. The service is decently fast and the staff is happy to cater to customization requests. They speak fantastic English as well. Highly recommended.

google_reviews.author: Hadar izhak
google_reviews.text: We had a great time! The burger was great and the side dish was tasty and generous. The service  was very good.  Highly recommend the blue cheese burger.

google_reviews.author: Mattia Giovannini
google_reviews.text:
Very good restaurant in the heart of Graz. Stuff very friendly. Great quality of food. I opted for a double patty blue cheese burger which I highly recommend. Good selections of beers as well.
Plenty pool tables which require reservation.

google_reviews.author: Philipp Klaus Heinrich
google_reviews.text: One of my favourite places in Graz. I was born in Graz and since I was a teenager I would go there with my Dad to play Billiard, drink something and have a burger. Phenomenal atmosphere and cool location right in the coty center. You can also play Darts, Chess or different types of Poker there. The Burgers are really good and the insane selection of beer makes it very unique. I would recommend this place everyone who's chill and wants to have a good time.

google_reviews.author: HRISTINA
google_reviews.text: Amazing place, cool people, biliard tables and tasteful plates and snacks with unlimited choice of beersss. Definitely recommended!!! 🇦🇹💚

google_reviews.author: M F
google_reviews.text:
Think twice about going to the Santa Clara restaurant.
The owner of this location has been caught on camera and subsequently fined for on multiple occasions smearing what we can only hope is animal and not personal waste on another local in the vicinity. Wthout any form of protective hand covering might I add. Potential guests should be aware of this and consider who is handling their food if they choose to visit.

google_reviews.author: Logan Lynch
google_reviews.text: I would be wary of dining in this establishment, especially if the owner is involved in the food preparation - very much lacking in personal hygiene. Most of us learn to wipe as children, but I suppose not all of us can be so enlightened.

google_reviews.author: mark higgins
google_reviews.text: I would be sincerely worried if he is also the one preparing the food. Owner is disgusting..

google_reviews.author: JOEL
google_reviews.text: Very high quality and cuisine! We had a soup (€6) followed by antipasti, or fresh vegetables and fish from the display case (€38 for two people). Highly recommended! The frequently mentioned owner is not present. We received very warm service from the owner.

google_reviews.author: Alvin Monzinalla
google_reviews.text: The laudable owner of this restaurant repeatedly used his entire brain capacity to smear his body's waste products on the door handle of a neighboring business. This ensures that the competition works even harder and is thus demonstrably committed to maintaining culinary diversity in Graz. I would like to personally thank him for this noble act and place my homemade "chocolate slice" in front of the entrance to his restaurant. Since he seems to have a great fondness for brown products, he can then enjoy it after work.

google_reviews.author: Aspa Lanara
google_reviews.text:
A masterpiece of unprofessionalism and absolute disregard for customers
If you value your time and basic respect, stay as far away from Gasthaus Stainzerbauer as possible.
We made a reservation an entire month in advance (January 18th) for February 17th. We received a written confirmation, yet when we arrived, we were met with a closed door and a shocking level of incompetence. Despite their official operating hours on Google and at their entrance clearly stating they are open until 23:00, the staff informed us they were closing at 19:00.
The experience turned from disappointing to insulting due to the receptionist’s behavior. She attempted a ridiculous ‘creative interpretation’ of the English language, trying to convince us that the word 'today' in their January email actually meant 'that day' in February. Even when we presented the actual confirmation email on our phones, she flatly denied its existence and showed zero interest in providing a solution or even a simple recommendation for another place to eat.
To be stranded in a foreign city, on a Tuesday evening following a public holiday and carnival, with a confirmed reservation that they refused to honor, is unacceptable.
This establishment suffers from:
Total ignorance of their own operating hours.
A non-existent booking system that fails to track confirmed emails.
Appalling customer service and a lack of basic English communication skills.
A complete embarrassment for the local hospitality industry. Do not trust their 'confirmations'—they are clearly meaningless.

google_reviews.author: Martha Milch
google_reviews.text: As a globe-trotting food enthusiast, I can’t help but be utterly charmed by my experience at Gasthaus Stainzerbauer in Graz. Nestled beneath historic vaulted ceilings and bathed in the soft glow of candlelit lanterns, this venerable inn exudes rustic warmth and quiet sophistication. Outside, the tranquil courtyard wraps you in greenery and serenity—perfect for a summer lunch. Every dish felt deeply rooted in tradition yet refined by the skill of Chef Wiener: from the sumptuous Styria Beef and tender braised Kalbsbackerl to the delicate Beuschel and beautifully fried Wiener Schnitzel. My favorite was the beef liver, accompanied by horseradish-infused potato mash—bold, comforting, and expertly balanced. Service shines here, with gracious hosts like Josef Pfeifer guiding the evening with elegance and ease. The wine list is staggering in variety and quality, elevating every morsel. Stainzerbauer isn’t just a meal—it’s a heartfelt journey through Styrian culinary heritage. Highly, highly recommended.

google_reviews.author: Philip J-D
google_reviews.text: Booked this lovely place for a smarter meal during a sporting tour for a group of 9. Lovely food including immaculate presentation of some traditional Austrian classics. Highly recommended!

google_reviews.author: Josh C
google_reviews.text: The food is good, the atmosphere is relaxed and the staff is friendly. The place is located a few steps from the cathedral and has great offerings in food and drinks. I arrived at an unusual hour for food and the staff was very helpful and friendly. Totally recommend it!

google_reviews.author: Cyril Karpenko
google_reviews.text: My experience here was absolutely incredible.  Nice and polite host, a historic atmosphere and ambiance of this place. Also, I just loved the beef liver I was served that day. The idea of adding horseradish into the potato mash is just out of this world. This is just something I'll be doing myself when I cook it from this time forward, lol.

google_reviews.author: A.
google_reviews.text: Cash only. Extremely loud inside. I had to wire the money into the waitresses bank account 🤦.. Toilet reminds me of my time in Syria. Chair had a pointy spring sticking out, poking my bum. You can't make this stuff up.

google_reviews.author: Hendrik Bower
google_reviews.text: It's... ok. But only that. I only go here when other people choose, and for the life of me, I have no idea why they ever do. It's dark, gloomy (but not in an Irish bar kind of way which you could find cosy), the service is friendly enough but you'll often have to chase them down to order stuff or pay... even when it's empty. Meh.

google_reviews.author: Noal Balint
google_reviews.text: cash only and not many food options, but for what it does offer (drinks and ambiance), it’s very nice :)

google_reviews.author: Aleksandar Stanisic
google_reviews.text: They sell Cigarettes. Cool staf.. Great for a solo drinking and roaming start

google_reviews.author: Simon Graf (Simon Graf)
google_reviews.text: Nice and unique atmosphere with fair priced coffee

google_reviews.author: D Lacroix
google_reviews.text: Great neighborhood café. Cozy atmosphere and excellent service. Had the Lolita toast with smoked salmon, v good horseradish/beet spread.

google_reviews.author: Saurabh Jyot Singh Khehra
google_reviews.text:
We were in Graz for a day and ended up at this magnificent cafe.

The interiors were regal, the atmosphere suave and we were delighted to find this hidden gem of a cafe.

It is a bit far from the city centre but a bus from there will drop you close to the cafe.

The staff were super friendly and we loved our lunch here. The food was 5 star quality and we wish we could have spent more time here but we had a train to catch :)

Hopefully see you guys again sometime :)

google_reviews.author: Nebojsa Simic
google_reviews.text: Simply an amazing experience. The staff is extremely nice and friendly, service is flawless and the setting is cozy, inviting and altogether very relaxed. Garden offers a refuge from the busyness of the city and provides a quiet atmosphere for morning coffee or afternoon brunch. Very good, honest food and great coffee. I would recommend the Fotter chilli bowl or the avocado bowl.

google_reviews.author: BariczzsoltOfficial
google_reviews.text:
Such a lovely place , we have visited for brunch which was great ! Also we got seated in the garden which is a big plus and unique !
So highly recommended to this Cafe

google_reviews.author: Diana Lynovytska
google_reviews.text:
Lovely cafe to have a breakfast/ brunch - the food is good, as well as coffee. The atmosphere is relaxing, a lot of cute things inside.
Though, the place is a bit pricey and it’s almost impossible to be seated without a reservation, even on Monday morning.

google_reviews.author: Abdul Razak Fakhouri
google_reviews.text: Went there on a Monday around lunchtime with a friend. We were welcomed right away, service was quick, and they handled our special requests perfectly. The pizza was excellent, the tiramisu really good, and the double espresso spot on. Only downside: no soap in the men’s restroom. Overall, a great experience — about 9 out of 10.

google_reviews.author: Ahmet Yüksek
google_reviews.text: The pizza looks great also the dough is well prepared/cooked but the ingredients did not have a strong taste even though I have chosen a pizza with truffle which should be quite strong in taste

google_reviews.author: Alberto Pascual
google_reviews.text: It's a good restaurant for pizzas. They have a  good variety of them in the menu and they are made in the oven. The dough is elastic kind, more than crunchy, but even not being a fan of that dough style, I have to say the pizzas are good. The service is friendly and nice.

google_reviews.author: Bogdan Lupu
google_reviews.text:
Better pizza than many places I have tried in Rome! The taste is amazing and everything is done very quick, as they have a professional oven so that the pizza is done in 90 seconds.

The waiters aren't fluent in English but they understood all of our requests and everything was to our liking. I highly recommend stopping by if you're looking for a taste of Italy in the heart of Austria.

google_reviews.author: Julian Jank
google_reviews.text: Galliano is offering the best Pizza in Graz! (Shared spot with maximum 1 or 2 other places) They are very friendly and the pizza is always excellent!

google_reviews.author: Анна Ткаченко
google_reviews.text: If you drink a glass of wine for €3 and want to pay by card, be prepared for the staff to give you a terminal for €10, and of course no one will tell you this until you ask. The service is terrible.

google_reviews.author: Philipp Forstner
google_reviews.text: I was at Hasenstall on 03/31/2023. This place is a hidden gem for people who love traditional Austrian party culture. Bartenbders are very nice, the drinks were spot on and there is no other place to go if you want to experience Austria!!! 🤙 check it out guys!

google_reviews.author: KinEst Fest
google_reviews.text: Very nice!

google_reviews.author: Thomas Wagner
google_reviews.text: Absolutely disgusting. Even if you arrive late at night, you expect a certain level of cleanliness. Simply awful!

google_reviews.author: Data Protection
google_reviews.text: A must-visit in Graz, whether for pre-drinks or a night out, this charming and characterful Graz cultural venue offers music that's perfect for singing along, shouting, or simply chatting. If you're looking for a truly authentic Graz party experience, this is the place to be – small but mighty 🎶 and the atmosphere is always fantastic! 🥳

google_reviews.author: M
google_reviews.text: Had 2 deliveries recently. Great in time and the pizza was great

google_reviews.author: Nohz Zhu
google_reviews.text: Waited 50min to get the order canceled... now i need to order somewhere else which will also take that amount time... frustrating...

google_reviews.author: Senor Spielbergo
