google_reviews.author: Oktay Kocyigit
google_reviews.text: Ok

google_reviews.author: Kurd Ahahaha
google_reviews.text: My visit was disappointing. The food, especially the meat, had a strange taste. Furthermore, there was no staff visible, which gave the impression of a lack of service. The cleanliness also left much to be desired. Overall, I cannot recommend this restaurant after this experience.

google_reviews.author: Tanja Dobrovoljski
google_reviews.text: Went there for brunch today, and I must say everything was perfect. The food was delicious, the people working there were very friendly, and the overall atmosphere was great. They have a charming garden in the inner courtyard with natural shade and a small sandbox area for children. Highly recommended.

google_reviews.author: Martha Milch
google_reviews.text:
I recently spent a relaxed morning at Die Scherbe, tucked away on Stockergasse. From the moment I stepped inside, I appreciated the modern, light-filled space—with high ceilings, warm wood furniture, and sunny windows opening onto a lively pedestrian street.

I started with a globally inspired brunch—rich roast pumpkin with dumplings, followed by fresh falafel and a vibrant root vegetable soup. Each dish felt thoughtfully crafted, offering flavor and freshness while staying hearty and comforting.

Service was genuinely warm and flexible. Staff were quick to accommodate dietary needs and menu explanations felt friendly, not rushed.

The atmosphere balanced buzz and calm perfectly. Guests chatted casually, natural light flooded the room, and the bright tables made the space feel welcoming and easygoing.

Die Scherbe stands out for its creative, inclusive menu, sunlit ambience, and genuinely caring hospitality. It’s a local gem where brunch feels like a relaxed culinary trip around the world—I can’t wait to return.

google_reviews.author: iva kurelja
google_reviews.text:
Food is very tasty, only the portion size is a bit small if you’re very hungry.
Very friendly personnel and lovely atmosphere. 😊

google_reviews.author: S. habibi
google_reviews.text:
The foods we tried were incredibly delicious. I loved the pumpkin pickle that they serves with dumplings.
The staffs are very kind and they make time for clients who don’t speak German to describe the menu and foods.

google_reviews.author: Phoebe Ting
google_reviews.text: Food was quite nice, particularly the root vegetable soup? Not sure what it was called but it was peppery and tasty. Service was very swift and fuss free.

google_reviews.author: Tamás Kovács
google_reviews.text:
I had a really good choose, good basic and the topping was delicious. The staff was kind and helpful. Price/quality ratio is okay.
I really recommend it if you want to eat a good Italian pizza.
Maybe the atmosphere would be better with more Italian staff but I liked the many glass of wine on the wall.

google_reviews.author: N B
google_reviews.text: In the world there is Milky Way and Northern Lights, there is a mother that is looking with lovely eyes on her newly born kid, there is Viennese opera and French wine, there is the kindness and freshness of the sunrise, and there is a restaurant Due Amici in Graz with its awesome pizza, pasta and risotto.

google_reviews.author: Rene Moreira
google_reviews.text: I did not eat there because the opening hours was until 11pm but I arrived 10.15pm and they were closing, then should put it closes 10pm, I came next day and the person who talked to me recognized me and even after I had put a bad review about the time , he was very professional and the pizza was delicious!

google_reviews.author: Mina Grace
google_reviews.text: I asked about Takeaway service and he told me that it would be at least 30 minutes. I’m not sure why because there were hardly any patrons in the restaurant. I told him I’d look over the menu and let him know. He hid in the back room. I waited for 10 minutes, but he didn’t come out for my order. I won’t return here. There are plenty of other great Italian restaurants in the area that have better service

google_reviews.author: Balazs Koranyi
google_reviews.text: The food was great but the service was crazy slow. From the time we ordered, pizza took 40 minutes. From the time we sat down, it took 60 mins to get food. The waiter was not even visible. Just to pay, I had to wait 10 mins by the bar for him to appear

google_reviews.author: Qais Baqain
google_reviews.text:
Place nice but slightly small and smokey though, the staff was really friendly and prices are great ;) especially with the portion you get :D
In summer (warm weather)  I guess they have outdoor seating which I believe would be nice and enjoyable

google_reviews.author: Jyothish Atheendran
google_reviews.text: Good food. Good vibes. They accept payments with cards. A convenient pub near the TU building.

google_reviews.author: Patricia Brocker
google_reviews.text: Looks a nice student meeting place must have a drink there some time still have not visited.

google_reviews.author: Woody
google_reviews.text: Came for the burgers, def gonna come again for the burgers. Lovely atmosphere in the outside area

google_reviews.author: Patrick Edlinger
google_reviews.text: Reasonably priced great food and drinks. Nice atmosphere

google_reviews.author: Dror Alon Digital
google_reviews.text:
Loved it!!
Located in Innere Stadt, the historic heart of Graz, Bar 28 combines the relaxed charm of a neighborhood pub with the energy of a modern sports bar. Only a short walk from the Hauptplatz and the Mur River, it sits among old façades and narrow streets where history and nightlife blend effortlessly.

Inside, the atmosphere is warm and genuine — soft light, classic wooden tables, and a bar lined with craft beers and local brews. The playlist shifts between rock classics and indie favorites, adding to that familiar pub rhythm that makes you want to stay longer than planned. Screens show football and Formula 1, yet conversations flow just as freely as the beer.

The food is far above the usual bar level: freshly prepared burgers, crisp fries, and seasonal snacks that pair beautifully with a cold Austrian ale. You can taste the care in the kitchen and see it in the service — friendly, fast, and unpretentious.

google_reviews.author: Daniel Wi
google_reviews.text:
I've enjoyed the atmosphere at this bar.
Me and my girl recommend it.

It gave us such a Graz vibe 😁.

We visited in May, the weather outside was getting warm so everybody was sited at the outside terrace. There are cars passig by since it is a norma neighborhood street, but the people in Graz don't use cars that much so it was not noisy,  nor problematic.

We had some beers. Weissbier for me, pale ale for my girl.
On the food side we wanted some fries or chips, we didn't see that on the menu so we stuck with our beers. They have burgers, nachos and stuff but we did not try them.

Another aspect to take into account is that above the bar there are residential apartments and it is recommended to keep your voice down. We were ok with that.

A plus for the bar is that it has a very chill, nice vibe and it is open until lat 12-1 o'clock at night which for Graz is a huuuge thing 😅.

google_reviews.author: Simon Ward
google_reviews.text: Really really great burger with fries, and my partner had the chicken salad, it was so nice that we went again. The staff were very friendly and put up with my school German (they all spoke very good English). The pinball machines were great fun. Relaxed atmosphere, highly recommended.

google_reviews.author: Stephen Ellwood
google_reviews.text: Went to a Christmas party expecting to be able to talk to my colleagues. Barman insisted on playing the music so loud we had to shoutat each other all night. There was also a buffet by the food was cheap and not very tasty. Not recommended.

google_reviews.author: Karina Rosa
google_reviews.text:
If you are in Graz, this is the place you have to como !

Lovely Sports Bar!!

Lovely atmosphere, delicious food, great service and very good drinks!

google_reviews.author: Victoria St
google_reviews.text: Super nice place! The view is fabulous, you will enjoy your time there. Food is delicious, portions are big. The choice is quite nice so everyone should find something to eat. Coffee is delicious.

google_reviews.author: Monika Hilner
google_reviews.text:
I love the concept of this restaurant. Organic, breakfast all day + traditional dishes.
The interior was also fire.
I strongly recommend this place, great food, nice staff and amazing atmosphere.

google_reviews.author: Gulnar
google_reviews.text:
The location and the outdoor seating is very welcoming. Even calming to some extent as one can hear the fountain on the street.

The food however could be much better when it comes to taste. We ordered the some green breakfast and breakfast burger. Quite tasteless, both of them.

Would give others dishes a try one more time before deciding to not go back again.

google_reviews.author: Martin T
google_reviews.text: One of my favorite brunch spot so far! Very cool atmosphere and nice staff! Outdoor patio was amazing!

google_reviews.author: Aleksandra Czyżycka
google_reviews.text:
We were chuffed to find this place while looking for a breakfast spot. We loved the area and the food. They had a lot of different options - both sweet and savoury.
The breakfast was delicious and the view really nice ;) They have interesting tea flavours.
It was swamped inside, but the staff was friendly and quick. You order and pay at the bar, but then they bring everything to you, so you can relax while waiting.
If you like the plates or mugs, you can buy them inside.
In the evening they have a variety of drinks, and since the mountain looks even nicer at night, I think it's a great place for a drink in the evening.
If we stop in Graz again, we will for sure come back. They also have a place in Vienna.

google_reviews.author: Vee J [Vi Gì]
google_reviews.text:
As often happens, the best memories are born from unexpected turns. I was invited to a professional gathering at Das Eggenberg, without knowing much about the place - or Graz, to be honest. While the rest of our group headed off in search of a "nice local restaurant" downtown, two of us stayed behind, planning to join them later.

We're coming from Italy, so when it comes to pizza, pasta, and anything labeled or sounding authentically Italian, we know.

While the rest of the group ended up at one of those pretentiously named Italian places serving Austrian-style pizza and pasta (think: more dough, less soul), we were busy enjoying surprisingly perfect Neapolitan pizzas in a place that sounds like a beer hall but cooks like Naples - and flips burgers like Uncle Sam himself.

Das Eggenberg delivers a wonderfully odd but charming experience. The name says "classy Austrian heritage," but the vibe is cozy, cool, and just edgy enough - with warm, earthy lighting and unexpected street-art graffiti that somehow works beautifully. The staff is relaxed and welcoming, and the drinks and local brewery selections are cold, solid, and satisfying.

No over-promising. No pretending to be something it’s not.
Just good food, good people, and a space that feels effortlessly right.

Because sometimes, you don’t needed to sound good to be good - you just need to deliver.
And Das Eggenberg does exactly that.

Next time, this is the only place to be.

google_reviews.author: Gulnar
google_reviews.text:
The interiors were great and had a hip vibe going on. The service was nice as well.

The food however was average. We ordered 3 burgers and one Asian sweet and sour dish with prawns and a pizza to go. The taste wasn’t impressive and therefore the value for money wasn’t good. There are definitely better burgers and pizzas in Graz.

However, I might be interested in trying their breakfast option as the interiors are very charming and would like to visit again for a different environment.

google_reviews.author: Noa Musa
google_reviews.text:
The service was really friendly, and the interior is hip, modern, and Instagrammable.

Their homemade lemonades were excellent, and I'm saying this as someone who usually doesn't like homemade lemonades because they're typically bland. These, especially the strawberry one, were amazing.

The fries were alright—just normal fries, but crunchy. The chili mayo, however, was bland and not really spicy, more like just plain mayo.

The burger (Vincent Van Käse) was disappointing. The beef patty had an overwhelming greasy taste, and the bottom of the bun was super soggy from the sauce, which I assume was supposed to be ketchup. It was a real letdown.

My friend tried their pizza and was happy with it, but my burger arrived 15 minutes before the pizza, which was frustrating. We were told it's because they come from two separate kitchens, but this is not good kitchen etiquette.

Overall, would only come again to try the pizza.

google_reviews.author: Máté Kocsis
google_reviews.text: The food was excellent, one of the best pizzas I've ever eaten. The place itself is gorgeous inside and outside too and the staff is very fast and kind. 10/10 would recommend for you to visit if you are in Graz. You are even luckier if you live in Graz because you will have the opportunity to revisit time and time again. The prices are also okay for a restaurant that looks this nice and serves great food.

google_reviews.author: TheHipsterCher
google_reviews.text: Their pizza is a must try. It is so good and worthy of every bite. The restaurant is more a hipster vibe.

google_reviews.author: Muntazir Abbas
google_reviews.text: The place is extremely pleasant with open and wide seating area. Summer days and nights can very charming. Its a friends and student cafe. A must visit place behind the Technical University of Graz old campus.

restaurants.name: KFC
restaurants.summary: Restaurant chain known for its buckets of fried chicken, plus combo meals & sides.

google_reviews.text:
I love kfc, but...
I've been trying kfc in a different counties, and this one was the worse of all so far.. the wings without salt, some of them raw, they mess up the order.. the potatoes as well eas the worse in my life, and I use to fight that kfc has better than other fast food brands (not in this case) the personal was lovely, barely speaking English but at least was trying to help out (Graz is well known as the student's capital in Austria, there should be at least 1 person who speaks english) the atmosphere was okay, it was clean so can't say anything about it.

google_reviews.author: Johanna Fliesser

The worst of KFC I've tried so far, I've been to others which were better.
The chicken just tastes bland - as if it's just a synthetic mass.

I've eaten here twice, and both times it wasn't satisfactory. Definitely room for improvement!

google_reviews.author: MRL Media Limited
google_reviews.text:
I often come here with my friends for dinner and we always have a great time 😍🍟

The chicken is always hot, crispy, and super tasty 😋🔥 – exactly what you expect from KFC! The staff are very polite, friendly, and helpful 😊🙌

Perfect place to eat, chill, and enjoy with friends 🥤🍗🎉.

The staff here are super friendly and welcoming 😊🙌. They take care of customers quickly, keep the place clean 🧼✨, and make sure you have a great experience every time.

Highly recommended if you want delicious KFC chicken and great service! 🍟🥤🍗

google_reviews.author: Daney Elev
google_reviews.text: KFC never disappoints. I stopped by last night after work, tired and hungry, and wow the chicken hit different. Crispy outside, soft inside. Staff was smiling, even tho it was late. Felt nice.

google_reviews.author: LaVell Thompson, Jr.
google_reviews.text:
If I could give them 3 1/2 stars I would do that. Unfortunately, Google only allows whole-star ratings. My friend, with whom I attended the restaurant last week, said to give them 4 stars. Thus, 3 and 1/2 would be a compromise.

Overall, I had a fairly good experience here! I would absolutely eat here again in the future. There are no glaring errors or grievances that would discourage me from returning here.

I guess I'll start with the general feel of the place. The decor on the inside is very unique! In the first place, it's not very well-lit. Let me clarify that. To say that it's not very well-lit suggests that it's dark. That's not true at all. It's that the lighting is very low and intimate! So if you'd be looking for an experience that is illuminated, this would not be the place for you.

But I think for the person or individuals or couple who would want a more intimate and less travelled place, one off the beaten path, this would be a great find! We went on a Friday evening, I believe, and it was very easy to sit. There was no long wait to get a table. I'm not sure where everyone would have been on a Friday night, but it was not busy to our advantage!

What you will find in the videos and photos I uploaded is that the title of the restaurant reflects the interior's decor! There are LPs and various photographs and such scared about the whole restaurant / bar areas. The restroom area was also pretty unique! You'll find a lot more lighting in that area. lol There is a very interesting grunge and graffiti decor written all over the walls. That was also a very cool find!

I guess if there is one thing that I had a minor grievance with, it would have been the dough of the pizza. I guess it's "bar pizza" and not "restaurant pizza?" I'm not sure if that makes sense, but it's the only way I can articulate the matter. It's not really a complaint, per se; yet, I have to be honest in this review as I always am in all of my reviews. Even though I enjoyed the very thin nature of the crust, it left something to be desired.

The doe had no real flavour to it, nor did it have any kind of real consistency. It was kind of like eating salted cardboard or something of the sort. I think it was a cheapish version to save money. This would have been the thing that resulted in my giving them three stars as opposed to four stars the way my friends suggested. Again: THAT WAS NOT AT ALL A COMPLAINT. It was...a minor grievance. As I stated in the beginning, I would eat there again!

The final thing I'd like to discuss is the great service I received! Our wonderful server, Marten, was excellent! Not only was he very affable and friendly, and spoke great English, he was very easy on the eyes. I told him at least twice period! Lol

I would be remiss if I did not mention the low cost of the food! For what we paid, we ordered an extra large pie that came with four toppings! That would be unheard of in the United States. We were both very happy considering the fact that my friend ordered a beer with the pizza.

Yes. Despite my thoughts about the chosen dough for the bar / restaurant, I had a pleasant experience here. I would eat there again in a heartbeat.

google_reviews.author: Tanja
google_reviews.text:
Better than Sägewerk in my opinion. Just like the other ones, the best option here is pizza. Keep in mind that this restaurant does not serve dishes á la card, instead you need to select the four toppings yourself.

Again: There is no menu.

google_reviews.author: Hendrik Bower
google_reviews.text:
The atmosphere isn't awful but it's certainly not somewhere you go for atmosphere. Reasonable drinks prices, very good food prices. Toilets need much more regular cleaning, but that's true across the chain (and indeed, most bars in the city).

All in all, decent place for food and beer.

google_reviews.author: Erick Garcia
google_reviews.text: Very nice place where you can get wraps, pizza, or some beberages at a very good price. The atmosphere is great, and the personal is very kind...

google_reviews.author: Jere H.
google_reviews.text: Very kindly staff. Beer was good and pizza super delicious. As a tourist we got good english service.

google_reviews.author: Denis Miksic
google_reviews.text: Very nice, urban/avangard caffe in middle of Graz. Coussy atmosphete, friendly barwoman. Music fro whitesnake and scorpions to berry white and prince. Recomendation for ginpunch 4.95 eur at wintertime. Great view at street behind big wndows

google_reviews.author: patrick valarezo
google_reviews.text: Nice and cozy place to have a beer.

google_reviews.author: Hansjürgen Fras
google_reviews.text: Cool

google_reviews.author: Dumb fairy
google_reviews.text: Simply my favorite bar in Graz. The staff is so friendly! A cozy bar ☺️ They also have excellent wines and their own schnapps, highly recommended! Super great ☺️

google_reviews.author: Thomas MAIER
google_reviews.text: The homemade vermouth there is legendary. My tip: try "Frohmut" (cheerful), it's encouraging and cheerful. The owner and staff are very friendly, with a hint of vintage charm: Hemingway is about to walk in...

google_reviews.author: Anne Lam
google_reviews.text:
A short walk from  Schloss Eggenberg, this lovely traditional styrian tavern was a warm and welcoming spot for a late lunch. Generous portions starting off with a piping hot clear beef broth soup with liver dumplings - simply comfort food at its best!  Then my husband and I tackled a side of potato salad and an ENORMOUS plate of schnitzel breaded with pumpkin seeds (yes, it’s enough to share!)  Thick, tender and juicy schnitzel chop, yet perfectly crisp-fried (not oily tasting), it was really very very good!
Highly recommend for what I can best describe as an “honest ingredients” unpretentious quality meal! Definitely can recommend this one!

google_reviews.author: Me Sen
google_reviews.text: Wonderful to eat! Our dogs had also a great time. We found this accidentally.

google_reviews.author: Serge Brault
google_reviews.text: Great ambience, great food (I never saw my girlfriend enjoying a meal like that!)

google_reviews.author: Barnabas Nagy
google_reviews.text:
🌟🌟🌟🌟🌟 5-Star Experience at Roschitz, Graz

We had an absolutely wonderful dining experience at Roschitz. Every dish was fresh, delicious, and prepared with care – true 5-star quality.
•	The Fitness Salad was vibrant and full of flavor.
•	The Wiener Schnitzel was perfectly crispy and tender – one of the best we’ve had.
•	And the Kaiserschmarrn? Simply divine – fluffy, sweet, and unforgettable.

A special shout-out to our waiter Alfred, who was attentive, friendly, and went above and beyond to make our evening great. 🙌

Highly recommended for anyone visiting Graz and looking for top-notch Austrian cuisine with warm, professional service.

google_reviews.author: Polia Nedyalkova
google_reviews.text: Food is great! The atmosphere and music was lovely. The waiter Adam was so friendly and spoke really good English!

google_reviews.author: Przemek Piątek
google_reviews.text: Nice place, friendly service. Decent food. Definitely will come back one day.

google_reviews.author: Sebastian Pruckner
google_reviews.text: Best place in town🔥🔥

google_reviews.author: Ulrich Ahamer
google_reviews.text:
Café Stockwerk on Graz's Jakominiplatz is a wonderful, cozy pub, student bar, and bar...
The service is exceptionally attentive and very friendly and welcoming. Guests are greeted with a strikingly warm welcome.
There's regular, great live jazz music, and dance classes have also been offered recently.
The restaurant's decor is pleasantly dominated by wood, and in the warmer months, you can enjoy a pleasant outdoor seating area on the first floor under the arcades of the inner courtyard.
For the hungry, there are a few simple dishes, but hardly anyone comes to Café Stockwerk for the food. And around Jakominiplatz, there's endless food at every turn anyway.
So: If you like music, value a good draft beer (Schladminger Zwickl!), appreciate fine wines, or the occasional cocktail... then Café Stockwerk in Graz on Jakominiplatz is highly recommended!

google_reviews.author: Christian Bauer
google_reviews.text: I celebrated my birthday last Saturday at Stockach with about 30 people, and I can only say it was fantastic. Everything was absolutely perfect! The service, and even the owner himself, were extremely friendly and attentive. I organized a little pub quiz, and they proactively offered me suggestions on how to best implement it in the front area. I was given a microphone specifically for this, so I could play my own music in the front area without disturbing normal activity in the back. My guests all told me that the food was also very good. Unfortunately, I didn't get to try the legendary beer-battered chicken wings, but I'll definitely do that later. I can highly recommend the Zirbe, and I also liked the selection of different beers ;) In summary, I can say that Stockach is now one of the best bars in Graz for me.

google_reviews.author: Magdalena Wasserfaller
google_reviews.text: Café Stockwerk is a really nice place. Perfect location, right on Jakominiplatz. Whether for a drink, a birthday party (I celebrated my 30th there), or one of the many events like concerts, dance evenings, and dance workshops, whether alone or in a larger group, there's something for everyone. The service is friendly, and there's a good selection of drinks. I especially loved the special spritzers.

google_reviews.author: Martina “nana” Polecova
google_reviews.text: Excellent service, caffee and cakes. Unfortunately the place really smells after smoke what a bit disturbed the atmosphere, besides this it was everything all right.

google_reviews.author: Satvika Sati
google_reviews.text: Lovely place to relax with a drink or a meal.

google_reviews.author: Jeremy Dodier
google_reviews.text: Great services

google_reviews.author: Fuad Imsic
google_reviews.text: Great

google_reviews.author: Gerti Dembler
google_reviews.text:
If you're a university student looking for something cheaper and with more personality than the soulless sawmill, you've come to the right place!

The owner makes every effort to create a pleasant atmosphere for everyone present.

google_reviews.author: Daniel Ebner
google_reviews.text:
This is my favorite Buschenschank near Graz. If you don't have the time or the will to go to a place in the South Styrian Wine Route, Wastl is a great alternative.

Considering the close proximity to the "city", the view, if seated outside, is as scenic as it gets. The indoor ambiance is rustic and cozy, the quality of the food is great, staff is friendly and caring and prices are comparably cheap, as is custom for these places.

Particularly noteworthy is the fact that the main courses are served with a delicious small salad of your choice as a side dish.

google_reviews.author: Liam Kiernan
google_reviews.text: Decent spot but charged us €8 for 2 slices of plain bread. Better options down the road

google_reviews.author: Wilhelm Graupner
google_reviews.text: Excellent view and white wine

google_reviews.author: chang oscar
google_reviews.text: very nice

google_reviews.author: Oksana Gavris
google_reviews.text: Super!

google_reviews.author: Karin KG
google_reviews.text:
Large portions of average quality. The food is rather basic. Exactly what you'd expect from a self-service restaurant.

However, the staff is consistently disappointing. Especially at the food counter, their friendliness is abysmal.

google_reviews.author: Reinhur Reinhard
google_reviews.text:
🍽️ INTERSPAR Restaurant in Murpark: A clear recommendation!

The INTERSPAR Restaurant in Murpark completely won me over. The exceptionally friendly and attentive staff deserves special mention, making the visit a true pleasure. In addition, the restaurant is always clean and well-maintained, which makes the experience very pleasant.

The value for money is absolutely outstanding and hard to beat. You get quality and a generous portion for your money. I don't understand how anyone can still complain about the price. Compared to other options, it's still relatively inexpensive.

The positive experience is rounded off by the ideal location right in the center, making the restaurant easily accessible.

Service: ⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐

If you're looking for good, affordable, clean food in a friendly atmosphere at Murpark, this is the perfect place!

google_reviews.author: MarAuGra
google_reviews.text: You don't expect top quality. We ordered fried chicken today. Old grease, yellow and soggy meat. How can you ruin something so "decent"? Either it was kept warm for ages, not fried long enough, the meat was poor quality, or a combination of all of these. We left it. I've never eaten anything so awful! Absolutely unacceptable!

google_reviews.author: sarina pinczar
google_reviews.text: Great value for money. It's just a shame that they don't always offer pork or chicken schnitzel. I found the portion size of 4 cevapcici rather small – I could have easily eaten 6.

google_reviews.author: H L
google_reviews.text: Charging €4.20 for a salad labeled as a meal deal, just because it's not a meal deal, is simply extortion!

google_reviews.author: Hunter Mulligan
google_reviews.text: My second time traveling to Austria, I was greeted by a well English spoken person (Michael) Who provided great suggestions for the area, the menu and was overall the best host I could’ve hoped for! High suggest stoping by

google_reviews.author: Enes Berberi
google_reviews.text: That guy who works in the kitchen, Faizan, such a good guy. Makes a very good pizza and very funny as well

google_reviews.author: Károly Kasza
google_reviews.text:
Friendly place, nice service, great portions (also great price/value ratio).
Extremely dog friendly :)

google_reviews.author: GE
google_reviews.text: Years ago, they had great pizza at great prices. Unfortunately, now it's not nearly as good, and suddenly the prices are incredibly high. Almost 15 euros for a sadly subpar pizza.

google_reviews.author: Lukas schuchlenz
google_reviews.text: Friendly staff. Pizza not good, too much corn for example. I wouldn't recommend it.

google_reviews.author: Virgil Ionita
google_reviews.text: Just came back from having lunch here where I ordered the vegetarian menu. I ate the super which I enjoyed and then came the second dish, which was simply not eatable so I just put it to the side. First waitress asked me if everything is OK, I said no and the second waitress came to check. I said I just cannot eat because the taste is bad. Her reaction was: "there is nothing I can do about that" and then she pulled out her POS and I had to pay it but did not say anything about it. Next question was: " should I pack it for you?". I just smiled. I am not sure how can it be that this place has 4.5 stars but I will not go there anymore.

google_reviews.author: hagit nussinovitch
google_reviews.text: Great food, great price, really recommend this restaurant!

google_reviews.author: Ruei-Cheng Lin
google_reviews.text: Nice food, good for romantic dinner and after workdrink

google_reviews.author: M Y
google_reviews.text: A very pleasant place with a very nice interior and friendly owner. Delicious food and the dessert to sell the sole for. Surprisingly not crowded.

google_reviews.author: Marvin Lübbers
google_reviews.text: I was there because my work has a deal with them so I get 7.9€ off and only had to pay 2€ for a 2 course menu. No drinks included. It was good but just not my cup of tea. Didnt get a taste of their card either.

google_reviews.author: Clement Neo
google_reviews.text: One of the cheaper places I've come across in Austria. Food was good - you can customize your own pizzas, burgers etc. Price was good and variety of beer available. Servers were friendly and helped us translate the menu and understand how to order. Nice darker pub ambience and place to chill at night

google_reviews.author: Jose Wave
google_reviews.text: Nice place, nice food and nice people! Customizable burgers, fries, salads, pizza, kartoffel and many kind of beers! The portions are so big and the prices are more than good! The two waiters were so friendly, even one of them translate all the ingredients from German to English for us! We really appreciated that! Totally recommended! If we come back to Graz, we will come back to the Bierbaron for sure!

google_reviews.author: D C
google_reviews.text: Good burgers and pizzas for very good prices

google_reviews.author: Boryana Slavcheva
google_reviews.text: Nice young people, various kinds of beer and perfect for catching up with friends. Highly recommend if you are in town :)

google_reviews.author: Candy Zimmermann
google_reviews.text:
Location: as it is close by the University there are lots of youngsters.

Restaurant: in a wooden style and a little bit dark. They have a small outside area with about 7 tables.

Employees: were nice, young and friendly.

Bathroom: was Okey..

google_reviews.author: Mark B
google_reviews.text:
Found this bar by chance while visiting Uhrturm, They have the Best! Aperol Spritz 💯 the smoothest Aperol I ever had.
Prepared with love and attention by Ricardo ✨️

google_reviews.author: Ivana Trgalova
google_reviews.text: Perfect cozy place with good wine and great service.

google_reviews.author: Juho Ahlberg
google_reviews.text: Lovely place with a great selection of drinks and an extraordinary level of service!

google_reviews.author: Arda Deniz
google_reviews.text: Perfect atmosphere, kind staff, good taste

google_reviews.author: Haticenur Aykın
google_reviews.text: We spent quite good time. You should give a chance.

google_reviews.author: Adina Olau
google_reviews.text: The pizza was delicious. The profiterol was very good. Staff were very nice and the food arrived in 10-15 minutes. I recommend!

google_reviews.author: faisal alkaabi
google_reviews.text: One of my favorite restaurants in Austria, especially Graz. We sat outside with a beautiful panoramic view and a lovely atmosphere. The service was great, the food was delicious and irresistible, and the prices were reasonable for the service and delicious food. I recommend that everyone in Graz pass by the restaurant.

google_reviews.author: Paul Hackett
google_reviews.text:
Great pizza and tiramisu. Fantastic service and atmosphere.

Gluten free pizza is also available.

google_reviews.author: Denis
google_reviews.text: Restaurant was very busy at the time of our visit but we managed to get a table. We tried pizzas and Cesar salad. Everything was good, also the waitting time was quite short.  They offer variety of pizzas, pasta, salads and deserts. We also liked the relaxing italian music.

google_reviews.author: Tomiris Nurgaliyeva-Kamiński
google_reviews.text: It is a good place, but can’t say that it is outstanding in any way. Prices are not the cheapest, so you might expect something better, but it was a normal experience. It was tasty, good, but nothing outstanding. Waiters took very long to answer

google_reviews.author: Rad S
google_reviews.text: I have been to this restaurant, and also their other location. Sushi is very fresh. A reservation is highly recommended beforehand. Ideally for 2-3 people. Loved everything about the food and the service is also very good.

google_reviews.author: Addicted ToPhotos
google_reviews.text: Very kind people🫶 Fresh simple delicious sushi, without weird added stuff!

google_reviews.author: Oleksandr Serhiienko
google_reviews.text:
This place is the best sushi bar in Graz! The stuff there is mainly Japanese, and very kind. The menu doesn't have lot of positions,  but I find it as a plus, since you can be sure about the quality of products. This is very authentic and tasty place.
But be ready that you will need to reserve the place in advance,  since it is always overbooked. The place is pretty small, it can take 15 people at max, if you are more then 2 person, you will need to sit on the bar. The price there is above average,  but all this is worth trying it.

google_reviews.author: Sarah Lesjak
google_reviews.text:
08/2020

Delicious, high quality food in a cosy small sushi bar.

Yamamoto Graz offers typical dishes of the Japanese cuisine such as different types of maki rolls, nigiri and sashimi as well as warm dishes such as ramen. One can also have a bento box, a mix of warm and cold dishes, and many options are vegetarian as well. Almost all offered food is gluten free, upon request they serve gluten free soy sauce, however, it isn’t always available as it is rarely being asked for.

Service staff is alright. They are attentive and discrete, yet at some occasions I wasn’t sure if I made myself understood as the waiter answered quite brusquely. Nonetheless I have not had any issues with my order so far.
The meal is prepared freshly and it takes a reasonable time between ordering and serving. It is plated up appealingly and the portion size is quite generous.

Seating inside is very limited with two tables for 2 and some seats at the bar, where one can watch the chef prepare the dishes. Larger groups may be accommodated in the garden during the warm season as they have outside seating with tables that offer seating for up to 6 people comfortably.
As it can be very busy and is very well known, booking is highly recommended, especially when outside seating is not available.

google_reviews.author: Alice Lan
google_reviews.text: Very delicious of the 5 dishes on 10.08.2021. Super friendly waitress. We enjoy the lunch.

google_reviews.author: LemLem Oliveira
google_reviews.text: The coffee is great and so are the cakes! We tried the chocolate chip cookie, cherry chocolate cake and the red velvet muffin and we were not disappointed! The barista was very friendly and helped me choose some great coffee Xmas presents! Definitely worth a visit if you’re in the area!

google_reviews.author: Norbi Whitney
google_reviews.text:
Perfection. These guys know exactly what they’re doing.

Really great coffee, with a huge menu of hot and cold options (including non-coffee) as well as a great selection of sweets.

Friendly staff, milk alternatives, indoor and outdoor seating, plus loads of beans available for sale from themselves and other roasters.

Wish I was staying in Graz longer, as this would instantly become my daily go-to.

google_reviews.author: Mariette Sorgdrager
google_reviews.text: Such a cool coffee spot! Wonderful coffee, a lot of vegan treats, and super friendly baristas! I had a café latte with soy milk (there's also oat milk) and a lemon pistachio vegan banana bread 🤤 delicious!

google_reviews.author: Mauricio Azevedo
google_reviews.text: Certainly one of the best places around the university district to have a good coffee. The place is small, but the atmosphere is so nice that it is worth visiting. There’s a good variety of coffees and also small foods to eat. The bagel and the chocolate cake are specially delicious.

google_reviews.author: Coffee Lover
google_reviews.text: A bit expensive, but there is good specialty coffee. The staff was not very friendly, unfortunately they didn't even say that you could ask for latte art. This can only be seen on an exposed board. This really spoiled my coffee experience😞. You can't park in Street, only about 10 minutes away (paid).

google_reviews.author: Emma Hammett
google_reviews.text: Must try, amazing brunch

google_reviews.author: Marco Fellner
google_reviews.text: Super Location 🙏

google_reviews.author: Thomas .Baumgartner
google_reviews.text:
My wife and I won a breakfast from Radio Soundportal at Café Ritter.
Without this prize, we would have hardly found this place, as it only opened a month ago.
The restaurant is beautifully decorated both inside and out.
The entire ambiance, as well as the staff, make you feel immediately at home.
Breakfast for two costs €25 and includes pastries and drinks.
From salmon to prosciutto to olives, everything is included; the portions are more than sufficient for two people.
We will definitely come back. The food and drinks were excellent, and the service was friendly and attentive. The prices are absolutely reasonable for the quality offered.
Great restaurant and definitely worth a visit.

google_reviews.author: Henry Krieger
google_reviews.text: Although we had to inform the staff that we wanted to order, it was still quite nice. The latte was fantastic, and the cake was from BILLA.

google_reviews.author: Tom Tom
google_reviews.text: It was freezing cold in the restaurant because they apparently don't heat it overnight, and the staff left the courtyard door open that morning at the end of November. The door handle on the restroom has been loose since the reopening and is falling out, and the soap dispenser has seen better days. When a coffee costs almost €4, you'd expect everything to be okay. As another review described, "You feel like you're in your own living room," but given the temperature, that's not the case.

google_reviews.author: Dionysia Trampouli
google_reviews.text: It's been amazing going to Cafe Faudon! The atmosphere was great and the service was quick and polite 🙂 Everything was very clean and brand new! I undoubtedly suggest it!

google_reviews.author: stamatina mpirmpili
google_reviews.text: I loved it! Great service, the owner very kind and helpful! I will come again.

google_reviews.author: Nadia Sketo
google_reviews.text: Perfect ❤️❤️

google_reviews.author: Peter Katzbauer
google_reviews.text: I visited this Italian bar and was absolutely thrilled! The coffee from Trieste is top-notch and perfectly prepared. The selection of specialties is impressive, and every dish I tried was a delight. The atmosphere is inviting and authentic, which makes the visit even more enjoyable. Definitely a place I'd happily recommend!

google_reviews.author: Katharina Jeller
google_reviews.text:
It's simply a place you love to come back to!

Culinary delights, an incredibly friendly owner, great music, and a Mediterranean ambiance attract happy guests, and that's really important to me!

One thing that could be improved: opening hours from midnight to midnight 😉!

Looking forward to the next Prosecco, which they have on tap here and is accordingly delicious! 🥂

google_reviews.author: Isabella Unger
google_reviews.text: Not suitable for vegans! they only offer some types of bread as vegan options, no sweets or sandwiches.. very disappointing since more and more people are being vegan!

google_reviews.author: Anil Tilanthe
google_reviews.text:
One of my favourite bakeries.

Lot of discount offers during the closing time or earlier.

Great sweets and cakes 😍.

Staff is also very nice.

google_reviews.author: Kevin Pham
google_reviews.text: Staff was really nice and spoke English (I can’t speak German). The quiche/salad combo is so good. I would eat here frequently if I lived here.

google_reviews.author: Petra Katharina Schulz
google_reviews.text: It's unpleasant when countless birds feast on leftover food in the outdoor seating area, ashtrays overflow, trash is lying around, and countless glasses are left on the tables... etc. It doesn't make the atmosphere appealing... And the potato salad was cooked just barely enough to be palatable without being too raw... For these prices, Auer should try harder!

google_reviews.author: the crime:cat
google_reviews.text: I used to love going to this Auer. Simply because it's never busy and you get your rolls quickly. The quality has never been the best. The prices are actually outrageous for what they offer. Today, the employee even yelled at me... I'll never see them again!

google_reviews.author: thomas butler
google_reviews.text:
Overall a great game!
Not the result I think we are all looking for but that’s just the way things go. Nigeria had a great shot at it but lacked the attacking quality in the final third. Argentina have shown time and time again that they are able to deliver when they need to. And they had the players that could produce something special when needed.
Food was also good

google_reviews.author: Helton Calmon Rosa
google_reviews.text: Really nice place for dinner and some beers. They have some typical Austrian foods and the best scrum (typical drink in Austria). I have also tried the Cordon Bleu, one of the best in Austria. You can seat inside or outside, anywhere will be perfect. The only problem for non smoking people is that all restaurants people are allowed to smoke inside but all of them also have a non smoking area.

google_reviews.author: Mihaela Iancu
google_reviews.text: Delicious food and excellent local wine list. Fast and friendly service. Great location in the middle of historical center of Graz

google_reviews.author: Jen
google_reviews.text: Friendly staff, great services. The beef main course and the chocolate mousse taste amazing. Just the pumpkin soup is a bit salty.

google_reviews.author: Sandie Smith
google_reviews.text: A long wait for a table not helped by people moving to other tables on the terrace without discussion with staff.  A delicious meal ruined by some very poor service.  Just one example:  a 15-20 minute wait for the wine list is unacceptable.

google_reviews.author: Jyothish Atheendran
google_reviews.text: Very good service 👸. Average tasting food. Extremely overpriced 💸.

google_reviews.author: Gabriele Scandola
google_reviews.text: Low price and high quality. You can choose among different hot dishes, and you can take many different vegetables in your cup. You can take a reservation but it's not mandatory. Very good to have lunch in the campus. There are a lot of places to sit on, both externally and internally

google_reviews.author: Bsod Bsod
google_reviews.text: Extremely overpriced for what you get. I paid €18 for a portion of mediocre chicken wings, soggy fries, and a bland salad. For a Mensa, this price point is outrageous—elsewhere, you could get two to four proper meals for the same amount. The quality simply doesn’t justify the cost. TU Graz should seriously reconsider having this restaurant on campus and look for a provider with a better price-performance ratio that actually meets student needs.

google_reviews.author: Lukas Niederl
google_reviews.text:
Super expensive for the quality and small portions they deliver, especially considering it being a mensa. I just paid 18 € for an average quality burger with a small salad.
Having lunch below 10 € is impossible since the latest price hike.

google_reviews.author: Mahdiyar N.H.Gilani
google_reviews.text: Really reasonably priced food with large portions. The options at times are very limited if you have dietary requirements, however the food quality is always very good. It would be great if there was also an English menu and also if there was some sort of Halal options available. Overall, a good food court right in the middle of the campus and open to all!

google_reviews.author: Miroslav Vanek
google_reviews.text: Absolutely fantastic experience at Noonbar in Graz! 🍜 The ramen here is outstanding – from the delicious chicken crispy to the unique peanut butter ramen, and the chef’s special veggie ramen that was simply perfect. The flavors were rich, balanced, and unforgettable. The tea paired beautifully with the meal, making the evening even more enjoyable. Spotless hygiene and a warm atmosphere topped it all off. Highly recommended for anyone looking for an amazing dining experience in Graz!

google_reviews.author: Rabia Dogan
google_reviews.text:
I have mixed feelings about Noonbar. Each time we visit something feels weird about the service. Last time we visited, a man took our orders, he was giving us recommendations even though we didn't ask and he seemed to have been annoyed since we didn't follow his recommendations. I am not quite sure if I want to visit again even though I like the sushi there.

When you visit a restaurant you want to chill and enjoy the food. You don't wanna feel like someone is constantly trying to sell you something.

Good service is one thing, being pushy is another thing.

We were not very lucky with the delivery either. Once we ordered Ramen and gyoza. Ramen was really average and gyoza was not packed well, it had opened on the way and the food was all over the place.

I expect a better service for this price because it is not cheap at all.

google_reviews.author: Alex Kirilov
google_reviews.text:
I really wanted to like this place, but unfortunately, my experience wasn’t great. The service could be improved the waitresses seemed a bit lost and unsure about their work. I’m not trying to be rude, but good service makes a big difference in the overall dining experience.

About the food, it’s not really Japanese cuisine, more like an Asian fusion style. The ramen, for example, didn’t taste like traditional ramen. The broth should be clean and rich with chicken, beef, or pork flavor, but this one just felt like a random soup.

And one strange thing, I ordered a bottle of beer, and they poured it into a wine glass right in front of me. It was honestly confusing, especially in a country known for its beer culture.

google_reviews.author: Isabela Trufanda
google_reviews.text:
Love the food and the atmosphere, the Ramen is very tasty and well done. We tried Chicken Ramen, Roast Beef Sashimi and Shrimp rolls ♥️
The owner is very friendly and his recommendation are the best ♥️
When I will be back in Graz I will sure come here and eat again ♥️

google_reviews.author: Aischaa
google_reviews.text: This is one of my fav. restaurant. The food is amazing the pork belly despite having a lot of fat just melts in your mouth. You can taste that the ingredients are fresh and the meals are made with love. The drinks are also very good and they have a lot of non- alcoholic opinions. The restaurant is everything it promises and more.

restaurants.name: Gasthaus Kokol
restaurants.summary: Unassuming 19th-century inn offering an informal restaurant, modest rooms & free breakfast.

google_reviews.author: Peter Sziberth
google_reviews.text: Best food ,best price ,best cook ,best waiter , best entertainment !!!!😎😎

google_reviews.author: Steve Riederer
google_reviews.text: Unfriendly

google_reviews.author: Viktor Rusz
google_reviews.text: The food must be delicious, reading the comments, we were not lucky with that, only with the rooms upstairs. The whole place is in dire need of renovation, it looks like it hasn't been spent on it for many years! Windows are cracked (glued together with glue), you can't open them, bed is falling apart, wall switches and the whole kitchen are dirty, sticky to the touch. I think the furniture is from a trash removal. Otherwise, it would be in a good location away from the bustling city center. Plenty of parking, free wifi. I can't say anything else good about it... our company paid for it, luckily we were only here for 1 week.

google_reviews.author: Uwe JF
google_reviews.text:
Good, home-cooked food. Very well seasoned.
The terrace is small, but nice and sunny. The interior is nicely rustically furnished. The restrooms are no longer up to today's standards.

google_reviews.author: Tom Hirschi
google_reviews.text: An aging restaurant that needs more than a coat of paint. Very friendly staff, attentive and quick. The food is plentiful, but there's still room for improvement in terms of taste.

restaurants.name: KF Erde

google_reviews.author: Ignacia Sáiz
google_reviews.text: Very delicious! Totally recommend!

google_reviews.author: David
google_reviews.text: Good vegan food for fair prices.

google_reviews.author: Li Pau
google_reviews.text: It tastes so, so good, I always love coming here for lunch! I also think the prices are really okay.

google_reviews.author: D. F.
google_reviews.text: Great lunch menu, fair price, plenty of seating, friendly and efficient staff. Sometimes busy, but that's how it should be :-)

google_reviews.author: Magda Putz
google_reviews.text: Vegan without compromise, so so good food, model canteen for all of Austria

restaurants.google_address: Universitätsstraße, im ReSoWi 15/1. Stock, 8010 Graz, Austria

google_reviews.author: Julia Hoffer
google_reviews.text:
Cocktails very nice (but a little expensive). They have some special ones you've probably never tasted anywhere before, which is pretty cool. Nice atmosphere.
Only negative side: When I visited, the bartender working there was kinda rude and acting weirdly. But maybe there are other bartenders that are nicer and better with customers.

google_reviews.author: Pigeon
google_reviews.text:
Drinks were beautiful and the recommendation from the bar man we're on point for what we were looking !

Would be a 5* but the toilets require a lot of attention, no paper and felt unclean

google_reviews.author: Joann Mistica
google_reviews.text: Our new favorite bar. Cozy and competent barkeep. Music isn't too loud. The drinks and the ambience are pleasant.

google_reviews.author: Elija Kulmer
google_reviews.text: Splendid! Amazing Cocktails, great wine selection, smooth atmosphere. Highly recommend

google_reviews.author: Airon Delgado
google_reviews.text: Great place to share a drink after work, very nice atmosphere and staff...  For sure a great bar option to go to Graz, it was my first time but for sure not last time there

google_reviews.author: Natascha Eibl
google_reviews.text:
One of the best Thai restaurants in Graz. Pass Thai is delicious, also the soup and curries. The Laab could be a bit less spicy and with less roasted rice.
Also desserts are very good. But not much choice, your could include mango with sticky rice or something else like sweet Thai tea desserts.
Atmosphere and staff is very nice.
Choice of tea could also be bigger.

google_reviews.author: Daniel Boyd
google_reviews.text:
Quick service! Very helpful with adjusting dishes according to allergies.
Easy self serve drinks, delicious food- tastes absolutely amazing!
Something for everyone: vegan- gluten free- paleo - meat eaters
Will be coming back!

google_reviews.author: Alisa Kittirattanaporn
google_reviews.text: Authentic Thai food with very nice staffs!!

google_reviews.author: Belinda Viesca
google_reviews.text:
The food is AMAZING!!! Even my 3 year old loved it and ATE LOTS!!
We will definitely order from here again and we will be happy to go eat there once the lock down is over!

google_reviews.author: Qais Baqain
google_reviews.text:
The place is small and good. The staff is very friendly! The prices for the food are quite good for the food/amount that you get :)
You can pay with cash or card and you can even but some ingredients from the place directly

google_reviews.author: Dimas Otaño
google_reviews.text: A tat expensive expensive in general,6.4€ for a Guinness, but in general it's a very nice place to hangout. Good music too.

google_reviews.author: berke ayrancioglu
google_reviews.text: A nice pub close to the city center, near Lendplatz. The place is a bit small but warm. There are many drink options including various beers. Waiter was really kind and friendly. The music was good. This is one of the rare pubs in the city that is open after the midnight

google_reviews.author: paolo
google_reviews.text: I´m part of an ethnic minority and I was insulted  by a group of Germans with apparently  the good being of the Austrian  landlord .. since nothing was said in my defense...typical from this fake tolerant Graz scene...fact...no International beers, no food, stench of smoke, music ok, unfortunately the only place open till late in the city center, ...not much choice than  I think they like it that way don't they?

google_reviews.author: Chris Peel
google_reviews.text: Good atmosphere with plenty of people but get there early if you want a seat because it’s very small and pretty dark. It’s also nothing like an English pub but still a nice enough place to visit. Great pizzas.

google_reviews.author: H Williams
google_reviews.text: Very friendly and knowledgeable staff. Pizza from Contra Punto next door was really great too. I loved the music too, very nostalgic.

google_reviews.author: Gyorgy Rakoczy
google_reviews.text: Very cosy restaurant. Staff was very kind and the food was delicious. Try pork belly and kaiserschmarnn with eiscream for two persons which was a killer. They also have weissbier and good dark beer. Meals for kids also available.

google_reviews.author: Zoltan L
google_reviews.text: Limited menu choices, mediocre food for slightly above average price, slow and inattentive service. The atmosphere of the place good, too bad for the negatives. Expected more based on others reviews. For me this fell short.

google_reviews.author: Johan Zegers
google_reviews.text:
Authentic restaurant in tje city center of Graz. They make the Snitzels themselves, fresh when they are ordered, you can hear them preparing them in the kitchen.

The owner greets you at the entrance and makes sure that the waiters inside are taking care of you.

Food, beer and wine are all good. Good place for a revisit. Nothing fancy, just authentic Austrian food.

google_reviews.author: Nick Hopwood
google_reviews.text: If you know the staff you’ll have a lovely time. If not, don’t bother. I sat there so long being ignored until a customer told the waiter to come over. Then they forgot my order so I sat there waiting for ages. Food was overpriced.

google_reviews.author: Mirza Memisahovic
google_reviews.text: Imagine ordering a Cordon Bleu and a Schnitzel, finishing the driest plate ever and then the check arrives.... you have to pay for the blob of ketchup AND two lemon wedges? The price you ask? 1.50 euro for blob of ketchup and 2 euro for a lemon wedge. Amazing, great!

google_reviews.author: Simone Prattes
google_reviews.text: David is one of the nicest waiters ever!😊 Saskia is a doll😍 epic food, nice drinks - go and check it out💪🏼

google_reviews.author: Audrone Telesiene
google_reviews.text: Typical. Average. Normal.

google_reviews.author: Manuela Swinn
google_reviews.text:
I had the pleasure of experiencing Liebig's catering for a special celebration – and I'm absolutely thrilled! Everything was prepared with such attention to detail, and the delicious finger food was not only a true delight but also of the highest organic quality. I was particularly impressed by how flexibly and thoughtfully they catered to all needs: from meat lovers to vegan and gluten-free guests – everyone was truly happy!

The on-site support was extremely attentive, pleasantly calm, and incredibly friendly. I felt completely at ease. The communication beforehand during the planning phase was also excellent – ​​incredibly helpful, open to all requests, and professionally executed.

It was simply perfect in every way – a huge THANK YOU to the entire Liebig team! I wholeheartedly recommend Liebig and am already looking forward to our next celebration together.

google_reviews.author: Alexandra Jammernegg
google_reviews.text:
We visited Liebig for the first time this evening and were absolutely thrilled. Almost everything is organic, seasonal, and regional, with a small carbon footprint and zero waste. And the whole atmosphere is very relaxed; there's even a small zero-waste shop inside.

Great selection of drinks, especially fantastic non-alcoholic options, and also very good (natural) wines.

We had the antipasti platter for two (vegan/vegetarian), two different bruschetta, and then the large tapas sampler. We also had espresso (served in cups made from recycled coffee grounds) and a truly moist and aromatic lemon-lavender cake.

Everything was perfectly seasoned (I fell in love with the carrot salmon!), beautifully presented, and plentiful.

The service was attentive and very friendly.

The owner (?) is very knowledgeable about wine, and you can tell how proud he is of his selection.

Verdict: Highly recommended. We'll be trying their amazing lunch specials soon 💕.

google_reviews.author: Sandra
google_reviews.text: I stumbled upon this restaurant by chance today, and I was delighted. The atmosphere was fantastic, the service was absolutely lovely, and my meal was a sensational taste experience. I felt very welcome and will be back.

google_reviews.author: Sam Ansari
google_reviews.text: I Just love Austria and gratkron is beautiful like me. Fernello have some magic to keep people new taste 😊

google_reviews.author: Klaus Zlöbl
google_reviews.text: Good cakes and ice cream. Good service.

google_reviews.author: Patricia Brocker
google_reviews.text: Lovely Cafe delicious cakes service excellent

google_reviews.author: traian puscas
google_reviews.text: Ok

google_reviews.author: Engelbert Schlogel
google_reviews.text: Good

google_reviews.author: Marianna Bachledova
google_reviews.text:
The food was very good, but having to install an app to order is a bit of an overkill. Well, you can order with the waiter, but you must be familiar with the menu which I was obviously not since I came here for a business trip.

Guys, pls, just give us a normal menu on a paper, this is so customer-unfriendly. I don't want to install an app to get a beer.

google_reviews.author: David Cerkez
google_reviews.text: I visited twice, and I thoroughly enjoyed it. The music was excellent, the service was outstanding given their workload, and the prices were more reasonable than at other pubs in Graz.  It had a lively, youthful atmosphere. Overall, it was a great experience.

google_reviews.author: Janwillem Heikens
google_reviews.text: I love this Pub. I have been going here for years and the food is great! Especially since it is 'just a pub'. What the cook can do with what is basically just a small Foreman grill is spectacular. Additionally I would like add that when it is busy they always tell me the food will take a while. Anyone that expects a pub with a tiny grill to serve 100 people within 20 minutes has never operated a small grill in their life. Highly recommend!

google_reviews.author: Alyssa Moore
google_reviews.text:
The food was good, unfortunately our group had a very unfortunate encounter with the waitress. We arrived as a pretty large group, but the place was almost empty. The waitress was unbearable. She was walking around kicking chairs to move them, and attending to us aggressively. We tried to pay at the end, not even splitting bills, and she was very scary, yelling at us to speak in German, and she felt moments away from being physical. It was horrible and she made our group feel upset and unsafe.

I do need to comment that the waiter (or maybe chef) was very nice and tried to smooth out the situation. He was also very pleasant when he delivered food or took our plates.

It was definitely not cool to be treated this way when we were a large group, very calm, and offering a lot of business.

google_reviews.author: Zoltán Tóth
google_reviews.text: Spent two night here with our handball team, super friendly stuff who are trying their best even in the rush hour and happy to take a drink and talk with you when it's over. The offered Mexican dishes are also very delicious, the great selection of evergreen rock'n'roll hits from the loudspeakers is just the icing on the cake. We'll be back for sure! ;)

google_reviews.author: Lucija Jakuš
google_reviews.text: Great place with amazing Poke. Every time I visit my firend in Graz, we eat here and will continue to do so. 2 vegan options available

google_reviews.author: Tomiris Nurgaliyeva-Kamiński
google_reviews.text:
The worker was super friendly, while the pokes were stunning too!
Little queues even on weekends,
So recommend going there if you need a fast snack

google_reviews.author: Claudia Carpena Höll
google_reviews.text: The best pokes in Graz! Love the food, and ambient. And very well located in the city.

google_reviews.author: Kathy Tudor
google_reviews.text: An unexpected find in Graz. I had a tuna poke bowl and it was a generous size and tasty. I had enough left over for the next day.

google_reviews.author: Oleksandr Protsenko
google_reviews.text: Tasty, fresh, fast, convenient, service including in English. Bravo!

google_reviews.author: Debjyoti Banerjee
google_reviews.text: Ceylon Curry, a restaurant located in Lend Graz, serves South Indian cuisine. While the menu offers a variety of options, the spiciness level is generally lower compared to other South Indian establishments. We had dinner at Ceylon Curry and found the chicken mango curry to be quite intriguing. The owner, who is originally from Sri Lanka and has a South Indian cultural background, is responsible for the restaurant’s culinary offerings. The menu includes popular dishes such as dosa, medu wada, veg pakora, and fish pakora.

google_reviews.author: Umesha Thathsarani
google_reviews.text:
Ceylon Curry Restaurant never disappoints! I tried their chicken kottu and it was incredibly delicious full of flavor, perfectly cooked, and packed with that authentic Sri Lankan taste. The portion was generous, and every bite felt fresh and satisfying.

Highly recommended for anyone craving real Sri Lankan food. I’ll definitely be coming back again!

google_reviews.author: Anita Velar
google_reviews.text:
We had paper dosa & chicken curry meal.
Paper dosa was missing the thin crisp.  Could have been crisper and thinner for paper dosa. Ordered seeni sambal which was nice, chicken curry was a tad too bland, missing the spices, could only taste the cloves.
The sides of chana masala and dhal and beans were good! Staff were friendly.

google_reviews.author: Thao Pham
google_reviews.text: We got the big Platter for 2 with Butter Chicken and Chili Chicken. The food was super and can be refilled too if needed. We were satisfactorily full. The only complaint we have is that the food took 40 mins to arrive. But at least the waiting is worthy

google_reviews.author: SN M (SNM bornwithwings)
google_reviews.text: They have options for delicious south indian food. Idli Dosa sambhar Medu wada etc. We had vegetarian food. Their Masala Chai is very good. The owners themselves takes orders and serve. Place is dimlit and little cold. But food is very tasty. They have other Srilankan delicacies

google_reviews.author: Veronika
google_reviews.text: Lovely small café with fresh pastries, excellent coffee and attentive staff! Opens at 5am so a great spot for early risers.

google_reviews.author: tarrantino
google_reviews.text: Always friendly and very welcoming! Cozy atmosphere and delicious pastries. Whenever I walk by with my dog, we always get a friendly smile. Thank you so much! :-)

google_reviews.author: Gerald B.
google_reviews.text: I've been here for breakfast several times now, and I'm always impressed. It's rare to find such friendly service. And the products are great, too!

google_reviews.author: Clemens Unterberger
google_reviews.text: Always very friendly service and good bread. While the selection isn't comparable to large bakeries, I find that positive; they sell what they have and certainly don't throw much away.

google_reviews.author: Jutta Sieber
google_reviews.text:
Anyone looking for delicious sandwiches, baked goods, and a Viennese Melange with a friendly smile early in the morning (5 a.m.) in Graz should definitely stop by Gustotheke. This owner-operated restaurant also offers limited seating for immediate enjoyment.
Insider tip: Sandwiches are freshly prepared to order on Saturdays.

google_reviews.author: Saso Turnsek
google_reviews.text: Really good sweets and bread, don't mention the coffee

google_reviews.author: WH Z
google_reviews.text: Never visit again

google_reviews.author: Messi 2014
google_reviews.text: Best Döner in the world

google_reviews.author: Max M
google_reviews.text: Probably the worst doner kebab I've ever eaten. The only positive thing was that I didn't get food poisoning. The meat and bread were cold and dry. I don't even want to know how long it had been sitting there.

google_reviews.author: Judith Pataki
google_reviews.text:
The taste was awful.

The pizza tasted only of cheese. If you order a pizza with bacon, you expect to get some—instead, they put cheap sliced ​​ham on it. "Stick to what you know."

restaurants.name: Burger King
restaurants.summary: Well-known fast-food chain serving grilled burgers, fries & shakes.

google_reviews.author: Nikola Sretic
google_reviews.text:
I'm every day here, sometimes twice a day. It is nice atmosphere, staff is polite and well trained. We have always a nice communication. Restaurant is clean, orders are correct, prepared and served really fast.
10/10, im recommending it if you are a fast food fan.

google_reviews.author: Eva Lednik
google_reviews.text: ME AND MY FRIENDS GOT FOOD POISONING me and my friends went to this burger king and ordered the food.All of us got a weird white spicy sauce with it that none of us ordered with. We ate but we felt weird right after. Our eyes were really swollen as were our lips to the point we thought we got drugged. I would just recommend you go to McDonalds right around the corner!

google_reviews.author: Evelyn J
google_reviews.text: Literally junk food! I found label in my cheese (look at the photo)

google_reviews.author: Roumaissaa Maissa
google_reviews.text: I had an incredible experience at #Jakominiplatz thanks to the amazing staff. From the moment I walked in, I was met with professionalism, they making sure customers felt valued, they handled everything with care. What stood out the most was their kindness. This location truly sets a high standard for customer care. I highly recommend visiting Jakominiplatz if you want an experience that’s efficient, professional, Cool and filled with heart.

google_reviews.author: Jyothish Atheendran
google_reviews.text: Good food. Good service. Enough space to eat in. Use the Burger King 👑 App if you want to avail good discounts. You can also order in advance and have the food ready when you reach there without much waiting time.

google_reviews.author: Stefan Friedl
google_reviews.text:
Nice "historic" atmosphere, service is friendly but sometimes hiding. The food quality is at a high level but still somewhat disappointing, just nothing special - we had the daily/weekly 4-course special (Feinschmecker Menu) for dinner.
Nevertheless, the overall package is definitely worth a visit and if you are after sweets it's must!

google_reviews.author: Moritz K.
google_reviews.text:
The most beautiful coffee house in Graz!
Amazing value for money and truly great service!

I happened to stroll into the Erzherzog Johann coffee house today. My brother had told me it was supposed to be good, and today it was a perfect fit.
I was no less amazed when I found nothing less than the most beautiful coffee house in Graz! This coffee house opens up to an absolutely enormous space, bursting with beauty. Plants, beautiful colors, and artistic wallpapers everywhere you look, and beautiful furniture that fits perfectly.
A glance at the menu revealed the next highlight. The prices are normal, as elsewhere, but in combination with this location and the service, they're really great!

The coffee came so quickly that I didn't take my photo until afterward, I was so busy looking!

I will be coming here regularly from now on and enjoying this place!

google_reviews.author: Zoltán Barta
google_reviews.text: A cafe with a unique atmosphere, with quality products and friendly service. In the summer heat, lemon soda here gives the tourist new strength. :)

google_reviews.author: Renate Z.
google_reviews.text: A very nice café in a hotel in the old town. They even offer coffee and cake. Very friendly service. I'd be happy to come back.

google_reviews.author: Tibor
google_reviews.text: Great atmosphere and amazing coffee! Definitely worth a visit!

google_reviews.author: Lisbora Likaj
google_reviews.text: Super cool place, cozy and very heart-warming interior! I really like the combination of white and green, and all of the plants inside. Coffee is great, cakes as well. Especially the Sachertorte is reallyy good! They have different options for breakfast (check the menu). A bit expensive compared to other restaurants and coffee places but 100% worth it. Don’t forget to have a walk around the lake and enjoy the nature 💚

google_reviews.author: Aleister
google_reviews.text:
The ambience is very nice and cozy, however I felt like the food was overpriced for what is being served to you.
The service is terrible and our cakes were quite tasteless plus the wrong cake was brought, but since we had to wait for a really long time till we could even give our order in the first place, we didn’t want to complain.
In my opinion it’s a bit disappointing to have such a beautiful location but then having a staff that ruins the experience.

google_reviews.author: Aleksiij
google_reviews.text:
Relaxing place with style
Great evening relaxed time with friend and lovely lunchtime on the terrace …. Food excellent…. Service extra efficient and kind ❤️

google_reviews.author: Virginia Marisela Salinas Guajardo
google_reviews.text:
Super nice to take a nice coffee and relaxing. The cakes are deli! I enjoyed so much.
Take a friend to have a nice chat 😉
Also you can take a walk around the lake. Take some food for the fish as soon as they see you they look for food!

google_reviews.author: Felix Olschewski
google_reviews.text: Beautiful building with an excellent location right next to the lake. Staff are polite, it's dog-friendly, all around a great place to have a quick coffee or brunch

google_reviews.author: Genc Demjaha
google_reviews.text:
Back Again – Even Better the Second Time!

I first visited Pink Elephant last year and had such a great experience that I absolutely had to come back—and it did not disappoint! Located in an extraordinary spot right in the heart of Graz, this place continues to impress with its vibrant atmosphere and top service.
The staff was incredibly helpful and welcoming, just like I remembered. A special mention goes to Daniel, the talented barman, who once again delivered drinks that were not only delicious but prepared with real dedication and professionalism. His attention to detail and friendly approach added so much to the overall experience.
Whether it's your first visit or your fifth, Pink Elephant is a must-stop in Graz for anyone who appreciates quality drinks, great service, and a perfect city centre location. Can’t wait to return again!

google_reviews.author: johnny
google_reviews.text: The bar is very nice, amazing cocktails and good food… the only problem i had with it was the service. We had such a bad experience with the waiter.. He was extremely rude and felt as if we were unwanted there because we were tourists. Besides that, the cocktails taste as good as they look..

google_reviews.author: megha sheth
google_reviews.text: We ordered loaded nachos and it was good but I didn't get pico de gallo salsa. I asked the server girl and she gaslighted me to believe it doesn't come with it. I didn't cross check until home and it was. Otherwise food was ok. Campari was bad.

google_reviews.author: Stefano Rossi
google_reviews.text: Extremely long wait just to order. English menu was not updated so I had to translate everything with Google translate and wait another 10 minutes to be able to order. The food was ok but nothing special.

google_reviews.author: Majestic Sunset
google_reviews.text:
TLDR: If you want good drinks and atmosphere come here. Don't come here for the "Mexican" food.

As a Mexican coming to Austria, I expected the food here to not be anything like the states or Mexico. I know I won't come by a truly authentic Mexican restaurant and that the Mexican food here would be bad. Unfortunately, I was right. The food here was bad COMPARED to the USA/Mexico; I understand the cooking traditions here are different, but unfortunately for "Mexican" food, this restaurant is bad.

Bad aspects include...
Food: The salsa was very lukewarm; as a tomato based dish, it should be served cold as patrons can get sick from tomatoes that sit in room temperature for too long. The "nachos" were not true Nachos, the menu should just say "Tortilla Chips."" If you want Nachos, then you have to order the most expensive "Nacho" that has cheese on it. The burritos were very mediocre and came with a side of ranch that didn't pair well. The ranch is most likely a cultural aspect that the local patrons seemed to enjoy, I didn't enjoy it. The burritos and tacos should come served with salsa rojo or verde. Meat was not seasoned with any Mexican spice, but the guacamole was quite good!

Prices: The prices reminded me of a gentrification taco restaurant in Los Angeles, everything was overpriced. For the flavors I tasted, the burritos should be £10 MAX. It's insane how the small churros cost £7.90. The prices for the margaritas were okay.

Good aspects include...
Drinks: The drinks here were the best part. The Mexican Latte was essentially hot Horchata, which was delightful! The margaritas were ok, and their "Bio-Lemonade with Qinta" was good.

Atmosphere: Great atmosphere with nice servers who speak English.

google_reviews.author: McDonald's Graz
google_reviews.text: Brötchen Rainer has catered our company event. They were doing and excellent job! Both the quality of the products and the service were 5 starts! Thank you very much for the outstanding service! :)

google_reviews.author: O. H.
google_reviews.text: My very first sandwich in town. It was amazing!

google_reviews.author: amy hajdar
google_reviews.text: Top!!!

google_reviews.author: Charles Bernard
google_reviews.text: Also a pick up spot.

google_reviews.author: peter hoedl
google_reviews.text: BBB.p.

google_reviews.author: Jon Kern
google_reviews.text:
What a wonderful find in Graz! Traditional local restaurant with super friendly staff, great food and a great atmosphere.

When I asked about the beer, she said it was a very small brewery. She even went into the basement to look at the keg to find the exact name. But she knew it was Hausmi from St. Stefaner Private Brewery, and she shared the info from her phone. Way above and beyond the norm!

There was also a foursome playing a fun bar game. Most commonly in Austria and southern Germany it is called "Der kluge Bauer" ("The Clever Farmer") or sometimes "Fünfeck" (Pentagon).

About 20 Euro per person for a meal and drinks.

google_reviews.author: Andor Fabian
google_reviews.text: All food apart from mouldy salad was great. Nice krugel of cold Puntigamer; cozy, friendly space, quick service, moderate prices. 👍

google_reviews.author: Koen Zomers
google_reviews.text:
August 2025:
Returned to Graz with my family to have them experience the food here as well. Kids had the house burger, which was a huge and tasty hamburger with fries for just 11 euro. We took the lunch meal of the day for just 9,90 euro which today (a Friday) meant all you can eat spaghetti Bolognese, crispy fried fish, baked potatoes, rice, onion soup and a salad buffet. All as much as you wanted. Super good deal. And tasty. Big glass of cold refreshing water costs just 70 eurocents. Friendly staff. Just disguisting that sitting outside in the terrace was impossible due to the cigarette smoke of others. We moved inside. Floor in the restaurant was sticky and toilets really could use repairs.

2023:
Found this place while searching for large schnitzels. Prices are amazingly low here although the prices shown on the menu outside are sightly higher than they are on the menu you get at your table. Not sure which of the two I ended up paying. In either case they're low. I ordered the schnitzel first. Really nice size. Crispy and quite okay taste. As I was very hungry and mostly curious I ordered the cordon blue next. Also of large size but not really my kind of cordon blue. They apparently have a lot of local twists to the cordon blue. This one was with green peppers, white onions and a bit sour tasting cheese. Not really my kind of taste. Beer was good, staff friendly. American Express was supposed to work but didn't. A normal person would definitely have enough with one main course.

google_reviews.author: Tom Peddle
google_reviews.text:
