A very reasonably priced eatery, if slightly out of the old town. Worth the walk.

Service was nigh-on instantaneous and with a smile. Good portions and the cheese dumpling soup was delightful. Shall no doubt revisit!

google_reviews.author: Irina Brinzi
google_reviews.text: Incredible food, the meat was so tender and the potatoes were amazing. Such a nice and authentic experience, 100% recommend!

google_reviews.author: Korina Kyriakou
google_reviews.text: Came here often for ice cream and I love the authentic Italian vibe you get from the shop. Loved most of the ice cream flavours too, the cakes not as much, as the creams in them were a bit too stiff, but will definitely be coming back for many ice creams! And portions are very generous ♥️

google_reviews.author: megha sheth
google_reviews.text: Didn't buy anything  yet. But we went in to buy just glass of milk for my toddler on Sun eve when everything was closed. To our surprise, the owner gave a whole unopened bottle for free. Very kind ans generous owner 😊

google_reviews.author: Miraxh Jahja
google_reviews.text: Hands down one of the best ice creams I’ve tried. Fresh, smooth, and full of flavor.

google_reviews.author: Danny Cullinane
google_reviews.text: After a grey day in Graz, the sun started shining and the ice cream cones were calling. I chose two scoops, hazelnut yoghurt over Crème Veneziana. The top scoop was nutty, the bottom had a delicious chocolate swirl. A very good choice of very good ice creams.

google_reviews.author: Jacky V.
google_reviews.text: I wish I could review this place, but their opening hours marked in their own window are inaccurate. It says open until 23:00 every day. We went at 21:00 on a Saturday night and it was closed.

google_reviews.author: Manfred G.
google_reviews.text: Before meeting up with an old school friend, I had some time for a quick coffee. I had a pressing need and asked about the restroom when I ordered. A friendly young woman, apparently the owner, assured me that using the toilet wouldn't be contingent on buying anything. The coffee was good and very reasonably priced. The clientele was a mixed bunch, from young to old, and friendly throughout. A truly cozy neighborhood café that reminds me of when Graz was still a beautiful city full of pensioners and students. A welcoming time capsule, especially in this area around the train station, where nothing has improved.

google_reviews.author: Nicole Fischer
google_reviews.text:
I wasn't there to buy anything, but solely to pick up a package. Since GLS didn't leave a delivery slip, I had to rely on the opening hours listed here on Google. So I arrived around 7:00 PM. Apparently, packages can only be handed out until 6:00 PM, which I wasn't aware of. A woman immediately insulted me and accused me of being illiterate. The owner of the establishment, however, was relatively friendly. As I was about to leave, someone shouted after me again, saying I probably couldn't read and was too stupid to pick up my package at the right time.

I've rarely experienced such rudeness. The clientele gives a very antisocial impression.

google_reviews.author: Finley Förster
google_reviews.text: Great café! I'll definitely be back.

google_reviews.author: Nicolas W
google_reviews.text: Very traditional restaurant with excellent service.

google_reviews.author: Alexander Fink
google_reviews.text:
Great atmosphere and an extremely friendly and attentive manager!
Always happy to come back ☺️

google_reviews.author: Anita Wurzinger
google_reviews.text: Very good interaction with friends. On Fridays, Klaus serves excellent food. Chairwoman Luise does everything to make you feel comfortable.

google_reviews.author: Andreas Krasser
google_reviews.text: Wonderful neighborhood center and open meeting center in the Jakomini district

google_reviews.author: Mateo Milicevic
google_reviews.text: Right at the entrance we were greeted with a handshake and a big smile. What a way to leave a great first impression! The restaurant was packed, but we were immediately seated. I love my carbs and naan bread is usually the most convenient thing for me to compare across all Indian restaurants I've been to on my travels along the years, and this one (garlic naan), hands down, was the best one I have ever had! Along with it, we had butter curry chicken, Jhinga Vindaloo and a paneer salad. The portions are good and everything was so full of taste and flavor. For dessert we had Gulob Jamun and Kheer Khajuraho which was a nice way to finish the whole meal. The staff was very attentive and even took time to teach me some Hindi phrases. This restaurant is a must-visit if you like good Indian food, great atmosphere and outstanding service. Definitely know where to come back next time I'm in Graz. Dhanyavaad! :)

google_reviews.author: Graham Patterson
google_reviews.text: This was one of the best dining experiences of my life!! My friend and I enjoyed our meals very much and were surprised at the affordability. The owner was very kind and welcoming, and after we paid he asked us about our travels and told us how much he appreciated us spending time in his restaurant. Truly a wonderful spot, will definitely be revisiting should we find ourselves out here again.

google_reviews.author: Kristina Kušar
google_reviews.text: We had a very nice dinner in Ganesha. The waiter was friendly. We orderd paneer, butter chicken, garlic naan, rice. We were the first guests in the evening, and we liked that the restaurant was quiet.

google_reviews.author: Eureka Wagner
google_reviews.text: Very authentic and welcoming atmosphere. Huge variety of dishes, be it vegetarian or with meat. The Daal Tarka was very creamy (despite being without any cream) and the paneer naan crispy. Only the pakora could have been with more variety and quantity of vegetables, but all in all good taste. Thank you for the experience :)

google_reviews.author: Marc Wernli
google_reviews.text: Excellent Indian dishes, spices are delicious.  The owner and his staff are ery friendly and helpful. If you are looking for Indian gmfood in Graz this is a must try.

google_reviews.author: Kent Siebeneck
google_reviews.text: Excellent Moroccan food in the center of Gratz! The humus was probably the best I have had in Austria, and the chicken tangine and couscous were wonderful and took me back to amazing meals in Marrakech. Highly recommend!

google_reviews.author: Jeff Straathof
google_reviews.text: Food at Ya Habibi is superb! My wife and I traveled three weeks in Europe, and this restaurant was easily our favorite. Food is flavorful, and of high quality. Be sure to get the tabbouleh.

google_reviews.author: ghofrane nasri
google_reviews.text:
Very delicious food and warm atmosphere.
The quantity is generous.
The stuff is nice and friendly. Abdellatif was so kind to us .
We loved the atmosphere and would definitely go back

google_reviews.author: Tarik Causevic
google_reviews.text:
When I first found the restaurant and saw it had 5.0 rating (40+ reviews) on Google Maps I had my doubts, sounds too good. But, just as the pictures and stories of other reviewers showed, it deserves the rating.

The staff is very friendly, the chef takes his time to recommend dishes. The place is cozy with an oriental vibe. And then the food. Oh, the food. So good. So good!

Highly recommending a visit.

google_reviews.author: Rio Olio
google_reviews.text: We came here with high expectations because of the ratings on Google. And YES, it was a very very nice dinner for us. The foods were very very tasty. They do not have soooo many on their menu, so you will not confuse yourself. The best one for me is the hummus and couscous. The drink was also amazing. Definitely coming back if we are visiting Graz again.

restaurants.name: Subway
restaurants.summary: Casual counter-serve chain for build-your-own sandwiches & salads, with health-conscious options.

google_reviews.author: Richard Healy
google_reviews.text:
Just called in for a late night meal. The server was hardly conscious.
I ordered a foot long chicken sandwich. The displayed price was €5,99. He changed €9;99. Asked for my money back.

google_reviews.author: Noam Ozer
google_reviews.text:
The service was really bad.
The staff was not kind at all and impatient, hardley responsive for what I asked him for. He left my bread in the oven for  way to long , put the sauce on the out side on the bread and put very little of the vegetables I asked for, my bread was messy and everything fell out right when I tried to eat. The staff was just  not really interested at making the food.

google_reviews.author: Melissa Zalomir
google_reviews.text: I come to eat at this place regularly. The food is always fresh and the prices are good too.

google_reviews.author: Тимур Лісовський
google_reviews.text: Floor is a bit dirty, service is good just because they know English, food is really tasty.

google_reviews.author: pspsawasdee
google_reviews.text: The employee doesn’t care about his job at all. Too little sauce too little veggies. Everything fell apart.

google_reviews.author: Gwen LG
google_reviews.text: I did not even order as the waitress was unfriendly like never seen in my life

google_reviews.author: Regina Emeye
google_reviews.text: They make things easier for we Africans. Iike them and they are very friendly

google_reviews.author: Matej Bunderla
google_reviews.text: A nice place to drink a fast coffee

google_reviews.author: Appia Yaboah
google_reviews.text: The place is well and nice thanks you

google_reviews.author: Marcus Krebs
google_reviews.text: is nice 👌🏻

google_reviews.author: Maximilian Pleyer
google_reviews.text:
Best Gelateria in Graz, daily freshly made Gelato, firm consistency, great flavour, wide choice of flavors and less sugar. 👍🏼
We also had a seasonal Schilcher Sturm Wein ~ amazingly delicious 🤗😍

google_reviews.author: P Huff
google_reviews.text: Good chocolate gelato on a cone!

google_reviews.author: Nikola Hanušová
google_reviews.text: Absolutely perfect! Thanks for doing such a good job.

google_reviews.author: Tomislav Vukovic
google_reviews.text:
Nema pola-pola
They dont offer half-half scoups

google_reviews.author: Wolfi Wallner
google_reviews.text: Ok

google_reviews.author: Raluca Müller
google_reviews.text: The Pizza for the kids was delicious, also salads. Their baked brrad is fantastic. For the Kids Menü, they get small presents and juices..a hit

google_reviews.author: Karin KG
google_reviews.text:
I used to enjoy visiting Paradiso in the early mornings for a quiet coffee and some honest breakfast but since it reopened recently it's no longer appealing.
The renovation is well done but not all that glitters is gold! Breakfast options are rather boring and way too expensive for what is on offer.
What a disappointment!!!

google_reviews.author: Mr. Vu
google_reviews.text: This is definitely for the locals. The location and views are amazing, the food is good, and the service of the staff are on-par with what you would see in an American restaurant! I was served by a lady named Kali, and she absolutely makes you feel at home :)

google_reviews.author: Alice Dylan
google_reviews.text: This restaurant is a bit hard to find if you aren’t a local! We both had gnocchi, one portion was overcooked (and small), the other one was just perfect. The pesto, on the other hand, was divine. I’d recommend the Limoncello Spritz, which not many restaurants serve. For the price, this is a decent option for food in Graz.

google_reviews.author: Kwanchanok
google_reviews.text: Comfortable place, away from crowded,good taste ! Will come back

google_reviews.author: Karin KG
google_reviews.text:
A culinary work of art! Artis impresses not only with its excellent ingredients and creative cooking, but also with the artistic presentation of the dishes. Nothing is left to chance here. Every dish arrives on the table like a work of art and tells a story. From the carefully selected tableware to the sometimes truly delicate decoration, every meal must also be enjoyed as a feast for the eyes.
During our visit, we deliberately took our time to appreciate this before letting the small culinary wonders disappear into our mouths. There, a true explosion of flavor unfolds. Although various Asian, predominantly Japanese, influences are clearly played with here, this is never superficial or intrusive, and at the same time Philipp Dyczek and his team remain true to their culinary roots.
We enjoyed every moment of this evening. Artis is a culinary enrichment for the unfortunately far too predictable and often modest culinary scene in Graz.

google_reviews.author: Rodney Torres-Mabe
google_reviews.text: Best meal I've had in a long time. Was truly something special.

google_reviews.author: Roni Kollender
google_reviews.text:
Amazing experience! All the dishes were extremely interesting and with a very special mix of flavors.

The service was very good and felt professional and welcoming.

I celebrated my birthday there and recommended this place for a special occasion.

google_reviews.author: Ormonde - Industrial Cleaning Servcies
google_reviews.text: Only had time to try their dessert and wine, both were amazing (normally tasting menu only), can’t wait to come back for their tasting menu. Staff are amazing, special thanks to Sebastian for accommodating us and being super professional:)

google_reviews.author: David Annez de Taboada
google_reviews.text:
An incredible experience. We went for the surprise menu of 7 courses and it just blew me away. All the courses were exquisite and perfectly timed. We coupled it with a Viogner from Lazio and it was truly fantastic. Value for money lends itself towards the tasting menu as for 70 euros you get an incredible amount of course. Service was friendly and the head chef came to explain his signature dishes which was a nice touch.

One of the best places I've eaten in Graz and genuinely worth the money. Just make sure you go for the tasting menu.

**I'd like to highlight that a chunk of these reviews are not about the new chef or the actual restaurant so do take time to read through them**

google_reviews.author: Scott Strachan
google_reviews.text: Our first morning in Graz, and we happen across this wonderful breakfast location. Excellent food and such a friendly young woman serving both tourists and commuters alike. Well done!

google_reviews.author: Dragan Georgievski
google_reviews.text: Very pleasant staff and excellent food. A must try experience in Graz

google_reviews.author: Lucas Pereira
google_reviews.text: Good food, unfortunately really bad service and unfriendly staff.

google_reviews.author: Maja Gajić
google_reviews.text:
This place is in a great location. Patisseries are always fresh.
Fast service, perfect for picking up breakfast while waiting for the next connection.

google_reviews.author: khokha_72
google_reviews.text: Great coffee they serve 👌🏻

google_reviews.author: S 8
google_reviews.text: This is a place we like to stop for a drink when we’re in Graz, with a lovely view over a quiet square. Unfortunately, the terrace heaters weren’t working (the waiter said they were broken), and the prices aren’t exactly low.

google_reviews.author: A. De Silva
google_reviews.text:
Limited menu, decent for drinks and snacks. Waiter seems impatient, customer service might not be his calling.

Ended up having 1 drinking and left for a more pleasant atmosphere

google_reviews.author: Michael Wexler
google_reviews.text: We stopped for a beer and a snack, both of which were acceptable. The service was provided by an impatient waitress who didn't give us time to order, but threatened, order right away or I'll come back when I can. It's worth noting that there were only three of us, but the waitress didn't know her place. She mistakenly thought we were the servers and she was the customer and made the rules. Not recommended at all

google_reviews.author: Ilona
google_reviews.text: Good drinks, affordable prices, nice location. Very friendly staff.

google_reviews.author: Richard Jennings
google_reviews.text: Went 3 times but only managed to get served in reasonable amount of time first visit. You either do table service or you don’t. Read book for a while on two occasions and then gave up having not been served and left without buying anything.

google_reviews.author: Vladimir Marjanovic
google_reviews.text:
Great little spot for some savory food. Me and my wife went there today for a question CK lunch; at my insistence she got Saltimbocca Alla Romana and I got chicken in gorgonzola sauce with polenta. Both were fresh and tasty and well worth the price (20+19 EUR).
They have a great selection of authentic Italian meals and they know how to prepare it.
Staff is also top notch, with a bit of typical Italian gruffness; don't take it personally, that's the part of the experience. They're approachable and helpful and will make sure you enjoy your meal.
I highly recommend it for a lunch of brunch experience, especially since here in Graz the most food you will find is of more Arabic origin (kebab etc - also great, but a bit overrepresented) so having a bit of authentic Italian cuisine with such a great atmosphere is a pure delight.

google_reviews.author: Oleg V
google_reviews.text: True Italian cuisine! I love it, I know what I am talking about. Hence the comments that the food was bad simply mean the people who left them don't like the Italian cuisine. We had lasagna, aglio e olio and risotto with porchini con shrimps. All three were delicious and authentic!! And I also dismiss reviews of rude staff. They must have asked why their carbonara came without cream thus having driven the staff crazy - that's my best guess ))

google_reviews.author: Daria Yermolova
google_reviews.text: The fish soup was nice, the boloniese, however, was plain and had hardly any meat or sauce in it. The tea costs 2.30 per cup for a cup of hot water and a tea bag. The service wasn't horrible, but was definitely not good either.

google_reviews.author: Like Clubs
google_reviews.text: A nice little Italian based restorant with excellent pizza and food. Nice and heated seating area outside. Fast and courteous service and our server was English speaking. Excellent Italian Illy coffee, which is priced a bit steep but the food is priced OK. We actually visited again same day for some mulled wine and that was excellent as well.  Overall a very positive experience.  Would gladly visit again.

google_reviews.author: Nija Dalal-Small
google_reviews.text:
Sat down and ordered food... over an hour later, no food to be seen. We all the waiter as the kids are getting really tired and hungry. The waiter whines that is been stressful. No apologies, no care. Promises the food is coming. It doesn't arrive. 10 minutes later we ask again. He said it'll be 5 minutes. 5 minutes later we walk away. He calls us over - finally the food is here.

And it's nothing special. Too much salt, a truly boring pizzabrot, one of the worst "greek" salads I've ever seen in my life.

We ate reluctantly having been left in sheer hunger and got the eff out of there. Nightmare.

google_reviews.author: Patrick Chiodi
google_reviews.text:
Absolutely loved this restaurant. Flavours were amazing. The chicken breast in a Mushroom sauce was exceptional. This is how good food should taste with soft chicken, strong well balanced flavours and a hearty mixture of vegetables in the dish. The Mushroom sauce was the star and everything just had to be dipped into it!
My slow cooked beef ribs were not what I expected, but the dish was excellent.  1 big fatty ribs and two melting beef pieces all tender and juicy, with roasted sweet potatoes asparagus and a nice sauce on the side. You had ti squeeze the  asparagus centre out of the tough exterior shell to enjoy its soft and sweet core, yum! I dipped the sweet potatoes in the sauce which was s flavour i can't describe, but i loved it. The recommended drinks queue perfectly with the dishs.I wish I could go back

google_reviews.author: Mariam Hambardzumyan
google_reviews.text:
Visited in May 2024.
A cozy place loved both by locals and tourists. The interiors are nice, I absolutely loved the forged lamps with colored glass. Kind of sets the right atmosphere.
I ordered pumpkin soup (4.9€), tea (2.6€) and Kaiserschmarrn (10.9€). The prices were great! Even after the slight increase in prices in 2025, the prices stay one of the most affordable restaurants in the city. And taking into account the quality of the food, the value for money is really good.
Unfortunately, I forgot to ask to bring the tea with the dessert, so It was served first.
The pumpkin soup came pretty fast, and it was the creamiest, silkiest, tastiest pumpkin soup I ever had! The spices were complimenting the vegetables, the cream and pumpkin seed oil were a great addition, the croutons were crunchy.
When I finished the soup, the Kaiserschmarrn  was served, and it was HUGE. I would recommend ordering it when you're not alone, it's almost impossible to finish it all alone.
In Herzl they serve Kaiserschmarrn with plum compote, and it's a wonderful combination of sweet and sour flavors. The dough was airy, fluffy and tasty, the strawberries were delicious and added freshness to the dessert, I wish there were more!
This was the best meal I had during the three days spent in Graz, thank you, Die Herzl team!

google_reviews.author: Steven Dordick
google_reviews.text: Excellent restaurant, delicious food friendly service and a great location!  Cant wait to return!

google_reviews.author: Jasmin Hussain
google_reviews.text: Some friends took us there. I found it to be a great experience. I had the Salad which came with a pumpkin seed oil dressing. The dressing was very well prepared and I have never had that tasty pumpkin seed oil. My friend ordered the backhendel which I found just ok.

google_reviews.author: marblik
google_reviews.text: In Austria, it’s hard to find a place to eat late, but there are spots like this one where they wait for you all evening. The cheerful staff guides you through a maze of rooms to your table and is always ready to help you choose delicious dishes to feast on. And the food? It’s absolutely exquisite. That’s actually an understatement. The flavor and texture of the ingredients are stunning and exceptional, whether it’s a potato, bread dumpling, stew, or sauce. Traditional Styrian dishes at the highest level. The service is delightful, and the prices are low. I tip my hat and recommend it to everyone.

google_reviews.author: TheHipsterCher
google_reviews.text: We came here as it was one of my dream wedding banquet venue with this traditional Austrian interior. I love the environment and atmosphere. The old male waiter gave an amazing service so my fiancé tipped him in the end. Their Turkey schnitzel is so juicy and unique, love every bite of it; also the side salad with pumpkin seed oil. Their sobert desert is very refreshing, it is a must try.

google_reviews.author: Jyothish Atheendran
google_reviews.text: Good food. Good vibes. They accept card payments and the staff speaks English.

google_reviews.author: Martha Milch
google_reviews.text:
I recently spent a delightful evening at Gasthaus zur Alten Press, tucked into the bustling Griesgasse in Graz. As soon as I stepped in, the cozy, rustic charm—with its wooden panels, vaulted nooks, and historic stone flooring—made me feel instantly at home.

I began with a flavorful Rindsuppe (beef soup) complete with strudel and leberknödel—perfectly hearty and warming. For my main, I savored their signature Schopfbraten (pork roast)—tender and rich, served with lovingly prepared sides. Vegetarians aren’t left out: the Topfen-Hirse-Laibchen (quark-hillet patties) were a delightful balance of texture and flavor.

I rounded off the meal with a classic Milchrahmstrudel, light and creamy, truly a pleasant finish. The service stood out—naturally friendly, attentive, and just the right pace to make the evening feel relaxed and cared for.

Gasthaus zur Alten Press delivers honest, regional cuisine in a warm, timeless setting—great for a comforting and authentic taste of Graz. I’ll definitely be back to explore more of their hearty menu next time.

google_reviews.author: Spencer Zhao
google_reviews.text:
A lovely place to enjoy traditional Styrian style cuisines. Highly recommend the pork cheek, so tender and juicy.
The vibe is quite chill with tons of antiques and Austrian styled decorations.
Waiters can speak both German and English, the menu are also in both languages.

google_reviews.author: Bu Poly
google_reviews.text:
2023/11/17（FRI) 18:30 5 people
Warm place and service
Quite place
Soup is nice
Turkey, salad , pumpkin seed oil matching well
Main dish 13~20 euro

google_reviews.author: Pamela Mudd
google_reviews.text: Get off the tourist area and treat yourself to an amazing breakfast here. The owners are lovely, kind and generous people. The food is excellent and very large portions. Don’t miss this cafe for an authentic experience with excellent ambiance and food!

google_reviews.author: Jeannette Scheweder
google_reviews.text: Breakfast was generally okay; more toast would have been nice, and perhaps a bit more courtesy when paying would have been appreciated. While the work is commendable, that's not the guest's problem.

google_reviews.author: Anton Keck
google_reviews.text: A great place with super delicious food. They also serve rolls! A very nice, funny owner.

google_reviews.author: Anton Christian Hofer
google_reviews.text: A very nice café right across from the Austrian Health Insurance Fund (ÖGK). The pastries, the lovingly prepared dishes, and the ambiance are all top-notch. But what truly stands out is the owner's friendliness. She manages to make every guest feel like a regular from their very first visit; I think that's a skill that can't be learned, but rather a testament to her genuine warmth and naturalness. In short: I can wholeheartedly recommend Café Traude!

google_reviews.author: Salina Benâbbou
google_reviews.text:
Very friendly hosts. Always friendly and accommodating.
The coffee tastes very good, and the rolls are also super tasty.
I really enjoy being there and can highly recommend the coffee. :) 😀
It's great that you exist.

google_reviews.author: Matej Ferencek
google_reviews.text: Great place to stop for a good coffee or any other drink. Nice interior and very friendly staff.

google_reviews.author: Branko Kuzmanović
google_reviews.text: Top!

google_reviews.author: Tomislav Pobi
google_reviews.text: 😎 💪 💪 Like

google_reviews.author: Andreas Berger
google_reviews.text: Super friendly. Cozy. Everything was perfect for a few after-work drinks.

google_reviews.author: Sabine Novak
google_reviews.text:
The Café-Bar Lounge Kommerzial is simply phenomenal.

Enjoy a relaxing coffee in the outdoor seating area during the day – before, during, or after shopping – and then party at the bar in the evening – it's simply unbeatable.

Everyone is welcome, young and old – there's something for everyone.

And for those feeling peckish, there's a variety of snacks available.

Plus, there's free parking and barrier-free access.

The owner, Mr. Miljic Bojan, looks forward to your visit.

google_reviews.author: Paul
google_reviews.text:
Such and amazing Bakery. We had heard about this bakery before our trip to Graz, Austria. It is the oldest operating bakery in Graz Austria. It was apparently founded in 1569. The exterior façade of this bakery is simply breathtaking. And of course, the pastries inside are over the top amazing.
Our local friend told us that the original bakery was in a different location and was then moved to this location around 1880.
But this building itself was constructed in the 14th century and is still very much all original.
I would recommend you stop in if your in Graz Austria
Paul
Calgary, Canada

google_reviews.author: Surachai Pramualcharoenkit
google_reviews.text: Hofbäckerei Edegger-Tax is the oldest bakery in Graz, famous for its beautifully carved wooden façade and long-standing royal heritage. The atmosphere feels warm and traditional, making it a charming stop for visitors. The bakery offers a delicious selection of freshly baked pastries, breads, and sweets, along with very good coffee—perfect for a relaxing break or taking photos in front of its iconic storefront.

google_reviews.author: A G
google_reviews.text:
Edegger-Tax Hofbäckerei – A sweet piece of Austrian history in Graz (May 9, 2025)
Stepping into Edegger-Tax on Hauptplatz is like walking back to 1569 – the oldest bakery in Graz and one of the oldest in Austria. The ornate baroque façade with its golden eagle, arched entrance, and historic sign instantly transports you. Inside, the smell of fresh bread and pastries fills the air, and we couldn’t resist trying two classics: the warm, flaky Germknödel filled with plum jam and topped with butter and poppy seeds, and the perfectly dusted Topfengolatsche (quark-filled pastry) with its crisp edges and creamy filling. Both were heavenly, and eating them on the square while watching the city go by felt like a real treat. A must-visit for anyone who loves tradition, quality pastries, and a touch of imperial Austria. Highly recommended – worth every calorie! 🥐🏛️

google_reviews.author: ChunkyCherry
google_reviews.text:
Being from Copenhagen we’re blessed with some of the best bakeries in the world, and I will always compare bread and pastry quality with the level we have at home and unfortunately Edegger-Tax left me disappointed.

I bought a large loaf of bread, a vanilla croissant, a pastry with cream and fruits, a nusskipferl and two small semmels. The Semmel and loaf was great. Nice crumb and super good flavor in both. The pastries however were quite disappointing. I don’t know if that’s how they’re supposed to be traditionally here I Austria - dry and doughy - but I much prefer the light and airy version of French style puff pastries.

For the price and hype I think there are much better options in the city.

google_reviews.author: Nick Varricchio
google_reviews.text:
Always a thrill to seek out the "traditional" amongst the "progressive;" happy to know both can exist at this Styrian pâtisserie!
1st customer of the day and spent the subsequent 15 minutes deciding which pastry to bring home. The Linzertorte & Nusskipferl proved triumphant!
€5 for bliss!

restaurants.name: IKEA Restaurant
restaurants.summary: Chain store restaurant featuring Swedish-inspired food, including classic meatball & salmon dishes.

google_reviews.author: Mihaela Šalina
google_reviews.text: It was really bad,really expensive we paid 6€ for a salad that had cucumbers on like 5 slices and a litlle bit of beans,the tables are dirty,the women that works there wasnt really friendly but fine,and we paid 50€ for 4 meals without deserts or anything. Really bad

google_reviews.author: Samo Zavšek
google_reviews.text: The food is as expected from the standard ikea restaurant experience, but the staff is quite rude and impatient. Instead of being helpful they treat you with no respect and show no intention of caring for what they come across as. Even worse, a lady working there gladly helped some german speaking men and was rude to women customers speaking a foreign language.

google_reviews.author: Ekaterina A
google_reviews.text: This is one of that Ikea restaurants that you can’t just exit :( you need go through half of ikea to exit. Not really comfortable

google_reviews.author: Marko Kovačević
google_reviews.text: Friendly service but worst IKEA food in the EU.

google_reviews.author: Jakob Glieder
google_reviews.text: Nice ikea Resteraunt. Food is very affordable and not bad.

google_reviews.author: Daniel Serrano
google_reviews.text: The restaurant looks clean, but the service leaves a lot to be desired. The fish soup was good. The gnocchi in white sauce was excellent. However, the lobster risotto was bland — the only thing that tasted good were the truffles; everything else lacked flavor. The service leaves much to be desired, with a waitress who, when asked for tap water, only offered bottled water, even though other guests were given tap water. She also didn’t explain the menu properly.

google_reviews.author: Stephen Spencer
google_reviews.text:
This was one of the best meals we had anywhere in Europe and the lunchtime set meal made it an absolute bargain.
A fish soup, a main, a glass of wine and bread and it was all superb quality,
The restaurant is quite small but we never felt cramped and the setting is gorgeous.
Staff were superb, spoke great English, and had no problems dealing with a request for a gluten free meal, even with the lunchtime set special.
Had we stayed here longer we’d have been back for dinner as well, but for a special lunch, this can’t be beaten.

google_reviews.author: C
google_reviews.text:
food and service was amazing!
fish soup is definitely a must try. tuna steak was not bad, and the white solo asparagus was fantastic. they do use loads of parsley however.
i love my bellini too!

google_reviews.author: Lara Beker
google_reviews.text:
Nice place with great food and atmosphere.
We went on a holiday, and it was very sunny and hot, thankfully the restaurant is located in a narrow street with lots of shade and also sunshades.
We each got a branzino fillet, a side of asparagus and rosemary potatoes, and a spritz.
The total was around 80€, so a bit on the expensive side, but we enjoyed it very much.
The waiters were also very polite and helpful.

google_reviews.author: Gregory Fiderkiewicz
google_reviews.text: We seeked out the place since we were looking for a good, seafood restaurant. When we arrived, the staff were somewhat uninterested and robotic, and we felt like we were intruding, though the place was empty. The service were changing, as if 2 people were needed to serve one table, and it seemed they did not communicate. When we asked about the wine iptions, the gentleman seemed to be annoyed and impatient. Also, I had to open and pour the wine myself, since it was just brought to us and left on the side of the table. The oysters were great. But the rest of the food, like the mussels, seemed underseasoned and overcooked. The water that we ordered came in a dirty, dusty water bottle that had been opened before, and not at our table. It tasted like tap water, but we were charged 7 Euros for it. After I enquired about the practice of bringing an opened bottle of water to a table, the waiter got defensive and said that 'he can bring us tap water and bottled to taste and see'. We agreed, and he never returned. Overall we payed around 120€ for a very mediocre, non-inviting experience.

google_reviews.author: Korina Kyriakou
google_reviews.text:
I had the best scrambled eggs in Graz here! (The scrambled eggs with veggies and ham - but instead of ham I asked for cheese).
We came here for breakfast. The waitress was very friendly and even the other customers were friendly! The clientele is older, I think we were probably the youngest people there (in our mid-30s) but I liked the more classic and traditional feel of the place.

google_reviews.author: Shkelqim Hoti
google_reviews.text: Beautiful cafe in the heart of Graz. Cool art for sale on all of the walls. Lots of pictures of Arnold as he is a local, i hear. Had a nice conversation with one of the servers, Muhamet, a smart young man. Unmissable if you visit Graz.

google_reviews.author: Daryl Tay
google_reviews.text:
We were using our phones to translate the menu when a super friendly and helpful member of staff came by and told us about the daily lunch specials and introduced other items on the menu.

The food and coffee were great and the atmosphere is super comfortable and relaxing. Definitely recommended if you’re in Graz!

google_reviews.author: A. De Silva
google_reviews.text:
An excellent place to spend some time enjoying. Good coffee, friendly staff.

The cakes looked good although I didn't have one, kind of wish I did!

google_reviews.author: Stan
google_reviews.text: Beautiful cafe. They have a vegan breakfast option. Top coffee with oats milk. Elegant and peaceful.

google_reviews.author: Oliver Hutchison
google_reviews.text: Just what we were looking for on our quick trip through Austria: a nice easy pub-style schnitzel dinner with a beer in a place with lots of wood and what to me feels like a traditional ambiance. We weren't disappointed, and on a Saturday night at 8pm there was plenty of space for us and our dog, who was promptly given a bowl of water. It's just a short, pleasant walk past the Folk Life Museum on the edge of the old town.

google_reviews.author: Richard Healy
google_reviews.text: Good beer,shit food €14.50 for a €2 salad served with overcooked fried chicken. Even better they gave me two and one half. When I asked for the explanation my meal disappeared. Unfortunately I chose wrong.

google_reviews.author: Jessica Lopez
google_reviews.text:
Easy walk from the castle and the tourist sites. The service was excellent and the food was delicious. Definitely recommended the short walk to enjoy this place. Plenty of space inside the restaurant and a nice patio to enjoy the outdoor.
The menu is pretty extensive so there are options for every taste. Vegetarian options as well.

google_reviews.author: Ivan Hontić
google_reviews.text: Service is excellent and friendly, but too many guests for one waitresses. She was running from table to table running like crazy and despite her best attempts we still had to wait 10-15 minutes before we could order. I’ve had Wiener schnitzel with fries for lunch. I got ketchup instead of what traditionally I would expect Preislbeeren. Meal was fine but nothing special. Prices are way to expensive for the quality, amount and waiting time.

google_reviews.author: Conni Covington
google_reviews.text: I got the Käsespätzle and they were able to make it without Speck, which was nice.

google_reviews.author: Genc Demjaha
google_reviews.text:
Best place in Graz – Made Unforgettable by Nimi!

Café Bar Gotthardt is more than just a café – it’s a warm, welcoming experience in the heart of Graz. From the moment you walk in, you're greeted with the cozy charm of a traditional konditorei and the unmistakable aroma of freshly brewed coffee and homemade pastries.

What truly sets this place apart, though, is the exceptional service – especially from Nimi, one of the standout staff members. Fluent in Albanian, English, and German, Nimi is not only professional and friendly but also incredibly attentive. He takes the time to explain every item on the menu in detail, ensuring that each guest feels informed and welcomed. Whether you're curious about a specialty dessert or need help choosing the perfect coffee blend, Nimi goes above and beyond to make your visit special.

It's rare to find someone so genuinely passionate about hospitality. His multilingual skills are a huge plus, and his kindness and knowledge create a truly inclusive atmosphere. It’s clear that customer satisfaction is his top priority.

Whether you're stopping by for a quiet coffee, a delicious slice of cake, or a friendly conversation, Café Gotthardt – thanks to Nimi – guarantees a memorable and pleasant stay.

Highly recommended!

google_reviews.author: Olha Volynska
google_reviews.text: Amazing place and atmosphere! With this huge desert I returned in my childhood. The service was super too!

google_reviews.author: Predrag Jelovac
google_reviews.text:
Great place to start you day if you have to be in Murpark early.
Breakfast was delicious, service is OK, we got our coffee promptly, but food waiting might be a problem for some people.

google_reviews.author: Martin Nöst
google_reviews.text: The service was very friendly, and the ice cream was lovingly and appetizingly presented. Unfortunately, the ice cream itself contained a lot of ice crystals (like a finely crushed ice cube), which somewhat detracted from the flavor. We found the portion size perfectly fine. I found the whipped cream particularly delicious.

google_reviews.author: ines ines
google_reviews.text:
My boyfriend and I were craving a scoop of ice cream today, and as soon as we got to the counter, I immediately noticed that the employee seemed extremely unpleasant. She came across as if she had absolutely no desire for her job, and that's exactly what you sense as a customer. Personally, I even lost my appetite for the ice cream at that moment because I simply didn't feel welcome.

My boyfriend's ice cream was quite good, but in my opinion, the amount of ice cream was way too small. And to be honest: Even if the ice cream was excellent, the waitress's unfriendly and irritated demeanor ruined the entire visit. You felt like you were a nuisance, rather than receiving friendly service as a customer.

I think first impressions are extremely important, especially in an area like this, and here it was completely negative. For me, it's not just the product that counts, but also how you're treated. Today, the employee radiated zero enthusiasm, seemed annoyed, and thus took any joy out of the visit.

In the end, all I can say is that one star is almost too much, because while the ice cream was tasty, the overall experience was so disappointing that I definitely won't go back and can't recommend it to anyone.

google_reviews.author: Xavier Nascimento
google_reviews.text: Loved it go thereeeee

google_reviews.author: Manuel Strobl
google_reviews.text: Top!!

google_reviews.author: Althuser Theo
google_reviews.text: Good beer and cool decoration

google_reviews.author: Benji I.
google_reviews.text:
I visited Bar UI in Graz and drank two glasses of wine. The wine was well chilled and very pleasant. The wine selection was ideal for the size of the restaurant—manageable, but appropriate.
The service was very friendly and quick, and the drinks arrived without a long wait.
I sat outside, which was very comfortable, well-lit, but not too bright. The interior is modern, with some chic decor, and overall a real highlight. During my visit, there was live music, which made the atmosphere even more enjoyable.
Conclusion:
A very successful visit, great atmosphere, and pleasant drinks. I would come back anytime.

google_reviews.author: Silvia Hierzmann
google_reviews.text:
I actually think the bar is really great!

The music and the atmosphere are fantastic!

I also understand that you're not allowed to take glasses outside.

What annoyed me last time was that we were allowed to order a bottle, but weren't told that it had to be finished in fifteen minutes because that's when closing time starts.

To me, it felt like, "Here we are, now get lost."

And the conversation with the waiter... It turned out alright, there were a few idiots there, but we always have those. Who were the idiots... us?

But still, it's a great bar, but that just bothered me.

Silvia

google_reviews.author: Petya Georgieva Miller
google_reviews.text: My husband and I love this cozy restaurant, its friendly atmosphere, and welcoming staff that was trying above and beyond to accommodate to our food restrictions. Their dishes are perfection — we had the salmon with veggies, the chicken with veggies, and the minestrone soup and all three were very tasty in a homemade style and with quality ingredients.

google_reviews.author: Allesha Song
google_reviews.text:
I had the seafood pasta today, and I have to say—it was the best I’ve ever had! The flavors were perfectly balanced, the seafood was incredibly fresh, and the pasta was cooked to perfection. Every bite was a delight! If you’re a fan of seafood, this dish is an absolute must-try. Highly recommended! ⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐

P.S. I only have a photo of the empty plate because it was too good to wait for a picture before diving in! 😄

google_reviews.author: shirin bb
google_reviews.text:
Best Italian  restaurant I visit in Graz
The food was great, the service  was excellent  the price was good, we
We were four people at the restaurant and the total amount we paid was €100 including the tip.
We also ordered two pizzas, cheese ravioli, a very delicious mixed leaf salad, and grilled calamari.
Everything was perfect!

google_reviews.author: Espen Arge
google_reviews.text: I spent a few days in Graz with fellow students drawing cityscapes, and il centro ended up being our little "home" in the city. The location is beautiful and the waiters and the owner are simply the nicest people. I would almost consider them our friends now:) We were allowed to sit at the restaurant multiple days to work, and ate lunch here every day. If we needed anything, it was already done before we could ask, for example blankets and a cup of tea when the weather turned a bit cold (and they insisted on us not paying anything for it). All in all a great place, and I absolutely recommend a visit - grab a pizza and a beer outdoors if the weather's fine, or a cup of tea in the cosy restaurant! (P.S. If you visit, look for the sketch i drew of the bar, it hangs on the wall:)) This will be one of the first places I visit when i return to Graz!

google_reviews.author: Dia Gejdosova
google_reviews.text: Pizza was amazing! Really tasty Neapolitan pizza. The waiter super sweet.

google_reviews.author: Ian P.
google_reviews.text:
A charming Pub in the Heart of Graz. 2 Minutes from the Main Square, in a small quiet Alley, with some nice Tables on the Outside. A good Selection of Craft Beer and Beercocktails meets some classic Beer they got on Tap.

The Nachos they got are definitly one of the Best in Graz. Three Dips, Sour Cream, Salsa and Guacemole just lovely. Loaded with Cheese and if you want it spicy you can get some Jalapenos on it.

The staff at a pub are what makes a pub special. And at the Falcara, everyone is so welcoming and makes you feel like a wonderful guest. I'm certain I'll be coming back 😄

google_reviews.author: Samuel Traussnig
google_reviews.text:
Great staff and service.
Perfect pint of guinness 🍀
Next time i will try the food as well.

google_reviews.author: Darren G
google_reviews.text: What a great spot in Graz - Niall and the team bring a little bit of home to Graz. Had the opportunity to have chats to Niall and experience a quality pint of Guinness. Lovely atmosphere and on sone nights ya can't miss the aul table quiz for a few giggles. Fair play Niall and best wishes for the future.

google_reviews.author: Florian Klösch
google_reviews.text: Had some really good hours with friends and some nice tabbed Guinness'. The service was very great and I felt in love with their Pulled Pork Sandwich what was really great.

google_reviews.author: Jakob Emanuel Truls Rundfloen
google_reviews.text: Really enjoyed my time here. The pub has loads of space and comfy seating. The beer is spot on—great selection! The three owners are super friendly and make you feel right at home. Definitely worth a visit if you're nearby!

google_reviews.author: Tal Israeli
google_reviews.text: Great Italian restaurant with delicious food. During my stay in Graz I've already visited this place twice because the food was so good. It's important to make a reservation by phone, since the place is pretty full in the evening. We tried both the pasta and pizza and everything was amazing. I'm sure we'll be back again in the future.

google_reviews.author: Shaun & Rita Eastell
google_reviews.text: Great place to stop in the square. Food was delicious, fresh and very favour some. Coffee was well made .

google_reviews.author: Andrea Mazelliu
google_reviews.text: After ordering, the waiter refused to serve us drinks at 18:17. He was very rude!

google_reviews.author: Juliána Biela
google_reviews.text: Pasta was OK, tiramisu perfect. Our waiter was kind and friendly

google_reviews.author: Tamara R
google_reviews.text: Very tasty pizza & lasagne con carne. Waitress was very polite too. Restaurant has nice decor and it is underground.

google_reviews.author: Anne Lam
google_reviews.text:
After arriving at Graz, this was our first Styrian meal and it did not disappoint - but rather set the standard!  Reservations definitely recommended at this busy restaurant/bar. We were seated in a cozy wood panelled dining area with banquettes.  Service was brisk yet attentive throughout.

Ordered the deeply satisfying cream of asparagus soup (served hot and perfect to start on a cool October evening!).
Schnitzel was huge (standard size) - pounded  thin, flavorful and an amazingly thin crunchy crust that seemed to almost float on top of the meat. A serving of lambs lettuce, potato salad, crispy bacon and the sweet vinegary tang with the unmistakable nuttiness of pumpkin seed oil dressing was the perfect side for the schnitzel - no need to separately order a side of rice with peas or parsley potatoes - which we couldn't finish!

A wonderful first meal in Graz!

google_reviews.author: Metilorange
google_reviews.text:
I have never been so disappointed in my life. Mind you, I have been to many michelin star restaurants. This is pure trash. Let's start with the food:
-The soup was ok
- Steak tatare was absolutely DISGUSTING, stale, not fresh, tasted like rotting flesh
-Wisner schnitzel was dry, mid, and overall in top 5 worst schnitzel i tried, the meat was foo thin, while the breaded part was too thick. They also added potatoes, which is logical- but what isn't logical is that they CHARGED US EXTRA FOR THE POTATO, which wasn't stated on the menu.
Atmosphere:
-I have experienced better atmosphere on a funeral -> There is no music whatsoever, only silence and pain. And everyone knows the music sets the atmosphere.
Waiter:
-Was rude, constantly interrupting us and hurrying us, trying to take our plate away, but it was obvious we were struggling to finish the meal because of how disgusting it was
-HE DEMANDED A TIP, which he obviously didn't deserve for being rude.

This experience has frustrated us, steak tatare is a bio hazard so we are thinking about calling an inspection on them.
AVOID AVOID AVOID

google_reviews.author: Love Josefsson
google_reviews.text: Really nice place. Excellent food. The best schnitzel I have ever had!

google_reviews.author: Francis Constantinou
google_reviews.text:
We recently dined at Landhauskeller on two separate occasions—once for lunch and once while hosting a client—and both experiences were exceptional. From start to finish, the service was exemplary.

The food was outstanding, with each dish beautifully prepared and full of flavor. We sampled a variety of menu items, all of which were generously portioned and thoroughly enjoyable. The staff were consistently friendly and attentive, contributing to an overall seamless dining experience.

The venue itself is beautiful, and even during a particularly busy service, the atmosphere remained vibrant and welcoming. Landhauskeller combines delicious cuisine, outstanding service, and an excellent location, making it an ideal choice for both business engagements and casual dining. We highly recommend it.

google_reviews.author: Martha Milch
google_reviews.text:
As a well-traveled food enthusiast, stepping into Landhauskeller is like entering a living tapestry of Styrian tradition and modern grace. Set beneath high vaulted Renaissance arches, the dining room emanates a harmonious blend of warmth and elegance—a place where cozy booths, ambient lighting, and mellow laughter invite you to linger.

Though the complete menu remains discreet, I savored a flawlessly crisp Wiener Schnitzel, tender veal, and a refined steak, each dish showcasing the integrity of regional ingredients. Dessert—the comforting apple strudel and indulgent crème brûlée—offered a perfect, sweet punctuation to the meal. The bar, dubbed "Katze Katze," adds a playful, imaginative twist, making each visit feel both elegant and intimate.

Service felt heartfelt and polished; staff made me feel genuinely welcome, and every detail—from the dish presentation to the wine recommendation—demonstrated thoughtful care. Landhauskeller isn’t just a meal—it’s an invitation to experience Styrian hospitality at its finest, wrapped in historic charm and culinary sincerity. A truly memorable stop in Graz.

google_reviews.text: High quality food and cozy environment. Like in a film, while crossing the door, you are directly thrown in the past, in a place with a little bit of modernity but that still keeps its soul and reminds the old good times. A very friendly local where a group of young  guys is giving all power to satisfy every customer request, serving cold Italian based meals, combined with a really good prosecco from barrel. There is also the possibility of drinking a good wine, no matter your taste, both white and red. I was there with a group of friends and with my girlfriend, both cases were honestly perfect, the upstairs are really reserved and romantic. In the end is Nice place where to spend some time and eat something good.

google_reviews.author: Dr. Dr. Immanuel Fruhmann MSc
google_reviews.text:
The Eckhaus is one of the many student pubs in Graz and right on the opposite of the famous Graz University of Technology / Technical University of Graz. From the patio of the Eckhaus you are only some meters away of the prestigious building built more than 100 years ago. The whole area is full of nice buildings and the Eckhaus is right in the middle of it. From here you are in some minutes by foot in the UNESCO-listed old town, the city center of Graz.

The staff is very friendly here at the Eckhaus, the opening hours are excellent (open every day until 2 am), and the food is very delicious. The pizza I ordered you can assemble yourself, meaning you check the boxes of your toppings like in the Bausatzsystem many pubs in Graz are famous for. So you build your pizza via a list of toppings. For my standard sized pizza, I chose salami, feta, onions, and jalapeños. Another I had the Souvlaki, which is very delicious as well. The meal comes with zaziki, pita bread, lemon, tomato and cucumber. There three sizes, small (3 sticks), medium (5 sticks), and the large one with 8 sticks of pork.
For the drinks we a yummy elderberry juice and a Puntigamer Radler, a traditional beer from Graz mixed with lemonade.

From Graz with Love!
Dr. Dr. Immanuel Fruhmann

google_reviews.author: Mario Baumann
google_reviews.text: After the reopening, the Eckhaus will remain a very cozy pub. Due to the renovation, there are now enough bar stools. The bench that runs along the entire wall is also a win for both the look and the coziness. The staff is very friendly and fulfills the wishes of the guests very quickly. The large selection of drinks and snacks invite you to linger a little longer than planned. I will gladly come again. Clear recommendation.

google_reviews.text:
Total chaos, €10 for a ticket when the audience consists of 7 people, 4 of whom were DJs and 2 waiters, is criminal.
Organizer: "Yeah, come at 9 p.m., then you'll get tickets at the presale price. There won't be any more later."?!?!? It wasn't a hoot anyway. How many tickets did you have, please? 5 or what?
Shrill cricket? More like stinking fried fish. That was nothing like that.
Next time I'll share a bottle of Doppler with some homeless people in the park; I'll have more fun.

I went there the first day I was in Graz and ate a Salami and Brie Bagel and drank an Oreo Milkshake. That was all perfekt.

The second time I went there was a day bevore leaving Graz and it was just as delicous!

The waiter invited us back at night for a birthday toast to a friend of mine and there we met the owner - really friendly staff all day!

all in all -  would recomend and I am 100% visiting again.

google_reviews.text: We went to warm up in the cafe while visiting the Christmas market. They kindly prepared us delicious coffee with rum:) Nice atmosphere and polite staff.

google_reviews.text:
Really nice place to spend some time in Graz.
We wandered in after walking a while in the city, the ladies spoke perfect English, we fortunately had space to sit down inside.
They were really helpful, we had hot chocolates, and it was really nice.
The atmosphere is very friendly, I loved the decor and my girlfriend told me about the secrets book in the ladies bathroom which is both hilarious and a really cool idea.
If I lived closer I would come more often, but the place absolutely is worth a visit.

Thank you! :)

google_reviews.author: Tila Nguyen
google_reviews.text:
Had the small Wiener-style schnitzel (pork), which was a good portion, tasty, and reasonably priced. However, charging €3.00 just for a small serving of Preiselbeeren feels quite high.
The staff was nice and friendly, and the atmosphere was fine overall. Nothing bad, but nothing particularly special either. An okay place for a casual meal.

google_reviews.author: Micki Thorson
google_reviews.text: Very cute & cozy spot with authentic styrian food. Lots of locals come here. The waiter didn't speak much English but was so kind & checked up on us regularly to make sure we were enjoying everything. He had a very kind & friendly smile. The food portions were generous so we weren't able to finish everything we ordered. My partner & I both agreed if we were to do this spot again, we would get 1 salad to split between us both. The item I ordered wasn't super filling on it own, but I ordered a beer too which was good & helped fill me up. I ordered the Emmentaler Formaggio Impanato which was essentially a breaded mild flavored swiss cheese with tartar sauce. My partner got the Balagnese Spagetti & I tried a bit of his & it was delicious. This is a must have if you are wanting to try authentic Graz food during your stay.

google_reviews.author: Kyo Asano
google_reviews.text: My friend who lived in Graz recommended here to me. AMAZING! I'm literally speechless about how great the food is here. They can literally take all my money if they keep bringing the food to me ngl

google_reviews.author: Gerald Sommer
google_reviews.text: Authentic Styrian restaurant and wine bar, offering good food and drinks, made by a team working unchanged for 20 years already. Do not expect a fancy atmosphere but indeed lovely staff really enjoying their work with a smile.  Experienced the lunch menu for 10,9€ which was excellent!

google_reviews.author: Beathe Amlie
google_reviews.text: Great service, big portion. The salad had way too much dressing, and was very bitter/sour. Would be better without it, but maybe this is the way they like it in Austria. The garlic pasta had also way too much garlic.

google_reviews.author: Ronald Stilting
google_reviews.text: It's located at the famous Glockenspiel and has above average comfortable outdoor seating and similar contemporary interior design. I only had cappuccino but saw well presented food passing. I don't understand why their terrace was nearly empty compared to the next-door restaurant.  Service was fast and efficient though not overly outgoing. When I wanted to pay with card,  I was told that it was only possible from 30€ upwards. Eventually they did accept my lower card payment.  I'll come back for their highly praised breakfast

google_reviews.author: Nati G
google_reviews.text: Putting any minimum amount for debit card payment is already ridiculous in this day and age, but 30 euros minimum?? That's just absurd.

google_reviews.author: Silvia Đuranec
google_reviews.text: A modern bar with extremely professional staff. It was raining during our visit to Graz so we chose this bar as our base, it was really cozy and we would definitely come back. 😊

google_reviews.author: Madeleine K
google_reviews.text: Nice and quiet seating area outside with huge fashionable furniture. Reasonable prices for breakfast which they serve until afternoon. Yummy omelette!

google_reviews.author: Kristina Jelenic
google_reviews.text: The interior is nice,the music was great.I liked the coffee.You can smoke there.It's in the centre.

google_reviews.author: Ian P.
google_reviews.text:
I don't think there's any other pub in Graz where you can experience the soul of Ireland with such heart. From the priceless, cool, well-tapped Guinness, which is probably one of the best in the city. (It's totally worth the price, Michael.) To the wide selection of whiskeys, beers, and finger food. Which is the best thing you can share with friends, besides the laughter and memories you can make together here.
Not to mention the broadcasts of Gaelic football, hurling, and other sports. I haven't been able to watch this sport anywhere else.
And if you're not so sporty, the pub offers a selection of board games, chess of course, and on quieter evenings you might even find yourself playing a game with one of the waiters. The team at Dublin Roads is top-notch in any case; their warmth and hospitality make you feel like you're in good hands every time and you look forward to your next visit.  And if you ever find yourself in a pub on a Wednesday, look forward to live music that will almost make you forget you're in continental Europe and not Ireland. And for those of you who like to show off your knowledge, a word pub quiz.
And finally, the most important thing for me: nachos. For me personally, the nachos at Dublin Roads share first place as the best nachos in the city.

google_reviews.author: Lex Lynch
google_reviews.text: Went here a little while back, but, as an Irish person, I can say it was amazing! The folks working there are all so cool and wonderful, the food and service is great (especially the chips). will 100% go there again if I'm back!

google_reviews.author: Kevin Hmr
google_reviews.text: I had an incredible experience at The Dublin Road! This pub truly stands out for its fantastic atmosphere, attentive service, the best Guinness I've ever had. The authentic Irish vibe and welcoming staff make it a must-visit. Highly recommend! Cheers!

google_reviews.author: Hendrik Bower
google_reviews.text: Great food, great Guinness, great service. Fantastic pub quiz too

google_reviews.author: M Mck
google_reviews.text: Best spot in Graz! They support the local animal rescue so a great place to spend your &&&

google_reviews.author: Daniel Steinkellner
google_reviews.text:
Top spot for nice Italian wine & food in Graz.
Simone offers high quality wines from various Italian regions and his wine tastings evenings are a great experience and possibility to explore new Italian wines.

google_reviews.author: Raphaela Haring
google_reviews.text: Great wines from Italy, all available by the glass, with excellent advice. Uncomplicated, top quality.

google_reviews.author: Peter Ortner
google_reviews.text: There's a really wide selection of wines, but don't worry, Simone (the owner) can give you really good advice. 😊 Excellent wines, we're very satisfied and will definitely be back soon!

google_reviews.author: Maria Hobisch
google_reviews.text:
We went there once a few months ago just for a glass of wine and a plate of antipasti and were very satisfied!
Last week we went again to sample the various processcos! Great service and explanations, and the owner didn't even know we were choosing the sparkling wine for the wedding and would be ordering three cases.
Definitely give it a try!

google_reviews.author: Anna Z.
google_reviews.text: Top wines from various regions in Italy, excellent food, and Italian hospitality. A wine tasting is definitely something you should experience.

google_reviews.author: Albert
google_reviews.text: A trip down memory lane to my youth. It's great that it's still there and hopefully will stay that way. We go there every now and then, and it seems like there have been more people at the club recently. As far as I'm concerned, it could be bursting at the seams with people at any time.

google_reviews.author: Koko gonscho
google_reviews.text: This establishment is there for you when you need it. When everything else closes, the Q is open and you can have a good time.

google_reviews.author: Melanie Pippan
google_reviews.text: A must-go club!!! Lukas as the boss is one of a kind!

google_reviews.author: Dumb fairy
google_reviews.text: Very cool club

google_reviews.author: Kastenfisch 1804
google_reviews.text: Bright near

google_reviews.author: Frederico Fortuna Polónio
google_reviews.text:
Canteen-style buffet, more focused to the student crowd; might also be useful for other professionals nearby, but they cannot enjoy the discount given to students.

The food is really nice, taking to account the price. Buffet choices are based on 'meal of the day's so although they might be limited one an one-off basis, it changes throughout the week, which is perfect for the student crowd.

Canteen-style assumes that seating can be cramped and table rotation is quick during lunch hours, so loitering is very difficult.

google_reviews.author: Alex D
google_reviews.text:
I forgot to take a picture of the food because it was so delicious. After many years, I came back to Graz, where I found Mido and his lovely team with the same tasty and low-priced food. For a student cafeteria, it definitely deserves 5 stars!
PS: Thank you KHG & AAI making this place possible.

google_reviews.author: xenia georgiadou
google_reviews.text: By far the BEST food I have eaten in Graz. Super comfy environment, very friendly people. Highly recommend it! Also as far as I have seen veggie and vegan choices.

google_reviews.author: Simon Watmough

google_reviews.author: Mario Jelicic

google_reviews.author: Geoff Newitt

google_reviews.author: Emir Imamovic
google_reviews.text: Best place,don t look for other.Billy is magician ,all meals is so taste and done in minute

google_reviews.author: patricia loriato
google_reviews.text: Is nice ristorant... billy ...is good chef

google_reviews.author: Sirvan Aktan
google_reviews.text: Super  ☺☺

google_reviews.author: Kristian Holjević
google_reviews.text: Nice

google_reviews.author: koen visker
google_reviews.text: Ordered two pizza's, one of them Never  arrived. Even after  promising a Swift (re)delivery.

google_reviews.author: Mori Qasimi
google_reviews.text: I’ve got the Salad with Chicken fillet, was the best I have ever eaten 👌. Very Friendly staff. Absolutely suggest it.

google_reviews.author: Grizzly 1703
google_reviews.text:
I pre-ordered three dishes and wanted to pick them up. The place looks like a back entrance.

They made me a burger even though I ordered pizza.

While waiting for the pizza, I overheard other orders not being handled properly either.

Both of the other dishes tasted like they weren't safe to eat... We threw the food away.

google_reviews.author: Johannes der Jünger
google_reviews.text:
This place is absolutely awful. Whether you eat in or order delivery, it doesn't matter.

Disgusting – beyond words. The burgers were a culinary disaster; I still feel sick just thinking about that awful food... never again...

This place should be shut down immediately!

google_reviews.author: Günther Neuhold
google_reviews.text:
I wanted to eat a pizza on March 25th, and because I was short on time, I called 15-20 minutes in advance and asked them to prepare the pizza. When I arrived 22 minutes later, the lady asked me if my order had been placed over the phone and if I could wait a little longer. A drink and 10-15 minutes later, I asked again where my pizza was. The pizza chef then told the waitress he had made the wrong one and he'd start over (after asking his friends if they wanted the wrong pizza)!

I'd had enough!!! I specifically pre-ordered, but then nothing was ready, and they laughed at me for making the wrong order. Absolutely outrageous!!!
There was no apology or even an offer of an alternative, not to mention that the restaurant would cover the mineral water I consumed!


Poor service
Rude chef

google_reviews.author: Daniel Babo
google_reviews.text: Went here for the Karadjordjeva, one of my favorite dishes, and it certainly didn't disappoint. Very good food!

google_reviews.author: Bojan Kuruc
google_reviews.text: This is really great Balkan food place in Graz! Must try if you love this type of food. Everything we ordered was delicious. Prices are normal, free parking available outside. Service was quick and professional. Highly recommended!

google_reviews.author: Aleksandra Czechowska
google_reviews.text:
This is not only THE BEST restaurant but also with most honest and wonderful people!!!
I forgot my bag with documents during our Europe trip and they send it to me back❤️🥹 wonderful meal, GREAT service and helpful people THANK YOU SO MUCH, best regards from Poland!

google_reviews.author: Apartmani Milenkovic
google_reviews.text: Great place, great service and delicious food. One of my favorite place to eat. :)

google_reviews.author: Miroslav Vanek

google_reviews.author: Gevatter Schwerkraft
google_reviews.text: Best place in town to grab a quick snack, you will find top quality sausages, Hotdogs, "Schnitzelsemmel" and Burgers. Hannes, the chef, is a grumpy nevertheless lovable local original, if you want to take away your food you better tell him beforehand.

google_reviews.author: Suleic Vero
google_reviews.text: Bester Käsehotdog Ever  !!

google_reviews.author: Michael Chizzola
google_reviews.text: Best in the world, we love it!!

google_reviews.author: Miro Miškić
google_reviews.text: Hot Dog Best

google_reviews.author: Wolfgang Ott
google_reviews.text: Bester Käsehotdog ever.

google_reviews.author: Johannes der Jünger
google_reviews.text: The place has been closed since June 1st, 2025. What a shame – I often got completely wasted there and passed out drunk. The friendly owner always treated me to Bernerwurst.

google_reviews.author: Josef Villendorf
google_reviews.text:
During this current virus situation, you can always pick up lunch from Gasthaus Lang to take home. As always, delicious, especially beneficial for older people.

I would like to sincerely thank the Lang family for being there for us even during this time. Thanks, Max

google_reviews.author: Josef Flatischler
google_reviews.text:
The restaurant is cozy and clean. There are non-smoking and smoking areas. The owners are friendly and also dog-friendly. They have a dog of their own, Nico, who is very sweet and greets guests. I have a small white dog. We have a senior menu and a regular portion menu. Yesterday we had roast pork, rice, salad, and dessert, which was always good. And there's also a garden.
The restaurant is recommended for young and senior citizens.

google_reviews.author: Ingrid Kuss
google_reviews.text: The food is always very good, the service and the chef make you feel at home, I always enjoy going there

google_reviews.author: Simon Wimmer
google_reviews.text: Want to feel at home with delicious food? Come by! Fabulous service and fabulous food

google_reviews.author: Mun DushanbePushkina
google_reviews.text: I only had tea and coffee but people seem to enjoy the atmosphere  and food. Graz is definitely has to own quirk and vibe with cafes like this one. Certainly,  a place to visit. It has soft seats and high chairs with the place to put your bag- ha- ha! WC are super clean and also has ducks..

google_reviews.author: Syd
google_reviews.text: Amazing customer service, reasonable prices, very cute interior and calm street to sit outside on the terrace! I can highly recommend the homemade Cinnamon Roll which is served warm and with a nice icing on top :) This definitely has become one of my favourite cafes here in Graz!🫶🏼

google_reviews.author: Lea Opravz
google_reviews.text:
You can only get breakfast till 12.00 and not even minute past that time. All the wraps and sandwiches that they offer would be much better (in the cold part of the year) if they were heated.
The tables were dirty and full of half eaten food and plates. We had to clean it by ourselves.

google_reviews.author: Anka Nižetić
google_reviews.text:
I arrived this morning with two small children to grab some coffee and breakfast. I ordered two coffees and two cinnamon rolls, expecting something soft and fresh like the cinnamon rolls I usually get in my country or other places I visit as a tourist.

I saw one cinnamon roll on the counter with other cakes, so I assumed we would receive the order right away. No one informed me that I would have to wait 15 minutes for the roll, or that it wasn’t freshly baked but frozen. When it finally arrived, the kids were excited—it looked nice with frosting and some fruit on top. But the moment we took the first bite, we realized it was hard as a rock and very dry—impossible to cut with a fork.

To me, it felt like a three-day-old pastry that had just been reheated and topped with fresh frosting. I wanted to say something right away, but there was a long queue at the bar. I didn’t want my kids to eat it, but they were too hungry so they ate half of each. When I eventually spoke to the staff, they told me the roll wasn’t old—it had been baked, frozen, and possibly overheated. They offered another cake as compensation, but we didn’t have time and didn’t want anything else to eat after that so we left.
Since we paid 17 euros for two coffees and two cinnamon rolls, I felt I had to share this experience for future customers. And to the manager and staff: I’m not here to teach you how to handle these situations, but the least you could’ve done was cancel the order and offer a refund.

Also, let’s be honest: a reheated cinnamon roll can never compare to a freshly baked one. I wonder—have you ever actually tried the cinnamon roll you’re selling for 4.5 euros? If you want to serve it this way, maybe consider half-baking it and finishing it fresh for each order. But most importantly, customers should be informed that it’s not fresh and that it requires time to be reheated.

google_reviews.author: Mariette Sorgdrager
google_reviews.text: What a cute coffee shop! Great coffee, quirky gifts, and a loving atmosphere. They really pull the duck there through. Had a delicious oat milk latte. Unfortunately no vegan treats that day but the cashier told me that if was just that day. Usually there are some. There was some vegan porridge if you feel like that.

restaurants.name: Scherwirt
restaurants.summary: Basic rooms in a down-to-earth guesthouse offering free breakfast & an outdoor pool.

google_reviews.author: Ernst Woisetschläger
google_reviews.text: Quiet, pleasant atmosphere. Good breakfast.

google_reviews.author: Martin Marinschek
google_reviews.text: A well-maintained guesthouse with a small but delicious breakfast buffet, including soft-boiled or fried eggs upon request. The jams were also very good, some of them homemade. The bed was comfortable, but the toilet in the bathroom had a somewhat unpleasant smell (like chlorine?), which is why we deducted one star. Plenty of parking is available (there's a quiet main road between the guesthouse and the parking lot).

google_reviews.author: Armin Ertl
google_reviews.text: We received a very friendly welcome. The rooms are newly renovated and look very nice. The breakfast was plentiful and delicious. We felt very comfortable.

google_reviews.author: Andreas J.
google_reviews.text:
Easily accessible, the rooms and bathrooms were clean and well-maintained. Free Wi-Fi and a TV round out the amenities.

Breakfast was lovingly prepared and there was something for every taste. The hotel's own pool was available for use from 8:00 a.m. to 8:00 p.m.

We took advantage of this offer because we had to leave early.

google_reviews.author: Peter Gerhardinger
google_reviews.text:
I was fleeing a devastating storm on my motorcycle and found shelter here.

The landlady was incredibly accommodating and let me store my bike.

I even got to take a dip in the pool until the first rain showers arrived. Then it got very dark.

The room and breakfast were fantastic.

I highly recommend this place, not just for one night.

google_reviews.author: Tobiáš Svoboda
google_reviews.text: Worst pizza I’ve ever had. The ingredients were cheap and the service was terrible. I would never visit this place ever again!

google_reviews.author: Michael Stauder (Unfallgangster42)
google_reviews.text:
I've never had such a bad experience!

I ordered three pizzas that never arrived. Instead, two wrong pizzas arrived two hours late. The Wolt employee tried to sort it out with the owner, but the owner simply hung up. When you try to call the listed phone number, you only get voicemail. Never again! I hope they go bankrupt.

google_reviews.author: Andrei
google_reviews.text: We ordered food 2 large hamburgers with bacon where there was no bacon on it and there were hairs in it our fries tasted of old fat and the food is inedible which is self-explanatory when you look at it we ordered via Lieferando and called the restaurant they were unfriendly and would not listen to reason

google_reviews.author: Martin Oisterschek
google_reviews.text: I was contacted by the restaurant, but no driver is willing to deliver my pizza because I live on the 4th floor... After 10 years of loyal orders, I will no longer be ordering from this restaurant.

google_reviews.author: Christine Praschag
google_reviews.text: A few days ago, I paid €23.50 for an order I ordered online. Today, I ordered the same order and received the same bill. But because I chose to pay in cash, the driver thought he could suddenly charge me two euros more and handed me a bill with no discount on it. Okay, two euros isn't a lot of money. That's just how you rip off your customers, and most will pay because they want peace and quiet. Not me. I complained to Foodora and sent the food back. Every scam gets exposed eventually. Stay away from this delivery service.

google_reviews.author: Suu Miles
google_reviews.text: Not sure what happened but the quality improved a lot during last year. Meat isn't overcooked anymore but perfect. Salads are so much better.

google_reviews.author: hasan Safyurek
google_reviews.text: Great and real... Natural food and polite staff...

google_reviews.author: Matija Zajc
google_reviews.text:
Good and authentic food
Entrance is actually from Nikolaigasse

google_reviews.author: Reihaneh Manteghi
google_reviews.text: Big portion of food, very delicious

google_reviews.author: Dr. -Eng. Mohammed Imran Ahmed
google_reviews.text: Good food

google_reviews.author: Maria Florencia Baulos
google_reviews.text: This restaurant is simply fabulous: the service is friendly and attentive, and the food is delicious and genuinely fresh.

google_reviews.author: Opritescu Azghadi Elena
google_reviews.text: Nice cozy place, pizza was delicious and big

google_reviews.author: David Pfeffer
google_reviews.text: All very good. Valtellina appetizer with good cured meats and basilica appetizer perhaps to be enriched with flavor. Meat as delicious as eating butter, really very tasty .. the only drawback for which I do not give full marks is the bread basket. I think that given that bread is also included in the cover charge that a customer pays, we should take an extra eye. Bread served in a basket lined with a cloth that I wonder how it can be washed with dry bread thrown away so that I wouldn't even give to the hens. Too bad because it is a detail that can ruin a beautiful evening surrounded by good food and nice staff.

google_reviews.author: Mircea Darau
google_reviews.text: I have been the first day at this place, and had a delicious meal. Second day I came back and due to some holiday they were on the way to close. The guy that served us was so nice(best experience of our life), and offered us the meal that we wanted to have again! Very very nice and quick service! Next time I will be in this area, I will come back!

google_reviews.author: Lari Moessa
