google_reviews.author: edib nakicevic
google_reviews.text: all good.

google_reviews.author: Matthias Matthias
google_reviews.text: 100% top

google_reviews.author: michael kemeny
google_reviews.text: Nestled in the heart of Graz’s, Schmidhofer im Palais offers an unforgettable dining experience. The restaurant’s modern interior with vaulted ceilings and a charming courtyard terrace create the perfect atmosphere for both casual lunches and elegant dinners.

google_reviews.author: Korina Kyriakou
google_reviews.text:
I got taken here for my birthday (I’m vegetarian) and it was one of the best veggie dishes I even had in a higher end restaurant! I had the baked risotto with tofu and pumpkin.
We have been to quite a few higher end restaurants and unfortunately most chefs are not able to put together a well rounded, tasty as well as satisfying vegetarian main dish. So I was pleasantly surprised! I love that they included the tofu as a protein because a veggie protein source is usually lacking from other veggie restaurant meals.
The waiters were very friendly and they knew the menu and wine offerings inside out. We were also offered a starter on the house which I really enjoyed. Again, they had a yummy veggie option!
The only thing I can say that I didn’t like so much is the intense sharp light lamp directly over our table but we found a way of dimming it down which made the atmosphere a bit cozier.
Also the dessert wasn’t really good, it was quite dry, tiny and not chocolatey at all (the chocolate tart). But the rest of the experience was great.

google_reviews.author: Lina Fievet Merkscha
google_reviews.text: The food was very good quality, but not worth the price. We ordered the tuna tartare, unfortunately the ponzu sauce was not like the traditional one I had expected. The other dishes were very good, especially the grilled octopus, and the crème brûlée dessert was excellent.

google_reviews.author: Gulnar
google_reviews.text: Went there for a birthday dinner and the food did feel like a celebration. We took their suggested menu and liked all the dishes.

google_reviews.author: Maciej Kapron
google_reviews.text:
The best Schnitzel in the most cozy part of Graz! As an international guest, part of a larger group celebrating graduation of a family member I must say anyone visiting Graz and looking for a unique experience with Austrian food - this is the place to go.

Located in the centre of town, the restaurant staff makes you feel very welcome and helps you get the most out of the experience, a greating from the restaurant's owner and you are taken to one of many comfortably spaced out tables. On the walls you'll find modern paintings, the kitchen is open so you may take a peak and the place really makes its guests feel very special.

Now onto the food. The menu is well balanced and everybody should find something for themselves. We had the opportunity to try several starters out of which, I would personally recommend the Schöcklfreilanderei. This, coming from a person who may not always pick eggs "soft-style" but if I had a chance, I would have that one over and over. The truffle smell does not hit but rather pleasantly evaporates leaving a bespoke after taste. If this is not to someone's liking, the Rote Rüben Carpacio is another very special item, particularly due to the refreshing taste of the Granatapfel.

For Schnitzel, it's been my second time at Schmidhofer and whilst I would absolutely want to have anything else from their menu, it would be a guilt not going for that one in my next visit (hopefully soon). It tastes absolutely phenomenal. Coming from the land of "Kotlet Schabowy" which is a look-alike brother we have in the country of Vistula river, it's much lighter and more pleasant in combination with lemon and jam - that's an honest review of someone who's likely to have their passport taken away as a result. Going for the Schnitzel, it's best to go for the simplest  potatoes with parsley - it completes this simple but spectacular dish very well.

To round off your experience with a dessert, I tried two options, the Plum Sorbet and the Schmarrn. Both are worth considering and the plum is a refreshing option whilst you do get vanilla ice cream with the Schmarrn and that just makes it a little bit more complete.

Overall, I cannot wait for the next visit to Graz and whilst I already have my favourites, this is a place where you can go ahead and choose anythign out of the menu not to be disappointed. and by all means, if you are in Graz, next to the castle and the Kunsthaus, Schmidhofer is a place you have to book and visit to get a unique flavour of the city and of Austria.

google_reviews.author: Mario Baumann
google_reviews.text: Located near the center, upscale restaurant with Styrian and international cuisine. Good selection of dishes - seasonally matched. The dishes were very good in taste and visually appealing gratin. The staff was trained and distinguished according to expectations. Gladly again.

google_reviews.author: lane j
google_reviews.text:
I had a late lunch there today and I was very disappointed in the food.  The salad was swimming in dressing, so much so that I waited till the lettuce on my fork quit dripping before I ate it.   I love steak tartar, I order it all over town.   But, today's was the worst tasting tartar I have ever had in Graz, period.  Both dishes were left un-eaten.
I politely explained why I could not eat the tartar, told them they could remove the plate, and asked for the check.   There was an excuse given about the cherry tomatoes that garnished the tartar causing the odd taste.  Huh?  There was no apology and no compensation.  Today's lunch was a VERY expensive basket of bread.
I will not return.

google_reviews.author: Jiri Polak
google_reviews.text: Breakfest was not a pleasant experience. No table ready, nobody around for 5 minutes. Tables were not clean. Nobody came later to us if we need anything. Summary: full self service meal in not pleasant environment.

google_reviews.author: Dr. Dr. Immanuel Fruhmann MSc
google_reviews.text:
The Parkhotel is truly an elegant and posh hotel located close to the Stadtpark, which is Graz‘ Central Park, and right opposite the University of Music and Performing Arts, which was the town Palais of the Archduke Johann, the brother of the Austrian Emperor.

This hotel provides seminar rooms as well as great food. The price of the dishes served in the Parkhotel restaurant is residing in the upper price segment but you get high quality for your money.

The staff is friendly and willing to help and since implemented in a hotel they are briefed to and used to speak English to their international guests. So that is quite convenient. And at the main entry of the hotel you are welcomed by a Saint Florian statue. Not only therefore but also because of the family name of the hotel owner family is Florian, the hotel’s restaurant is called Restaurant Florian.

I had a beef filet in sauce with Serviettenknödel (sliced bread dumpling) together with fresh green salad with pumpkin seed oil on top of it. The filet comes with Preiselbeermarmelade (cranberry marmalade) which is typical for Austria and very popular as it gives a sweet taste to your meat.

With Love!
Dr. Dr. Immanuel Fruhmann

google_reviews.author: Martin
google_reviews.text: We stayed at the Parkhotel and took the opportunity to have dinner at the Restaurant Florian. The menu is small but excellent. Everything tasted great. Only the dessert menu was a bit disappointing, as it still seemed somewhat reminiscent of the Advent/Christmas season. The wine list is extensive, and the selected Pinot Gris from Southern Styria was very good. It's not exactly cheap, but still reasonable.

google_reviews.author: Minuette Mima Macon
google_reviews.text: We visited as the part of the birthday celebration. The food was delicious, service was great and kids really had great time.

google_reviews.text:
To call this mexican food is a disgrace to mexican food...
That about summs it up.
I understand why one would like it it tasted fine, but if you have ever eaten mexican food real mexican than this will be a pure and utter DISAPPOINTMENT.

I got quesadillas with curry sauce and barbecue sauce. It was covered in sweet chili sauce. (The kind one gets in asian/sushi resteraunts) They were also filled with all sorts of obviously european vegetables.

Service and atmosphere were decent enough, and there were no real complaints.

I like it.
I love it.

restaurants.name: Alfred's Imbiss
restaurants.summary: Cozy spot open with an open kitchen & dining counter serving German & creative American fare.

google_reviews.author: Tim Spyridon
google_reviews.text: Roast Rack of Lamb was outstanding!!! Best I have ever had. Generous portion too with 8 chops. Also French Onion soup and chocolate cake were both delicious. Wonderful choice for celebrating our 45th wedding anniversary!

google_reviews.author: Anne Friday
google_reviews.text: My boyfriend and I came here and had a great time! Our dinner was delicious, and our server was excellent. My boyfriend ordered roasted duck - delicious! My halibut was cooked to perfection! Really romantic setting. Very European. Not easy to find good German food! Compliments to the chef! I love a light dessert, and my flourless chocolate mousse was so yummy - especially the crust.

google_reviews.author: Kevin Rieck
google_reviews.text: My wife and I had an excellent meal tonight at Alfred's! The German plate with schnitzel, bratwurst, sauerbraten, sauerkraut, and mashed potatoes was fantastic! The French Onion soup is one of the best I've had!

google_reviews.author: Michael Dyrenfurth
google_reviews.text:
The Chef's Table, actually a counter, at Alfred's was the ideal spot to appreciate the skill and concentration that goes into the making of a fine meal. Chef Heath is clearly a master at the top of his game and he and his sous-chef orchestrated superb meals for a small but full restaurant. Pity it was too cool and windy to enjoy one of their fine outside tables. We were there for the penultimate day of the Hilton Head Island Restaurant Week and we were glad that they managed to squeeze us in. But what about the food. Well, being raised by a fine German mother I think I know something about genuine German food and their Restaurant Week's special menu's Sauerbraten and rot kohl (red cabbage) with spaetzle  were right on the mark! Outstanding-well starred but tender, pullable-apart beef with sauce so delicious that just I had to ask for a second portion -- not that the first portion was chintzy but I guess I am just a sauce kind of guy. But I would do the menu a disservice if I did not also mention that the rot kohl was probably even better and more tasty than my mother's. Just the right balance of tang and sweetness on perfectly soft finely sliced and very tender red cabbage. Then the spaetzle were just the right side to complement the tasty sauerbraten as well's to sop up the extra sauce.  My wife's meal of a trout filet, flambéed right before our eyes, over mashed potatoes and grilled zucchini leaves with beurre blanc was also excellent she reported.

Out of sequence in my description was our observing the kitchen's evening startup which involved baking fresh dinner rolls and preparing a stack of potato pancakes - sure to be gone well before the evening ended. The Alfred's wine list offered us many well selected choices and we certainly appreciated the Paso Robles and Napa wines we picked. Then, however, I just had to have one of those tempting potato pancakes as my appetizer while my wife greatly enthused that her herb and mushroom soup first was the best she ever had. The postscript to our delightful meal was apple strudel for me and ice cream for my gluten intolerant wife. FYI the Chef and menu were fully prepared for this contingency.

We left the friendly service and outstanding food shared by this European enclave well satisfied.

google_reviews.author: Laurie Fettig
google_reviews.text: We stopped while on vacation to have dinner at Alfred’s. Hands down the best meal we have had our entire trip. Food was fabulous! Sat at the chefs table and not only got a wonderful meal but a great show. It was neat to see the choreography of the chef working and all the little details that go into making our meals. This place is a go to for both visitors and locals alike. I heard a local mention how he loves bringing guests to Alfred’s because the food is always amazing. Staff was pleasant and attentive to our needs. Will certainly be back.

restaurants.google_address: 807 William Hilton Pkwy Suite 1200, Hilton Head Island, SC 29928, USA

restaurants.name: Dunkin'
restaurants.summary: Long-running chain serving signature breakfast items & a variety of coffee drinks.

google_reviews.author: Dan J
google_reviews.text:
This Dunkin’ location has a very good spot, but unfortunately the overall experience keeps getting worse over the years. The selection of donuts seems to be smaller every year, and the biggest issue is definitely the service. The staff has never been particularly welcoming, but recently it feels even worse – as if serving customers was a burden. They rarely answer questions, are unfriendly, and often give the impression they’re doing everything reluctantly.

Another recurring problem is closing time. Even when you arrive 30 minutes before closing, you might be told that they can’t make certain drinks because the equipment has already been cleaned. Once, 15 minutes before closing, I was even refused donuts because the cashier said the daily report had already been done.

The offer itself is also inconsistent. A couple of years ago, they had a passion fruit frappe that was amazing – this year it doesn’t taste the same, and what’s worse, it’s rarely available at all. Out of five visits this year, I managed to get it only once. Despite being on the menu, most of the time the staff say they don’t have the ingredients, don’t want to make it, or claim the machine isn’t working.

Overall, this location could be so much better, but the poor service and unreliable menu make it disappointing.

google_reviews.author: Teodora Dicheva
google_reviews.text: I went there on a Saturday for breakfast and everything was fresh! Very polite worker. The tables outside are cool, keep in mind that you dont have much place on the inside. Tasty!

google_reviews.author: Jasper Tonato
google_reviews.text: We went here to pick-up our Too Good to Go reservation but unfortunately, during pick-up time, they cancelled the order since they don’t have assorted donuts that could cater the 6-donut selection. So I told the lady (she seemed to be the supervisor) that we travelled from Leoben to Graz just to pick it up. Upon hearing this, she offered if we want to take the remaining donuts which they could give 2pcs of each variant. We said yes it’s fine since we travelled just to avail it. She opened 2 more slots for us in the Too Good to Go app but unfortunately, it was reserved by other people just seconds after it was opened. She then said “you know what, forget it, since you travelled far away, I will give the donuts for free”. And I responded “are you sure, you will lost income”. She replied “it’s fine, we will take care of it. We don’t want to disappoint you since you travelled far”. That was really a nice gesture. We took the donuts, thanked them a lot and went on our way. Good job! 😎

google_reviews.author: Thanos Christodoulopoulos
google_reviews.text:
If I could I would give them 100 stars
The glase donut is the best!!!!!!

google_reviews.author: davide venturini
google_reviews.text: Good traditional cuisine at fair prices in a vintage atmosphere. Nostalgia guaranteed!

google_reviews.author: Patrick Skerbisch
google_reviews.text:
I know you don't really need this, but you'll still get a review from me!

The atmosphere is just like you'd imagine a beautiful old inn to be; you immediately feel comfortable and well looked after.

And of course, the warm and friendly service paired with excellent inn-style food—it couldn't be better!

A special highlight for me were the cheese spaetzle, which were simply delicious. Their own blend of cheeses makes it possible: unrivaled in taste and hearty at the same time, but not as heavy or fatty as they often are because of the cheese. Here, the opposite was the case: lots of cheese, yet not too heavy. Brilliant!

We'll definitely be back!

google_reviews.author: Elfa Lejniece
google_reviews.text: A very cute, traditional Japanese restaurant. Me and my family had a lovely time, the sashimi was fresh and good plus the desserts were amazing. My favourite deserts were mango and mochi balls. (The panna cotta was good too, but not my favourite) The prices were surprisingly cheap and for 4 people we ended up spending 75€😆, we took a lot of food and we were ready for 100, but nope

google_reviews.author: Raluca M
google_reviews.text: I would visit a lot till in February., when we were served some rice square dessert that was spoiled, it tasted sour and smelled awful, had to spit it out immediately after tasting. I know how it was supposed to taste as i was served the same dessert two days prior…Quite sad as this was my favorite place to eat.

google_reviews.author: ChunkyCherry
google_reviews.text:
Pretty decent place.

The interior is pretty standard Japanese inspired decor. But it’s cozy and really welcoming. We came here for lunch in a party of four and we were taken care of well by the staff. Granted, the restaurant was pretty empty but we were still welcomed nicely.

I had the crispy duck bento box, which came with a bunch of different sides and also started out with a miso soup. All of that for 14,70 euros which is an incredible price for what you get to be honest.

The miso soup was rather bland, and needed some soy sauce to lighten it up a notch. It had tofu and vegetables in it which was a nice addition.

For the duck, I wished the crust was more crispy. Some parts were nice and crisp, and others were a bit on the softer side. Besides that it came with picked vegetables, kind of like kimchi and that was absolutely wonderful. The Jasmin rice was average in my opinion, I didn’t feel like the quality of those were top tier. The grains were a bit short and I would’ve loved a bit more “stickiness”. The sushi was great, very nice side to the meal. Finally the cream dessert was also a nice finisher to everything.

All in all a pretty decent meal. For the price I would say it was perfect.

google_reviews.author: Suki K
google_reviews.text: Really lovely little restaurant with delicious food. Everything was fresh and tasty! The service was excellent

google_reviews.author: sentiss
google_reviews.text: Best asian food ive ever had and the staff is also extremely friendly

google_reviews.author: Hugo Sousa
google_reviews.text: Nice place for 5star Italian food! Nice employees and great place to have a dinner with a few friends!!!

google_reviews.author: Sofia Dikert
google_reviews.text: Very kind employees, super good food. Good vibe.

google_reviews.author: Mostafa Gad
google_reviews.text: It's realy a great place that serves delicious food.😋

google_reviews.author: nour mohamed
google_reviews.text: It's a very beautiful place I have ever gone

google_reviews.author: Pihla M
google_reviews.text: Super good food and kind workers.

google_reviews.author: Jovan
google_reviews.text: Cafe Konig, in the very center of the city, represents everything you expect from a cafe: a warm, traditional atmosphere, friendly and helpful staff who are fast—which, take my word for it, is difficult since the cafe is always full. The menu is rich with a variety of drinks, and in addition to cakes, of course, there are also salty snacks. All in all, a warm recommendation for Cafe Konig.

google_reviews.author: Patryk Stulik
google_reviews.text: Fantastic cozy cafe with a bit of old fashioned atmosphere. The apple cake was delicious. I liked their gluhwein which was a bit of a tea.

google_reviews.author: Mert Inanc
google_reviews.text:
We were looking for a rustic, typical Austrian café and came across this place. The service was very friendly and kind, and the cappuccino was delicious. We wished they had more options on the menu, but it’s definitely a chill spot to visit in the mornings to enjoy the service and coffee.

One thing that bothered us was the outdoor seating in the backyard—almost everyone was smoking, which made it less enjoyable for us to sit outside while trying to enjoy our breakfast. However, it seems like that’s a common thing here. If you’re looking to enjoy some coffee in a place with a bit of history, this café is worth a visit.

google_reviews.author: Dragutin Cividini
google_reviews.text: Nice old fashion local cafe. Coffe is not bad.

google_reviews.author: Aldrin Aquisap (Aldrin Travels)
google_reviews.text: The cafe feels like an extension of Graz’s medieval heart. May not be tor those looking for modern and bright vibe. The coffee was good though.

google_reviews.author: Pat Janzer
google_reviews.text:
Years of Happy Vegan Customers 🖤

We’ve been super happy vegan customers for years! We usually eat Pad Thai, Massaman curry, the chicken–cashew curry, yellow and green curry in vegan versions. Of course, we’ve tried almost everything else as well!
It tastes amazing every single time. 🖤

google_reviews.author: Rike Bergmann
google_reviews.text: We love this place! Super delicious food, especially the curries😉. The staff is extremely kind and welcoming and it's pretty affordable as well. Overall super great place we love coming here.

google_reviews.author: Amela S
google_reviews.text: It's a really nice place with good food. Portions are big, I couldn't even finish mine. Also the prices are very good and the place is not full during the dinner time. The food is delicious and they have a beer from Thailand, Singha.

google_reviews.author: Dora
google_reviews.text:
Very nice e Thai food restaurant! I ordered H8  (Gaeng Phed) Red Thai curry with chicken breast and white mushrooms (Agaricus bisporus). Full of coconut flavor, I am a huge fan of coconut! I love it so much! They shape the rice to a ❤️! So cute! Very nice and Jasmine rice, flora aroma long rice. Staffs were very helpful.

Be careful about the mosquitoes when you dine outside lol

google_reviews.author: Czer Berus
google_reviews.text: very nice restaurant near the jakominipllatz.  Inexpensive with a relatively large selection and slow (not lazy) operation.  The PaTaī was sharp, the mussels too.  ... can stay that way ... or improve

restaurants.name: Zur Steirerstub'n
restaurants.summary: Relaxed inn offering Wi-Fi access & breakfast, plus a restaurant with a terrace.

google_reviews.author: Tanja Gvozdenovic
google_reviews.text: The smallest schnitzel I've ever seen on a plate. The place is very nice and the service is fast and friendly. The food and the owners' insistence that a 5 cm schnitzel is perfectly suitable for the daily menu are ridiculous.

google_reviews.author: teresa tossut
google_reviews.text:
THE RESTAURANT

This place is absolutely NOT WORTH IT!
The food itself is good, but the waiters are extremely rude and impolite.
Don’t go there, for your own good!
I specifically asked for gluten free food (I’m celiac) and they brought me a regular one. I asked them to redo it and the new one had a peace of plastic inside.
At the end when we asked for the bill, they gave us the wrong one, and when we pointed out all these issues, they didn’t even bother to apologize.
On top of that when we finally left, they didn’t even say goodbye, as if all the problems were our fault.
Extremely rude and unprofessional

google_reviews.author: berke ayrancioglu
google_reviews.text: Nice place for local food. They also have lunch menu. Prices were reasonable and employees friendly.

google_reviews.author: Glenn Standish
google_reviews.text: A mixed bag. The young female waitress was very friendly. However, the older waitress got the drinks order wrong and brought the wrong beer. The soups and cordon bleu were great but the corquettes with the medallions were still frozen in the middle whixh is unacceptable.

google_reviews.author: Mukesh Yadav
google_reviews.text: Rooms are super small, and there no car parking. It is not a value for money and absolute caotic.

google_reviews.author: Jovan
google_reviews.text: Located in the very heart of Graz, the Altsteirische Schmankerlstub'n restaurant represents the best you can expect from a restaurant: a beautiful, traditional, warm atmosphere combined with very helpful and friendly staff who are always ready to give recommendations if there is any doubt with an otherwise excellent offer of very tasty food. The menu contains traditional cuisine that is quite adequate to the environment. All in all, a really warm recommendation.

google_reviews.author: Jessica G
google_reviews.text:
The restaurant has seating that stretches through the whole alley, so plenty of seating. It had a wonderful menu, an amazing selection. We got our drinks and mains super quickly, and it was so delicious. I was soooo full, but couldn’t not eat my last bite.

The dessert took a long time to get delivered. But it was good.

google_reviews.author: Victor Iliescu
google_reviews.text:
While researching places to eat in Graz, this restaurant caught my attention. And I said "why not?" This was one of the best decisions and a highlight of our trip to Graz.

The place is rustic, filled to the brim with that Austrian autentic charm. What's even best is that you get the full experience, as many locals are coming here to eat. The menu has a lot to offer: I do recommend the wiener schnitzel as it was very tasty and in plenty of quantity. We also tried some soups and we're also divine. Big props to everyone there.

I also have to give a massive shout-out to our waiter, as there was only one guy serving all the tables. A few times, a lady from the kitchen was helping him, but the service overall was very quick. He was striving to get every order quick and also making sure the guests are having a good time. And we had the best time.

google_reviews.author: Kelli Johnson
google_reviews.text: We enjoyed this tucked away treasure! Was close to our hotel and other activities that we enjoyed throughout the day.  We had some refreshing drinks and a quiet moment. A lot of visitors doing the same!

google_reviews.author: Joanne Tan
google_reviews.text: We have yet to try one of Austria's dessert Kaiserschmarrn and when we asked for this, the waitress was very kind to take our order which is not in the menu. We quite like it. Tasted like some buttery pancake that pairs well with whipped cream and berries jam. She also recommended the Spätzle which we find it pretty tasty coz of the burnt cheese that makes it awesome 😋 Nice roast pork and Schnitzel! Friendly staff ✅️

google_reviews.author: David Peter Fernandes
google_reviews.text:
Stopped over for a quick breakfast before our trip around graz, lovely place with a nice sit out, the host was nice and guided us on what to order. Tried a kasekreiner which is cheese suasage served with bread and classic sandwich along with a piece of dessert similar to cheesecake not sure what the name was.
Prices were ok considering the location

google_reviews.author: Aditya Rana
google_reviews.text: Amazing Café to come for lunch. Their dishes and service are both amazing. Would highly recommend.

google_reviews.author: Anita Varga
google_reviews.text:
The Café itself is really nice. The interior is quite modern and it's the perfect place for students, since it's just next to the university.
The selection of food is really good, with some excellent options for brunch, such as their toast sandwiches, or plates!

The only con I have noticed so far is that if you go there on Saturdays, there is usually no toilet paper in the lavatory. Also, there was a middle aged waiter serving us once, who was quite rude and impatient, and smelled of alcohol.

But other than that, I'd recommend the place for a place to study and eat!

google_reviews.author: Markus Sorger
google_reviews.text: Nicely located bar, directly around the university. You can get here really nice coffee and some delicious tea's. Usually it's never crowded. Only minus point, the waiters aren't really zealous. You have to wait really long or sometimes even go to the bar and order on yourself. They even have billiard pool dishes, secret hint, because you will always get here a table. All in all it's nice to have here a short break.

google_reviews.author: Pedro Silva
google_reviews.text:
Amazing place for remote work, either inside or outside. Quiet and calm.
Super service! I would suggest to anyone who would stop at Graz

google_reviews.author: Oldřich Olaf Poděbradský
google_reviews.text: Continuum is another of the Bausatz group restaurants. I'm not a big fan of this "make your own" meals, but if it works for someone, it works. Atmosphere here is cosy, more pub-like than a restaurant, service was ok, mostly young waiters. The problem comes when the food arrives. We had a burgers and boy - bun prefabricated and dry, meat patty prefabricated and dry, fries prefabricated, all of the food way to salty. Overall not matching restaurant quality for that price at all. But hey, if you're drunk at midnight and this is the only place you can eat it's sufficiant, Sunday afternoon sober was not a good time. The star of the meal was the draught Weißbier.

google_reviews.author: Samara
google_reviews.text: Nice place and service. Good food and music. It is a really pratical bar where you can compose your burger/pizza/scrambled eggs and even your cocktails. The bar is on of few places that open on Sundays. Not fan of the fact that they smoke on the inside thought.

google_reviews.author: Susan Wachowski Richer
google_reviews.text: Good meal, really good value  but impossible to find the server for 20 minutes before she came with the bill saying she was closing outside. We fancied another drink and wouldn't  have minded going inside but after that wait to order or pay, we decided to move on.

google_reviews.author: isabella “izy” schultz-wild
google_reviews.text: got a calzone (new in this kind of restaurant) with egg and it wasn't cooked and the waiter offered a new one. very nice. where i sat was very dark and that wasn't comfortable. the metal music was loud too, you had to yell to talk to the table.   the concept of the restaurant is very cool: you build your own pizza/burger/toast/calzone/salad/desert.... they have a lot of choices and i love that. one with better view would be "area 5" at jakominiplatz.

google_reviews.author: Miloš Djurić
google_reviews.text: Solid bar, you create your own cocktail. It is very cold inside.

google_reviews.author: Jyothish Atheendran
google_reviews.text:
Good food. Good service. A lot of options. Reasonably priced.

I tried the Cheeseburger 🍔 with bacon 🥓 and eggs 🥚 and it was nice.

One downside is, they don't accept payments with card 💳❌.

google_reviews.author: Fabian Jöbstl
google_reviews.text: This definetly one of the best places to go when you want some awesome food right away! Always open, great central location, super fast and friendly service and the menu is huge. You can get so many delicious meals, check out my pic of the menu! Cheers!

google_reviews.author: Lucy Constable
google_reviews.text: Photo doesn't do it justice but such a good burger!! Cheeseburger mit speck was incredible. Was looking for somewhere quick after arriving late. Lovely and friendly and quick even though there was a queue. Hot dogs looked amazing too!

google_reviews.author: Nikola Đurđević
google_reviews.text: Absolutely the best burgers I ever ate. Stuff are also really friendly. A very good and high quality food kiosk.

google_reviews.author: Yannis Koutiolas
google_reviews.text: Street food at its finest. The cheeseburger with speck and egg is their most famous sandwich and truly lives up to its reputation. The owner was very nice and playful with the customers. It's certainly worth to stop by.

google_reviews.author: Budmil Budapest
google_reviews.text:
Disclaimer :

I did not have a chance to buy anything at Chocolaterie De Naeyer and as I leave Austria in 36 hours I will probably never will.

Why I give 4 stars is because I am sure they sell exquisite Belgian 🇧🇪 truffles as I got a recommendation from a local but their opening hours are the most bizzare for a chocolate and artisanal beer shop I ever saw in my life (I travel extensively and visited all continents).

Firstly, who on earth buys chocolates or drinks beers at 07:30 am (their opening time on the 5 days in every week they are actually open).

Secondly, why close at 14:00 on Saturdays keeping in mind that Saturday afternoons/early evenings are when demand for beers and chocolates are at their highest?

google_reviews.author: Ana Montero Acosta
google_reviews.text: Best chocolate ever and very nice stuff. If you wanna enjoy a nice piece of chocolate, that is the place for you :)

google_reviews.author: Patrick Walravens
google_reviews.text: Looking for Belgian Pralines or even a Belgian trappist beer, Chocolaterie Denaeyer in Graz is the place to be👍

google_reviews.author: Lukas Pratter
google_reviews.text: Best chocolate in Graz

google_reviews.author: C Kai
google_reviews.text: My favorite shop for years when it comes to delicious chocolate!! Super friendly staff!! I can highly recommend it to anyone who has a sweet tooth now and then.

google_reviews.author: Cheryl Johnson
google_reviews.text: The Burger was recommended and did not disappoint! Plus, they served us water for the table gratis! Very friendly staff.

google_reviews.author: Werner Rösemann
google_reviews.text: Have gone here multiple times with 2-4 people. They consistently provide really good food and service. Great tasting burgers! Definitely amongst the best we've had in Graz.

google_reviews.author: Stan
google_reviews.text: They have a vegan menu! I had the vegan green gold burger with fries and habanero dip.  The burger is made of oats, pumpkin's seeds and vegetables. Delicious! A generous portion of fries. Habanero dip is very hot and tasty! Service is excellent, staff is very friendly. The place has a pleasing ambience. Loved the booth seating. A wide variety of alcoholic and non alcoholic drinks. Prices are fair.

google_reviews.author: Lisbora Likaj
google_reviews.text: The burgers and fries were great! They also have good vegan options. Prices were high and the downside is that you need to pay extra for any other thing, fries for 4+€, and dips for 1.5+€ (even for ketchup!!!) which is non sense to me. If you also get one of their homemade drinks (nothing special), expect to pay smth between 20-30€. If you want a cheaper option but a still a good one, go to Burgerista close by.:))

google_reviews.author: Dinko Pasini
google_reviews.text: I'm currently here with my ex wife. We're having a lovely time and the food is excellent (bacon story burger & bacon cheese fries). The terrace could be better, but hey - our marriage should've been also. Nevertheless, the excellent food made me reconsider getting back with her :)

google_reviews.author: Quentin Brault
google_reviews.text: Delicious food and so cheap for the amount you get! I haven't tried the hotpot yet (you need to reserve a day ahead for that) but I'll have to come back to try all the items on the menu. Everything available with fish, meat or vegan.

google_reviews.author: Daniel Pellicer
google_reviews.text: We went there today for the first time. Food was amazing. We ordered the banh mi with tofu, the vietnamese crepe and the noodle salat. For me the best was the Banh mi (vietnamese baguette) and i would recommend everyone to try that. The waiters were very welcoming and we are happy to go again there in the future

google_reviews.author: Iryna Vysotska
google_reviews.text: I liked this place and all food that we tried. Their soups are very tasty and sizes are good. Waiters are also super friendly. Menu is not that big but they have meals for any taste

google_reviews.author: Denisa Štefanigová
google_reviews.text:
We had tofu pho and tofu crepe.

We come from Brno where the Vietnamese cuisine is on very high level. We came here based on nice reviews and idea that the food will be good taste at least. We were highly disappointed, I normally don’t dare to write reviews, but here it was very bad experience. The pho had very poor broth taste, but mainly it was sweet which was strange and the main spice we tasted was clove. We also ordered the crepes, they were only heated up and very oily, it reminded us the czech potato pancake “bramboraky”. The filling was tasty / allright but one thing that saved the food and made us feel like let’s finish it was soya sauce.

Unfortunately don’t recommend.

google_reviews.author: Martina G.
google_reviews.text:
Banh Mi is delicious! Very authentic! The Crepes are good, but a bit boring & the herbs are missing(like you get normally in Vietnam). The Maracuja Juice ist very good!
Very nice personell & cute Location you can also sit outside)

google_reviews.author: Jefferson Salgado
google_reviews.text:
Saturday night with Music Jam 💜
100% recommended

google_reviews.author: Philip Delaney
google_reviews.text: Best cooked pizza yet.. lovely crispy base

google_reviews.author: Alexei Stachanoff
google_reviews.text: A melting pot

google_reviews.author: Sanel Durakovic
google_reviews.text: Great food and nice atmosphere

google_reviews.author: Mohammad Zhian Asadzadeh
google_reviews.text: Friendly staff, good atmosphere

google_reviews.author: Elli Pfeifer
google_reviews.text: Very nice café with great service, very nice owner and her employees.

google_reviews.author: Peter Heschl
google_reviews.text: A nice, cozy get-together with a glass of wine or a beer. They also have light snacks; you feel comfortable there. Sometimes it's a bit noisy, but it's just a nice pub.

google_reviews.author: Stanisław Sikora
google_reviews.text: Good beer on the road

google_reviews.author: Lukas Ott
google_reviews.text: Delicious pastries and very friendly service :)

google_reviews.author: eleanna top
google_reviews.text: The food was rasty food and the stuff friendly. There was plenty of room to sit and the temperature was ok. The only downside is that they accept only cash.

google_reviews.author: The Disaster71
google_reviews.text: Underrated restaurant in Graz

google_reviews.author: Cassidy Belle
google_reviews.text:
Best burgers I've had in a long time!
Great menu options, creative flavours, and quick service. Highly recommend!
They were also really careful for our group, as some of us had allergies.

google_reviews.author: FK Sarajevo The Best Of 1946
google_reviews.text:
Everything was amazing 👌
Here is my order.

google_reviews.author: Gianpietro Iacobucci
google_reviews.text: Worst burger i ever ate, I ordered speck on the burger and was nothing, meat was sloppy, pommes also. Never again.

google_reviews.author: Dimas Otaño
google_reviews.text:
Had food here on Sunday night, a few hours later the stomach pain started, the vomits and what else + a high fever. I was all day in bed yesterday and I'm still not feeling good.

I'm in the middle of my exam season so loosing a whole two days because of the noodles from this place really pisses me off.

Unless you don't mind getting super sick, vomiting and feeling like seven trains ran on top of you for a few days, I do not recommend EVER eating here.

I live 200 metres away and I had been there a few times in the last year, never again.

google_reviews.author: Krishna PadmanabhaDas
google_reviews.text: Its a nice small chinese place close to the art university.. the servings are quite big , so you wont leave this place hungry .. I wouldnt say the food is great , but they are quite okay , and they have very reasonably priced combo menus.. the lady who serves is very friendly even though she doesn't speak much english , and she got  me complementary warm  chinese Baiju everytime I was there ...

google_reviews.author: fabien
google_reviews.text: Simple and effective buffet, some dishes are really well prepared and quite authentic. As all the other Chinese buffets some dishes are not really Chinese as they have to adapt to the local tastes.

google_reviews.author: B W
google_reviews.text: Especially the vegan options are amazing (labelled vegetarian). Great Service and very friendly.

google_reviews.author: David S.
google_reviews.text: If you long for an authentic 1980s-style Chinese experience, including weird food that vaguely resembles something Asian, and MSG as the central ingredient, do give them a try.

google_reviews.author: Vlog GoooLLL
google_reviews.text:
Hi 👋 first of all, im travaller for the football games.
The atmosphere/place was good, i couldn't eat something because, before i go there i already eat something but i checked the menu and was looking good ! Thats why i given 4 star.
Service was good. Don't expect more, they did her job
The price is not expensive, it is what it should be.
Mr. Adnan helped me very good. The lady was to seriously.
The owner went all tables and talk with the visitors. That was nice and professional.

They even walk with me to the taxi. I asked where i can find the taxi, That was friendly

Vlog GoooLLL

google_reviews.author: Fabian
google_reviews.text:
Have been there about 100 times now, Shishas are perfect almost everytime. Service usually is also very polite, but not guaranteed, it depends on the day! But there are many new employees now, who are motivated and always friendly. Big improvement!
Also the location is perfect and it's quite comfortable. But if you really just want to chill and relax with friends, there are better, rather cozier shisha bars!

Downsides: if you book a table on weekends, you have to leave the bar after 2 hours to make room for new customers. Which makes sense, a lot of bars/restaurants do that, but it's still annoying and smth worth mentioning.
They also don't always serve food and have a very limited variety to choose from.

All in all, there definitely is a reason why I've been visiting that place for years now. Well deserved 4 stars tending towards 5 stars.

google_reviews.author: Gergely Palfy
google_reviews.text: Staff is nice, but it takes a lot of time till you get served. The shishas are very good and the coal gets changed frequently. The music is very loud which makes conversing difficult.  There are a lot of very pretty girls there usually.

google_reviews.author: Hendrik Bower
google_reviews.text:
The only "beer" they serve is Heineken - in Austria... come on.

The whole atmosphere is geared towards teenagers, so loud trashy music makes conversation difficult. The shishas were OK, and fresh coals brought round often enough.

google_reviews.author: João Costa
google_reviews.text: Good place with good ambiance. The workers are very nice. A lot of shisha flavours available as well as good music.

google_reviews.author: Debjyoti Banerjee
google_reviews.text: Gril and Box, a Chinese restaurant situated opposite the HBF bus station in Graz, offers a diverse menu of Chinese cuisine with generous portions. Convenient take-away options are also available, catering to both vegetarian and non-vegetarian preferences. The establishment accepts both card payments and cash transactions.

google_reviews.author: JZ
google_reviews.text: This spot is severely underrated, the food is good and filling and reasonably priced. They are also open after 22pm which is rare. Far better than anything inside the train station.

google_reviews.author: Crazy Racer
google_reviews.text: Pretty good food, got rice when ordered noodles and no sauce with dim sims. Boring tofu

google_reviews.author: Sandra Bad Ces
google_reviews.text: Everything we ate was delicious and with very good prices! Loved it 😊

google_reviews.author: Raphael
google_reviews.text: I really can't understand the bad reviews of this place. Me or my girlfriend pick something up from there at least once a week. For sure one of the better if not the best spot in Graz to pick up a hot box of noodles. The lady is always generous with the food and from time to time you can even catch her smile. Only thing that I would change is getting a separate fryer for the tofu (because vegetarian people generally don't like it if their tofu tastes like chicken) and repair the sign out front.

google_reviews.author: Monika Gstir
google_reviews.text: Quick and easy home-style cooking; the portions aren't too big, they're very tasty and convenient. I take the meals out of the freezer in the morning and heat them up in the microwave at 900 watts for 2.5 minutes at lunchtime – it's perfect!

google_reviews.author: Brigitte Pechtrager
google_reviews.text: The smell of the microwave alone is amazing – it smells like food!! – unlike with Meals on Wheels from ISD, where you have to clean the microwave with vinegar after heating up food to get rid of the stench.

google_reviews.author: Jan Ovcar
google_reviews.text: I feel like I never knew what Gyros is. No vegetables, meat completely overpowered with oregano, badly cooked, only half of pita bread which just feels cheap as hell - give me the whole damn slice not half of it. The price should be at least halfed to deserve a better rating.

google_reviews.author: Natali Vučić
google_reviews.text: After eating (see picture).. complete disaster.

google_reviews.author: Vulkin
google_reviews.text: Ordered gyros, meat was stinking up our apartment. Fries stale, god knows when made. The price is too high for what you get!!!

google_reviews.author: Princenza Oluje
google_reviews.text: Thanks! No1 Restaurant in Graz🥰 pizza and burger ist the best 🥰🥰🥰🥰🥰🥰

google_reviews.author: Turhan Akmese
google_reviews.text: Super

google_reviews.author: Clubsmen
google_reviews.text: Tasty espresso. Good personnel. Hot reccomends.

google_reviews.author: Luca Bell
google_reviews.text: Very friendly service. Had a great cup of coffee

google_reviews.author: Antonija Volarić
google_reviews.text: The best caffe I ever had, lovely and friendly people! I recommend!

google_reviews.author: M.G.
google_reviews.text: I won't go there again because the owner and the nimble waiter are extremely unfriendly to dogs. Whether you sit outside or inside in the aisle, you're not allowed to let your small dog—and we're talking a small Chihuahua—sit on your lap because you're violating hygiene regulations. What a load of nonsense! They think they're something special anyway. Thank goodness there are other places that don't have a problem with it. Who needs that? Nobody.

google_reviews.author: Luis Bruchmann
google_reviews.text: Very good cappuccino, and the waiter is always quick, cheerful, and humorous. And when the weather is nice, you can also enjoy your drink outside in the large bar area – simply wonderful in the summer morning...

google_reviews.author: Ekkehard Missmann

google_reviews.author: thors freunde blödi
google_reviews.text: The beer garden is great; the owner has his own unique charm and is very attentive. Unfortunately, we didn't eat there, but we've been twice before and always felt very comfortable. We also struck up conversations with other guests right away. It has a certain typical Graz feel, like it used to be. Top-notch!

google_reviews.author: Arno Sommer
google_reviews.text: Since I'm a vegetarian and the restaurant offered very little, the owner created a special menu for me. And she kept it going for weeks! I was absolutely thrilled, especially with the delicious food I was served. The service and the atmosphere were simply fantastic for such a small restaurant. It felt truly homey and cozy. I'll definitely be back.

google_reviews.author: Michael K
google_reviews.text: The atmosphere was awful. When we pointed out the incorrect order to the waiter (though I think it was the owner – white hair, cigarette in his mouth), he promptly gave us the middle finger. He was probably frustrated that the place was empty. No wonder.

google_reviews.author: Joseph Sciarrone
google_reviews.text: Hunger brought us there, and we were pleasantly surprised. The food was freshly prepared, the schnitzel was pounded, not a ready-made product, and it tasted just as good. We'll be back again soon, and won't just drive past.

google_reviews.author: Hans S
google_reviews.text:
Don’t listen to Kurt!!! This place slaps but please start accepting card man.

Service & drinks are gas - solid 10/10. Can recommend this place.

Have fond memories of visiting this place with Michi during Christmas.

google_reviews.author: Svea H
google_reviews.text: I was at a concert here. The staff was so friendly, the atmosphere was great, they paid close attention to fire safety regulations, and it was beautifully decorated for Halloween :)

google_reviews.author: Viva Alex (VivaAlexTV)
google_reviews.text:
This isn't just about classic coffee house culture or a cozy afternoon drink, but also about social celebrations of all kinds. From birthday parties and the usual festivities like Christmas, Carnival, Halloween, and the like, to wildly escalating karaoke parties and live performances, primarily on weekends, there's something for everyone. The staff is exceptionally friendly and communicative. When things get crazy and thirst strikes, the owner steps in and tirelessly fills the glasses, when he's not busy choking on his guitar, treating his guests to the full repertoire of Austro-Pop culture.

Thus, you could say that this place is a place to go for any time of day or night, and for any kind of mood, and offers something for everyone.

google_reviews.author: M. B.
google_reviews.text: A great place for any mood. Cozy for after-work coffee. Top-notch. Brunch every Saturday. Super varied and delicious.

google_reviews.author: A. M.
google_reviews.text: Many years ago, when the café was still called Bahia, I was a regular here and felt very comfortable. Now I've returned. The ambiance and friendliness have barely changed; it's just a shame that they only serve light meals.

google_reviews.author: Jan May
google_reviews.text: The cuisine impresses with its simplicity. The host also seems to have a knack for keeping prices reasonable, resulting in excellent value for money. Breakfast is warm and adequate. The rooms are inexpensive; you'll discover why if you decide to stay overnight. The room's basic functionality is its defining characteristic, but unfortunately, nothing else is particularly impressive. It would take a hardy Neanderthal to find this standard acceptable. But let the pictures speak for themselves...

google_reviews.author: Hubert und Ingrid
google_reviews.text: Excellent food at fair prices with friendly service.

google_reviews.author: Helmut McSchuly Schulhofer
google_reviews.text: Simply TOP-NOTCH – everything fresh and homemade. You can hear the schnitzel being pounded from the kitchen. The best fried chicken for miles around, and the best Cardinal Slice. Good, traditional Styrian cuisine, but there's much more. It's worth it. One drawback (or perhaps a plus): You should... make a reservation.

google_reviews.author: Heidemarie Latzko
google_reviews.text: I was invited to a birthday party and was very surprised by how many rooms this inn has. I felt very comfortable. The only thing that could be improved is the lighting in the room above the guest rooms; it could be a bit more creative.

google_reviews.author: Andrea BOTSARIS
google_reviews.text: Exceptionally good Styrian cuisine. Very large menu selection. Easily accessible by car with plenty of parking, mostly shaded. Very quiet despite being near a main road. Excellent value for money... There are also many retirees. Plenty of space for children and dogs too. If you want to eat like Grandma used to make, this is the place for you. The absolute highlight is the fried chicken with fries. Highly recommended... You can also order food by phone and pick it up at the agreed time. My dears, see you again... Thank you so much for an unforgettable lunch ❤️❤️❤️

google_reviews.author: Christian Chelu
google_reviews.text: The staff was very friendly and attentive, which created a lovely atmosphere. The dishes we enjoyed were absolutely delicious, and the way they were served—with style and attention to detail—was truly impressive. Highly recommend!

google_reviews.author: Michael Hartwell
google_reviews.text:
My wife and I were on holidays travelling through central Europe and came upon Koch Genuaa Atelier and our first impression was per the attached image (noting it was afternoon, getting dark and freezing with light drizzle - late December 2025), warm, bright, inviting and offering a huge selection of Biologica options.

We love Certified Organic (Biologica) Italian wines, in particular Villa Teresa's Prosecco....after travelling through several countries with none in sight, to see it out the front of this wonderful cafe really piqued our interest. As we stood there admiring the establishment and making up our minds if we should stop for a meal, Ute, the owner and gracious host, came out to greet us and walk us through the offerings, so welcoming and genuinely friendly, we had to go in.

We sat at the little table on the left of image right under the wine and alcohol selection cabinet, which was an awesome discussion topic during our visit, deciding on which local or European Biologica wine we should have with our meal. We should have been more adventurous and chosen an Österreich (Austrian) wine, but were so excited by the Villa Teresa that we ordered a bottle.

My wife and I have vastly different tastes, however agree that a good meal should be organic, seasonal, full of flavour and made with love and passion. That's exactly what we got. Whilst the menu is not extensive, there is enough selection to find something to have you walking away felling sated, nourished and very content, with good memories of the experience. A small menu is authentic, is made with precision and care and not prone to tourist trap huge selection quality deficiency.

Each of our meals were prepared to order in front of us. We watched the chef prepare these with care and passion, he was also serving us and was very professional and friendly, we felt like old friends not random walk-ins.

This is not a fast food experience, this is place to come and savour a glass of quality, healthy wine, have a great conversation (with your companions and the owner and staff) whilst your meal is being prepared, absolutely worth waiting for. But also noted there were selections in the cabinet made fresh by Ute and the team for take away or a quick bite in the cafe.

In summary, lovely staff, lovely cafe, wonderful experience. When we return to Graz we will definitely be visiting Koch Genuaa Atelier.

Thanks Ute and team for a magical experience, happy new year, wishing you all the best in 2026 and look forward to visiting again soon.

google_reviews.author: joan yang
google_reviews.text: Chanced upon KochGenussAtelier while visiting Graz. We were first surprised at the small dining space, 2 tables indoor and the healthy food. In wintery Graz, the salad, soup and apple cake baked by the owner's mother is stupendous. It is comforting,  healthy and delicious. We love the sincere and friendly service of the owner and the heart that goes into preparing the food.

google_reviews.author: Hannah Leschnik
google_reviews.text: The food is really delicious. The service is really friendly and welcoming. If you are in Graz it's really worth while to come and eat there.

google_reviews.author: zuzana_zu
google_reviews.text: Very fresh & tasty food. I woul recomend Pumpkin risotto with plums, Chili con carne was also really delicious.

google_reviews.author: Gabriella Rozsa
google_reviews.text: Yesterday we only had some drinks, more precisely orange punch and we loved the place and atmosphere so much that went back for early dinner today. I had hot Aperol that I had never had before and I have to admit  it was delicious, too. My friends had pizza and I asked for a small portion of gnocchi with cheese and spinach. Great atmosphere again, a very helpful and kind waitress and tasty meals and drinks. Hopefully we'll be back again, Danke sehr! Happy New Year!

google_reviews.author: gregori000
google_reviews.text: May be it is during the first week of the year, service was really “minimalistic”… we had to fetch our own pepper and salt. Food was not that good either. Soggy pizza and tasteless pasta. Will not go again.

google_reviews.author: A.C. Ismail
google_reviews.text:
its a litte nice restaurant, but it has nothing to do with "italian".The pizzas are alright, but ofcourse not selfmade.the dough is frozen and warmed up.so the pizza bottoms are bought, and then they top it with other fresh ingredients.nothing special, but if you hungry its fine.the service is dissapointing, even when the waitress was very nice, but nobody ever comes and askes if everything is fine and they dont see that your glass is empty and they dont ask if you want anything else.and at 21.15 i didnt get an espresso anymore, even when its open untill 22.00 (and in Internet even untill 24.00).they concentrate more on starting cleaning at about 20.00. so that they can go home.so the coffe machine was cleaned and all other guests who want a coffee after 20.30 after their dinner dont get it.and the answer is," because its not busy".
well its fine to eat when you are hungry but more not.

google_reviews.author: Gareth Simpson
google_reviews.text: I went in for an espresso, had it at the bar. €3,20 and then I was told that there was a €15 minimum to in pay by card. I asked why they didn't tell me before I ordered and was told that it was written outside. I looked on the way out to see a tiny message hanging on the wall not even near the door. These places who make so much money over harging tourists for mediocre food and service should accept cards for any amount. (Which is law by the way). Avoid at all costs!

google_reviews.author: Bryce Kerker
google_reviews.text: A bit hit or miss.  The tortellini was fantastic.  The salami and prosciutto pizza was pretty much just thrown together.  Meats stuck into large piles, cooked unevenly.

google_reviews.author: Audrone Telesiene

google_reviews.text:
Very friendly hosts, good food, and a lovely, shady beer garden.

Finally, a restaurant where I received a food recommendation! I often hear, "Everything's good here."

Thank you for the turkey strips with porcini mushrooms and penne.

The waitress is very skilled. It's also worth mentioning that the Budweiser on tap is excellent, as is the Bellini Cafe. The cozy dining room and the quiet beer garden complete this welcoming establishment.

The owner was still open, and there were several other guests inside. He didn't even bother to check who was coming in. We won't be going back to that place.

First of all the waiter was amazing, he explained everything to us in English even if we didn’t ask him to do it, he was kind and professional as well, atmosphere amazing, feeling like in a nature and in a living room in a same time, very welcoming atmosphere and cozy place.
Food also amazing we had 2 wienerschnitzel vom schwein und pommes frites. Very nice, recommending if you’re looking for calm and cozy place with amazing quality of food. Keep doing what you are doing.

google_reviews.author: NIXDE “Denamonix”
google_reviews.text: Such a beautiful place and lowly waiter

google_reviews.author: Fujimaru Takuji
google_reviews.text:
I had a Grill plate and salad for lunch.
The volume was fairly large, and the taste was excellent.
I wonder if they charge salad dressing fee to everybody, though.
Overall experience was satisfied.

google_reviews.author: Nico Prosser
google_reviews.text:
Since Grill-STOP opened, hardly a week goes by that I don't go there at least once. I'm completely sold.

1. The staff is incredibly friendly and attentive.

2. I absolutely love the atmosphere, both outside and inside, although I particularly like the bar inside ;)

3. And let's not forget the food! No matter what I order, it always tastes heavenly, whether it's a burger, calamari, or meat. I have no idea how they always manage to make the fries so crispy. A bowl of soup is a must for me every time I visit. All in all, I'm always extremely satisfied there, and I'm sure that will continue to be the case.

google_reviews.author: Deejay AvenTus
google_reviews.text:
I eat at Grillstop almost every day and I have to say I love the food there. Everything is always fresh, and it's one of the few places that prepares almost all of their dishes on a charcoal grill. I've already tried the entire menu and I'm truly impressed by the variety and quality of each dish. All in all, I have to say the mixed grill is my favorite, although I think the suckling pig at Grillstop is by far the best in all of Graz!

I highly recommend this restaurant!

google_reviews.author: Tom R
google_reviews.text:
Excellent quality, very tasty, friendly service, and quick service. Very invitingly presented, large portions. Everything was freshly prepared. 😊 Despite the daily special of suckling pig, I opted for excellent cevapcici, and my choice was perfect.
If the rather noisy passing cars and trucks were the crashing waves, you'd think you were sitting by the sea in Croatia. But I could have chosen a quieter spot than directly behind the hedge that separates the garden from the busy road.
I'll definitely be back!

google_reviews.author: Emanuel Hirsch
google_reviews.text: We were really looking forward to some grilled food and then found ourselves standing in front of locked doors with a sign saying "LOCAL FOR RENT," even though the internet said the restaurant was open. If you're going to close your restaurant, please be so kind as to announce it on your website, because this is an absolute outrage.

google_reviews.author: Harri Inkinen
google_reviews.text: If you are looking for an authentic gasthaus atmosphere, this is the place. On the scale of 1-5 how great of a gasthaus this is, it is a pure 5. Great food, all the dishes were unbelievably delicious.

google_reviews.author: Gerald Sommer
google_reviews.text: Typical Austrian Gasthaus with lovely service, great food like the Styrian Backhendl, moderate prices - really lovely smile included!

google_reviews.author: Matyáš Krahulík
google_reviews.text: A nice restaurant with very good service. The food was nothing special, but tasty.

google_reviews.author: Sophie Horvath
google_reviews.text:
The place was nice, the food was tasty and the show we got was even better. The service was fast and attentive.
We really hope that the drunk Lady and her dog got home safe and Sound. 🙂
Btw it was really kind of the bartender to literally bring her home.

google_reviews.author: Gudrun M.-T.
google_reviews.text:
Good food
Older place

google_reviews.author: Katerina Petrevska
google_reviews.text: My favorite place in Graz! Polite and respectful waiters and place full with positive vibes.

google_reviews.author: aygün doğan
google_reviews.text: Very good atmosphere, friendly staff, good vibes.

google_reviews.author: nikola strametz
