Great wine, great Snacks!
Service is really friendly. Location is great.
Parking space easy to find

google_reviews.author: Markus Kaiser
google_reviews.text: If you're into living room furniture occupying  the streets, that's the place in Graz to be. No seriously: go there. Thank me later.

google_reviews.author: Raphael Pircher
google_reviews.text: good

google_reviews.author: meltemi
google_reviews.text: The café offers not only delicious food but also a warm atmosphere and impeccable cleanliness. They serve the tastiest latte in Graz! The owners speak several languages, which makes communication easy. The café is dog-friendly.

google_reviews.author: Gregor Götz
google_reviews.text: As you can see, it tasted very good. The world is still alright here, keep it up! 🤩

google_reviews.author: Christian Egerer
google_reviews.text: Always very good menu offerings. With soup and salad.

google_reviews.author: Roca Dani
google_reviews.text: Ordered a lunch menu for pickup today. It was very, very good, friendly service, and a truly great lunch for a reasonable price. Perfect 🙏

google_reviews.author: Ursula Goessl
google_reviews.text: A lovely café...dear owners...right in the heart of Graz

google_reviews.author: Mihai Costescu
google_reviews.text: Just a 5-minute walk from the bus station. A small, cozy place with a great atmosphere, craft beer, and lovely music. Definitely on our revisit list. 🍻🎶

google_reviews.author: Colin Campbell
google_reviews.text:
A great little bar with a lovely owner
Plenty of seating both inside and out. Excellent choice of background music to chill out to. They do a bring your own vinyl night on Tuesdays. We'll arrange our next visit to coincide with this.

google_reviews.author: Winston Hoyle
google_reviews.text: Small place with good vibes. Homemade liquors and craft beer. Decent prices and the service is really nice.

google_reviews.author: Amy Loren
google_reviews.text: For sure there's no place like this on Austria. My favourite ever! Maria is such a fantastic host, the Limoncellos are just AMAZING, the decoration is so cozy... It's a place we can see tender everywhere, in each detail, even in the toilet for the ladies. My better experience on a place on Austria. I should took a lot of pictures, but I couldn't this time.

google_reviews.author: Aleksi Laurila
google_reviews.text: Lovable owner, amazing service, impcable products. What can I say I was blown away by the place. If you are looking for the best austrian craft beer - come here. You’l be surprised.

google_reviews.author: Alun Lewis
google_reviews.text: Not a bad little boozer

google_reviews.author: Günter Pratter
google_reviews.text: It always has been a small, fine restaurant with friendly service. You're immediately welcomed by the regulars!

google_reviews.author: Brigitte Schwab
google_reviews.text: Lives up to its name. Small but cozy.

google_reviews.author: LUCIE VASKOVA
google_reviews.text: Small, charming, but always fun. I highly recommend it.

google_reviews.author: Weihenstein “HAVC Tec”
google_reviews.text:
Guests urinate on the tree in front of the restaurant.
Absolutely unacceptable

google_reviews.author: ravid barzilay
google_reviews.text: In the evening there is a stand that sells rice, noodles and wok. Osman, the nicest person on the planet, is doing above and beyond to make his customers happy and satisfied. Very recommend!!!

google_reviews.author: Fran Cormack
google_reviews.text:
This place is open again, serving excellent Asian dishes.

We had the caramel chicken on rice and the Kung Po.

Both delicious.

google_reviews.author: Dávid Herskovits
google_reviews.text: not great

google_reviews.author: REIS J.E.
google_reviews.text: Ordered from Standl6 via Too Good to Go. Three boxes totaling five. The boxes vary widely; the price is €2.99, so I think it's reasonable. However, I would never pay €10 more than the regular price for a box with 90% bami. The flavors are okay, but not entirely authentic.

google_reviews.author: Vera Petra
google_reviews.text:
We purchased a €12.99 offer from Too Good To Go for €3.99, but only received a box of noodles worth a mere €4.50. The noodles were dried out, and the meat smelled sour. We were also asked to pay an additional 50 cents for the packaging.
When we inquired about this, we were very rudely told that it wasn't their problem and that the €12.99 charge was Too Good To Go's mistake.
Unfortunately, I had to leave the food there because it was inedible.
There are so many good restaurants in Graz, but this one is unfortunately not recommended.

google_reviews.author: Wesley Pechler
google_reviews.text: If I could give 6 stars I would! Friendly staff, quick service, and absolutely delicious food at very affordable prices. Highly recommend the tiramisu and the seitan wrap, which might have been the best wrap I’ve ever had. Both indoor seating as well as a terrace.

google_reviews.author: Fabian H (faboo)
google_reviews.text: Absolutely lovely place for a vegan meal in Graz. They offer a lunch option and a la carte after 5pm. We had the cheese burger, schnitzel burger and tiramisu. Everything tasted great although the texture of the vegan schnitzel could be improved. Service was friendly and fast, the atmosphere is comfortable and homey. My favorite part was the tiramisu which was super rich, creamy and with a nice bitter coffee taste, even non-vegans would love it for sure. I will definitely go back when I visit Graz again!

google_reviews.author: AMIT SINGH
google_reviews.text: A pure vegan restaurant, with two dining floors. Ground floor is a bit busy whereas the first floor has decent space with chair dinning as well as has low table dinning. First floor would be a good choice if you have children. Try their falafel plate which I think offer you a decent amount of delicious and healthy food. Overall, I would recommend a go if you really looking for vegan food.

google_reviews.author: Daniel Bender
google_reviews.text:
Nice service and food. But extremely intense flavours. Could be more "bland" for my taste, since the Chutney was sooo intense.
Went there with toogoodtogo, great portion for 4 or 5 €

google_reviews.author: Jure Šuklje
google_reviews.text: Good amount of outdoor seating available, so we didn't have any trouble getting a free table around lunch time, were we were quickly greeted by a friendly staff. It was quite hard to pick something on the menu because the options were so great. We ended up with a daily menu and a burger. Both were absolutely fantastic. A must visit when in Graz.

restaurants.name: Wurscht is mei Business - Heisse Würstel

google_reviews.author: D. Fork
google_reviews.text:
Standl 5 is not just serving hot dogs, it celebrates it.

Our kids got spontaneously very hungry and we spotted this place, expecting nothing but a hot dog. The first pleasant surprise was the charming owner organizing us a place to sit. We ordered several types of hot dogs and each of us was very convinced of his/her choice. Great buns, yummy sausages and excellent sauces etc.

Next day, we all agreed that we need a second round. And the great experience was repeated and even exceeded (service, food & atmosphere).

IN SUMMARY:

The ratings at Google and other places are really well deserved. This stall is a gem!

google_reviews.author: Max Mustermann
google_reviews.text: Friendly service and good food.  Had a delicious käsekrainer (Austrian junk food) with mustard, Semmel (bread), and freshly grated horseradish, with Gösser Bier on tap.   Brought back a lot of memories from my student years.   Staff was very friendly as well.

google_reviews.author: Lili Stajer
google_reviews.text: very tasty, fast, nice service, try the kartoffelsalad for sure

google_reviews.author: Lynette Chang
google_reviews.text:
Chanced upon this booth in the middle of the square - totally deserved the high ratings!

Simple food, good value, lovely friendly team who takes pride in serving the customers! You get to sit and people watch in the main square too.

google_reviews.author: G Donna
google_reviews.text: This restaurant focuses on farm-to-table freshness, and it really shows in the flavor. I had the grilled seasonal vegetables with a poached egg and hollandaise — super fresh and beautifully plated. The sourdough bread was warm and crusty, served with herb butter. You can tell the ingredients are top quality. The staff was knowledgeable about where everything was sourced from. I’ll be back soon to try more from the seasonal menu.

google_reviews.author: C SP
google_reviews.text: The coffee was very good but the croissants were disappointing. Although they supposedly have flavored fillings, the filling is very delluted in some sort of cream where you can barely taste anything. For example I instead of the pistachio filling, it was a white cream with a very faint taste of pistachio. Same with the Lotus one. Definitely not worth 4,5.

google_reviews.author: Arno Vonk
google_reviews.text: A must visit in Graz! Delicious  croissants and coffee 👌🏻. A very cosy place in the heart of Graz. And above all: very friendly and lovely owners 😍. We will be back soon 🥐🥐☕️

google_reviews.author: Sandra Sulzer
google_reviews.text: Very nice little self service cafe in the heart of Graz 🍵 we had the raspberry and pistachio croissant and they were very delicious. Even handmade, at least i think so🥐

google_reviews.author: Noel
google_reviews.text: Amazing service and very good Coffe!

google_reviews.author: Raymar A Lara Garcia
google_reviews.text: Lovely place. Delicious croissant and coffee. The staff is very friendly.

google_reviews.author: Ling Ho
google_reviews.text: Cheese sausage fresh from the grill with warm toasty bun on the side. It was prepared on the spot so it is fresh, warm and delicious! Service was very friendly too. We had the mayonnaise sauce to go with. Highly recommend, great for snack or light brunch while on the go!

google_reviews.author: Krzysztof Jelnicki
google_reviews.text: great pork sandwich (€5.9) - fresh and crispy bread, perfectly marinated and grilles meat, sauces, add-ons.  Highly recommended!

google_reviews.author: Patrick Havice
google_reviews.text: The Steierer Hot Dog & the Kartoffelsalat are perfection, and the men working the stall are fast and friendly. Definitely a great stop!

google_reviews.author: Ramon Duncan
google_reviews.text: I got there at 12:06 and was rudely informed that breakfast was no longer available. THE PLACE SUX

google_reviews.author: berke ayrancioglu
google_reviews.text: A sausage stand right at the city center(Hauptplatz). I tried Frankfurter, it was nice and the sauce was great. I think it was a bit pricey but it is normal when the location is considered. I would suggest for tourists

google_reviews.author: Ferenc Brukner
google_reviews.text: Super!

google_reviews.author: V. H.
google_reviews.text: The best pub with charm. Places like this must be preserved!

google_reviews.author: barbara Irrenfried
google_reviews.text: Competent and friendly staff. A brilliant chef. Great for darts. The exclusive parties with live music are top-notch. Best Frankfurt restaurant in town.

google_reviews.author: Georg Tiesenhausen
google_reviews.text: Always very cozy, very pleasant to have a beer with friends.

google_reviews.author: dirty
google_reviews.text: Beautiful view of Graz. Many tables nicely spaced, simply wonderful.

google_reviews.author: Gulfam Hussain
google_reviews.text: Today I visited LEND KEBAP with my colleagues. We tried their Döner. Honestly, the taste was pretty average. The prices are okay, not too high, but the bread was really hard, and the chicken wasn’t juicy at all. The sauces were also disappointing. What bothered me the most was that the owner was really rude. Overall, just a normal experience

google_reviews.author: David P
google_reviews.text: Fantastic kebabs, great beer and super people running the place. Stop here, it’s tremendous.

google_reviews.author: Lorenz B
google_reviews.text: These guys have good kebab, which i can recommend, however what i really enjoy there is the lahmacun. It's so Beautifully prepared and tastes like slices of heaven.

google_reviews.author: Simeon
google_reviews.text:
i know this place since several years, and i feel compelled to finally leave a review 🙏🏼

i really appreciate the variety of food they got, which is perfect for having options, without overwhelming a customer. the owners, or the staff there are so welcoming and great hosts, and everybody there feels treated with respect.

they say if you want to find a good place in the city to eat, go where the locals are, and this one is one of those. 🙏🏼

google_reviews.author: RV
google_reviews.text: We had a quick stop for a pizza at Lend Kebap while we were in Lendplatz. It was easy on pocket and tummy filling. I am impressed.

google_reviews.author: berke ayrancioglu
google_reviews.text: A small dumpling restaurant close to the historical center. They make both savory and sweet. From the savory, We tried the ones with meat and potato/cheese. Meat was also good but tge one with potato and cheese was better. As the dessert we had the one with poppy seeds with vanilla topping, it was also nice. The lady was really kind. Prices were not low but I guess understandable considering the location.

google_reviews.author: Josh Friedman
google_reviews.text: Absolutely delicious pierogis in the heart of Graz! Can't wait to go back, and took some frozen ones home too. Limited seating, so best to reserve -- a lesson I learned after I couldn't get a seat the first time I tried to go.

google_reviews.author: Dimas Otaño
google_reviews.text: The service is not the most welcoming ever, atmosphere is cute, but the prices for pierogis are outrageous. There's absolutely no justification for charging 15€ for what is dough and some pork minced meat. I will not be coming back. The whole point of pierogis is a quick, easy and cheap meal. Not paying 20€ for a few dumplings and a water

google_reviews.author: Jessie Mo
google_reviews.text:
The dumplings were okay, not really my taste, but fine overall. Unfortunately, the service was poor. The lady serving us seemed stressed and came across as unfriendly. I could not pay by card and was forced to pay in cash, which was not mentioned anywhere in advance.

Also, I wanted to order a small portion of sweet dumplings, but I was told that a small portion is only available for takeaway and I had to order a medium portion instead. This was also not mentioned anywhere.

Overall, not a good experience and I would not recommend it.

google_reviews.author: Micki Thorson
google_reviews.text: What a wonderful place! I ordered the apple juice with mineral water & the savory mix pierogi. Both were so delicious, I wish I could have this every day!

google_reviews.author: George Papanastasiou
google_reviews.text: Mittagsmenü, nice portion, ridiculously cheap, very kind staff!

google_reviews.author: Rosna Rose
google_reviews.text: Good food and nice place!!

google_reviews.author: Alexey Markushin
google_reviews.text: Tasty and cozy, they have children's corner with toys and books

google_reviews.author: Matko Đipalo
google_reviews.text: A great warm place.

google_reviews.author: Ricki Wedenig-Ottowitz
google_reviews.text: Super!

google_reviews.author: Elisabetta Valgoi
google_reviews.text: It got even better and prettier than last time. Great to take away as well.

google_reviews.author: Christoph Schmidt
google_reviews.text: Very nice place to eat. Food was excellent and the atmosphere was very good. Very recommended.

google_reviews.author: Andrej Galoić
google_reviews.text: It’s a good place to drink coffee but damn, it’s so much people there and impossible to find a table. We needed to wait some minutes for a table to be freed up. And the tables are very close to each other so it doesn’t give a feeling of privacy/intimacy.

google_reviews.author: Stephen Ellwood
google_reviews.text: Very trendy, bustling Italian restaurant. We were lucky to get a table. Food is really good, Prices are a little on the high side but ok.

google_reviews.author: Jean-Philippe Andreu
google_reviews.text:
We ordered a Heringsschmaus for 2 (as take away because of the COVID restrictions).
We got:
- 1x Maki (and I mean 1 single Maki, not a set of Makis)
- 1x Nigiri (schrimp) (again 1 single Nigiri)
- 1x sashimi (Tuna) (again 1 single sashimi))
- 3 different herring salads (~200ml each)
- 1x octopus salad (~200ml)
- 1x shrimps cocktail (~200ml)
- 1x ceviche with 1 finger sized piece of salmon, sword fish and sea bass
- Some focaccia bread
- Tiramisu for 2
For 78€ I was expecting a treat. The overall quantity was barely OK and the quality pretty average.
There was not a lot of fish all together and the shrimps cocktail was seriously lacking of shrimps.
Guys, I understand the situation with the COVID is not an easy one for restaurants but I felt completely ripped off.
Half of the price would have been roughly OK but for nearly 80€ I could have had 4x sashimi moriawase (big portions) at the yamamoto sushibar (one of the best sushi restaurants in Graz)!
So to conclude: I never ever will again order something at this place.

google_reviews.author: Petar Kamenecki
google_reviews.text: The staff were extremely welcoming, understanding and professional. The atmosphere was minimalistic yet elegant. The food (sous-vide lamb shoulder) was exquisetly made; very soft as well as having a rich depth of flavour. It also came out fairly quick, with little waiting time (note that it was not very crowded). The focus is definitely on a quality service, which the price mirrors. Recommended.

google_reviews.author: christina jameson
google_reviews.text: This has got to be the best meal we’ve had since landing in Europe 3 weeks ago. Go for the surprise menu - 3 courses plus a snack to start and it was fantastic - pomegranate cocktail was really good too! Won’t tell you what we had as it will spoil your surprise but really worth the money. Staff are friendly and very attentive too. Only negative for us was all the smokers!

google_reviews.author: Katherine W K
google_reviews.text: The service here is top notch, friendly and knowledgeable. Enjoyed the cream of celery soup along with a main of saddle of venison. Perfectly cooked medium-rare with a rich jus. Highly recommend.

google_reviews.author: Martha Milch
google_reviews.text:
As a seasoned culinary explorer, walking into dreizehn by Gauster feels like entering an oasis of modern elegance in the heart of Graz. The interior blends cool minimalism with warmth—cushioned chairs, soft lighting, and sleek bar artfully invite conversation. Morning sunlight filters onto the terrace at Franziskanerplatz, marrying urban charm with soft energy.

For lunch, I chose the thoughtful lunch set—a delicate starter followed by a beautifully plated main course, finished with rich espresso and a sweet treat. It’s a concise meal done exceptionally well. But the real gem? The surprise tasting menu—each course a refined revelation, inventive ravioli here, exceptional beef tartare there, all bursting with seasonal creativity.

The wine pairing elevated the experience—staff suggested bottles that complemented textures and flavors perfectly. Conversations flowed easily, drinks lingered leisurely, and service felt intuitive without ever being overbearing. Whether it's a quick yet indulgent mid-day meal or an immersive evening of surprises, dreizehn by Gauster delivers a culinary journey that's stylish, sophisticated, and deeply satisfying. Highly recommended for those who crave elegance, innovation, and heartfelt hospitality in every bite.

google_reviews.author: Patricia O'Brien
google_reviews.text: This is a high end establishment: fairly small menu though with much attention to detail. Great knowledge from our waiter who understood everything about the dishes and the wines - and even more about their 15 or 20 varieties of schnapps! Different.

google_reviews.author: Nastaran H
google_reviews.text: Food was good, atmosphere was nice

google_reviews.author: Moncef Amdouni
google_reviews.text: Lovely 🙂❤

google_reviews.author: Thomas A. PAL
google_reviews.text: STRINDBERG stands for QUALITY. The menu features delicious dishes – often seasonal. The server is simply WOW – an older, youthful lady with lots of energy and a great sense of humor – she's been doing her job with the same passion for decades = TOP. The food is very tasty, although the portion size of the Cordon Bleu could be larger. Plenty of comfortable seating both inside and across the street. Fair prices. Numerous free parking spaces (including underground parking) are available. Highly recommended!!! Best regards

google_reviews.author: Sandra Dauti
google_reviews.text: Das Essen wenn man bedient wird ist sehr gut. Ich selbst habe eine Stunde Mittagspause und bin schon des öfteren auf die Terrasse gegangen um meine Pause, mit einem Getränk und einem Essen dort zu verbringen. Leider bin ich auch schon aufgestanden und gegangen weil kein Kellner es für nötig gehalten hat vorbei zu kommen, am Nebentisch wurde den Leuten das Essen gebracht und ich wurde nicht gefragt ob ich etwas möchte, ich hab fertig geraucht und bin woanders hin gegangen, wirklich schade

google_reviews.author: Johanna Peitler
google_reviews.text: Attentive service, excellent food, wonderful atmosphere!

google_reviews.author: sameer ang
google_reviews.text: No restaurant with this name and worst food in city

google_reviews.author: moslem mohammadzadeh
google_reviews.text: Everything is great, food quality and the service

google_reviews.author: Aiman Syed
google_reviews.text: Authentic afghan food with reasonable prices.

google_reviews.author: Qurban Ali Mohammadi
google_reviews.text: sbs

google_reviews.author: Elia batool
google_reviews.text: The food wasn't good, the staff were unfriendly, they didn't have a prescription, and it cost a lot of money. Like, how hungry people were? Did they still pay once?

google_reviews.author: Wanda Chui
google_reviews.text: This is a nice local restaurant that serves Austral cuisine with some twist. The fish soup and grilled sturgeon fish we ordered were delicious and nicely decorated. But the pork belly & octopus was not as good, even though the dish looked very nice. The octopus was mushy and flavorless. It tasted like it had been in fridge for quite a while.

google_reviews.author: Martha Milch
google_reviews.text:
I recently enjoyed dinner at Caylend, a creative fusion restaurant nestled near Mariahilferplatz. The interior blends sleek modern wood tones and elegant lighting with a splash of Caribbean-inspired flair—it felt both stylish and relaxed.

I started with grilled octopus and bluefin tuna steak—each dish showcased bold global flavors with a local Graz twist. The pork belly duo was a standout, slow-cooked and deeply flavorful. Portions delighted without feeling heavy, and the tasting experience opened my palate beautifully.

The wine list and cocktails—like the Mango Margarita—added to the evening’s charm. Service was polished and welcoming. Staff guided my choices with knowledge and ease, keeping the pacing relaxed and enjoyable.

Caylend pairs inventive, globally inspired dishes with a refined yet cozy atmosphere. It’s a standout in Graz where flavor and flair meet—I'm already looking forward to returning.

google_reviews.author: Dmitry CuisineMaster
google_reviews.text: This fusion restaurant is a seafood paradise! I recently tried their exceptional fish dishes and was genuinely impressed by their quality and taste. Desserts taste fantastic! Definitely a must-try for anyone seeking innovative flavors and a remarkable dining experience!

google_reviews.author: Peter H
google_reviews.text: Managed to get a last table while in town. The food is super delicious and unique in flavours. Service is very friendly and food arrived just perfect amount of wait time. They also offer a wide range of wine lists to go with each dish. Highly recommend the tuna with octopus ink pasta. For desserts, definitely try the peanut butter with ice cream. Must reserve in advance.

google_reviews.author: Antonija Rittuper
google_reviews.text: Tasting menu was perfectly created, from first dish till the last one. Well done chefs!

google_reviews.author: MMA
google_reviews.text: A very cool place with friendly people who welcome anyone walks in and  they share food and culture with guests :)

google_reviews.author: Ralf Vetter
google_reviews.text: Gmota is a student organization based on the Chaos Theory. All activities are free, although donations are strongly appreciated. All necessary work is also done on a voluntary basis. Life in the organization is very simple.

google_reviews.author: Car Sch
google_reviews.text: I didn't even know there was a free room there. I'll tidy it up next week, and I've got some things for there too.

google_reviews.author: Marvel Forever
google_reviews.text: It's not always perfectly clean and tidy, but it serves its purpose.

google_reviews.author: Láďa Dvoukvítek Czerný
google_reviews.text: It was written everywhere that it was open on Sundays, but it was deserted, empty.

google_reviews.author: L A
google_reviews.text:
The food is ok. It’s not freshly cooked, and it doesn’t stay hot for long as it’s reheated in the microwave.
It’s the kind of a quick pick me up food, nothing more.
The Eggplant is too sweet and the tofu is very spicy.

google_reviews.author: Karim Abraham
google_reviews.text: Really good Asian food. They offer a menu each day for only 5.90€ and portions are pretty big. Totally recommended!

google_reviews.author: David S.
google_reviews.text:
When it opened, it used to be a great and friendly place, you were greeted by three smiling ladies and got a lovingly decorated bowl of food.
Unfortunately they became successful and now seem to make most of their money thorough the horrible, sickly sweet, deep-fried, plastic-packed junk food Chinese they took over.
Now Nam has a new, unfriendly waiter and a new cook; I had bone splinters in my Summer Rolls, and for the first time, the food tasted noticeably of MSG. This used to be a family restaurant, now it's a China junk food shop like all the others.

google_reviews.author: Nico Riedmann
google_reviews.text:
Best Pho I've had in Graz!
Very nice and comfy place too, especially when sitting outside.

Only thing missing from the menu is coffee

google_reviews.author: Katcha Bilek
google_reviews.text: There are not enough Vietnamese restaurants in Graz.. Nam is the most authentic, in both cooking and style of the place. Friendly staff. No messing. Great service. Easily the best Vietnamese in Graz.

google_reviews.author: Andrej Ecimovic
google_reviews.text: As someone who's been a regular for some  time, I can only attest to the other peoples reviews. This kebab shop has the best Döner in Graz. As a student who doesn't always have the time to make food, this kebab always came as a great lunch substitute, because of its sheer size. It never failed to deliver, it simply has the best taste. The bread is the main highlight as it's always freshly baked and the ingredients are always fresh. The sauces are also delicious. The other highlight is the owner; he's incredibly friendly and always looks out for his loyal customers... Shout-out to Ifri!

google_reviews.author: Ahmed Ehab
google_reviews.text: The best kebab ever .

google_reviews.author: G
google_reviews.text: Just great! Even if a whole bunch of students is waiting for their food the waiting times are still very short. I highly prefer this Kebap over the competition. Also, the prices are very reasonable and the staff and the owner are very polite.

google_reviews.author: Hendrik Bower
google_reviews.text: Tiny hut, great food. As well as the usual mild or hot kebab sauces, this place also does cocktail sauce and pumpkin-seed oil sauce (really worth trying). They also serve mini kebabs for €1.90. They cook the kebab bread fresh to order, even when it's busy. They do not pre-cut the meat when it's quiet to serve when it's busy (as is unfortunately becoming more common in Graz); meat is always cut fresh and to order. Outdoor seating surprisingly nice for a kebab place.

google_reviews.author: Mohamed Aboelyazid
google_reviews.text: High quality, great taste and friendly guys. Highly recommend.

google_reviews.author: Günter Ripp
google_reviews.text: Supper

google_reviews.author: Swen Butzh
google_reviews.text: I felt like I was back in Berlin 30 years ago. Just a pub, nothing more, nothing less, and the service was incredibly friendly. I'll definitely stop by again when I'm back in Graz!

google_reviews.author: Florian Stiebellehner
google_reviews.text: Upon entering this restaurant, the sight of the sausage salad shimmering in every color of craving in the refrigerated display case immerses you in a long-lost attitude to life. The nicotine-fogged glass doors are silent witnesses to a bygone yet sorely missed era. A glance at the milk cartons and the Doppler wine creates, even in times of scarcity, a comfortable feeling of security on the way home after an exhilarating evening of dancing by the rather lively band Alkbottle.

google_reviews.author: Irene Kosutnik
google_reviews.text: Very friendly and nice, even to old people.

google_reviews.author: Jasmin Branch
google_reviews.text: The waitress looked at us and cleared the table next to us, but didn't ask what we wanted. You don't even get a greeting here. After she'd chatted at length behind the bar and continued to successfully ignore us, we went across the street to the Kiss Bar.

google_reviews.author: Christina Pusnik
google_reviews.text: An absolute disaster – to be fair, this review is directed at the people involved; we didn't buy anything there. I took the liberty of placing an empty bottle I found on the bar – the elderly couple reacted extremely aggressively and chased me even though there were six small children present. Then, four police cars arrived at my daughter's birthday party because he had called and claimed a woman was causing a disturbance. Thanks to everyone present who witnessed this man's absurdity and quickly cleared everything up. It's a shame that Cineplexx tolerates this kind of behavior.

google_reviews.author: Haris N.
google_reviews.text:
TOP!
We've celebrated our daughter's second birthday party with her friends here now – and it was absolutely perfect again.

From start to finish, everything was perfect: The owners are warm, attentive, and genuinely care about each and every guest.

We also enjoy stopping by every now and then – whether before or after a movie – for a few relaxing drinks.

We absolutely don't understand the negative reviews. It's striking that many of them seem to come from guests of the restaurant across the street.

Instead of bad-mouthing others, you should put your energy into your own business.

google_reviews.author: Thomas A. PAL
google_reviews.text: A very nice place, attentive staff who were also open to special requests. The non-alcoholic cocktail was fantastic – incredibly delicious. Food rating = drinks rating. Free parking at the Cineplex parking lot. Highly recommended! Best regards

google_reviews.author: Huberta Sporer
google_reviews.text:
Great place. I always come here with my daughter before a movie.

The service is very friendly.

Cozy atmosphere.

I always enjoy coming here and highly recommend it.

google_reviews.author: Purgi
google_reviews.text: Good and fresh styrian food. I can recommend the lunch menu. Always tasty. The service is awesome.

google_reviews.author: Guenes Akin
google_reviews.text:
Review in a good beer mood – Steirer Box at Jakominiplatz:

The Steirer Box at Jakominiplatz is a place for honest food and real stories. If you want to eat like grandma used to cook, see top-rated chefs, pick up plum compote from your mom, or simply enjoy a hearty sausage from the farmer, stuffed peppers, or roast pork with roasted fat underneath – you've come to the right place!

Reds and blacks discuss personal sensitivities here endlessly; former footballers, whether red or black, still talk about it today – if they hadn't started drinking at the age of 12, they'd probably be in the starting eleven today.

Twins from the mafia are always there, but you can't mention their names!!

Twins have been arguing for almost, or sooner, 80 years; no one can tell them apart – not even artificial intelligence! Anyone who's gained weight immediately stands out!

A group of lawyers analyzes their negotiations, always offering free advice and reliving their long-gone student days here.

A kitchen salesman who never knows what he wants but always pays for everything is also a regular visitor.

And when the doctor is present, even Amir loses his sense of humor – and he cleans the kitchen!

A songwriter/comedian plays a card game called 'Dreier' (threesome) with one of the most stylish golfers his age and the shop owner, in a vulgar, witty, and lively way.

Conclusion: The Steirer Box is more than just the smallest stand at Jakominiplatz (so small that the architects of Minimundus couldn't replicate it). Nevertheless, there's a Carinthian regular who was a great soccer player and originally came from Minimundus, a super left foot player, or just so good!
Visit the Steirische Box - The Steirerbox - is a colorful mix of humor, real people, and honest food, a place where important stories happen and where you want to be right in the middle of it all!

google_reviews.author: mariia garcia
google_reviews.text: We've been eating and trying three of the sausages, accompanied by craft beer and delicious apple cider. The man was incredibly professional, offering helpful advice and serving us very kindly. We'll definitely be back. It was delicious!

google_reviews.author: Alexandra Pommer
google_reviews.text: Everything was perfect, the food was delicious

google_reviews.author: Gertrude Pichler
google_reviews.text: I would give 10 stars... but unfortunately that's not possible! Everything here is perfect: from the food to the staff!! The other guests are also pleasant and friendly. I love coming here!!

restaurants.name: Ginko
restaurants.summary: Hip, modern cafeteria offering a daily self-service buffet of international vegetarian fare.

google_reviews.author: Lorenzo Petrucci
google_reviews.text: I have been to this restaurant several times now. The concept is very appealing: every day there is a new buffet menu, and you can choose whatever you want and as much as you want, with the price calculated based on the quantity. All the food is vegan and very tasty. In addition, thanks to the spices used, the food is very colorful, which makes it even more appetizing. The staff are very friendly. I would particularly like to thank the waitress who recommended a delicious dessert today, which I had in addition to the Sacher cake I chose. The desserts are also vegan and very tasty. I highly recommend trying this restaurant at least once.

google_reviews.author: Pasquale Morrone
google_reviews.text: The food was delicious and the menu was varied. We were greeted by the young ladies who immediately made us feel at ease. Another plus was the warm welcome they gave our dog, Lulù. We absolutely must return.👍🏻👍🏻😁😁

google_reviews.author: N
google_reviews.text:
My taste buds have unique experience every single time I eat here. Or: How to turn anyone into a pure food hedonist without overpriced or unethical/unhealthy choice.
Hats off for every single detail and taste on the menu and in the restaurant generally.
You are my inspiration.

google_reviews.author: Vittoria
google_reviews.text: Really nice restaurant overall. It works as a buffet where you can choose whatever you like, fill your plate, then weigh it and pay based on how much it weighs. For two quite full plates and one sparkling iced tea without sugar, we paid €27, which felt reasonable for the amount of food. The space is big and comfortable, with another separate indoor area as well. There are also a few small activities for kids, which is a nice touch if you’re coming as a family. Just one thing to keep in mind: everything is vegan. We didn’t realize that from the Google reviews and only found out when we arrived. If you’re vegan, this is definitely the right place for you.

google_reviews.author: Martha Milch
google_reviews.text:
As an adventurous food-lover who follows the beat of the world’s best bites, discovering Ginko in the heart of Graz is like stumbling upon a vibrant oasis of vegan culinary creativity. Open, airy, and welcoming, the café floods with natural light, inviting you to explore its colorful, self-service buffet island—from crisp, seasonal salads to thoughtfully prepared warm dishes, all made with conscious care and priced by weight.

I delighted in mixing and matching a plate of beautifully fresh salads, hearty mains, and a fragrant soup—each bite felt honest and thoughtfully composed. Later, the pastry counter captured my heart: vegan cakes and tarts so luscious and delicious, each slice a testament to their in-house bakery’s gentle care and artisan craft. The minimalist décor and friendly staff only added to the cozy yet modern vibe.

True, the price-per-gram approach means your experience is as personal as your plate—but seeing how lovingly each dish is prepared, it’s a small price for such mindful indulgence. Ginko isn’t just a meal—it’s a celebration of vegan cuisine done with soul, style, and sincerity. A must-visit in Graz for conscious diners and anyone craving food that feels both good and great.

google_reviews.author: Manu Gupta
google_reviews.text: I love Nandos. It's tasty Mexican food, and just next to the University and Institute of Physics. Also inexpensive, and quick takeaways, unless you are there just after 12 ;)

Though if you are not a fan of fresh onions on pizza and prefer to have them fully cooked i’d suggest you order a pizza without onions because it did have quite a lot of them (i ordered the chicken pizza but the same goes for every pizza that has onions in it). I personally have nothing against it just beware if you are not a fan. The place itself is quite small but cozy and the stuff is really nice and welcoming. Definitely recommend!

google_reviews.text: I haven't actually been to the restaurant, just got delivery. But seriously, their pizza is amazing 🍕👑 The service is super friendly and professional, and the delivery is always perfect. My pizza is always hot, like it just came out of the oven!

The pizza is really good!
The owner is super friendly!!!
Highly recommend to everyone !!!

google_reviews.author: Sarah Troon

google_reviews.author: Kirran Arji

google_reviews.author: Danny Bùi
google_reviews.text:
Despite being labeled as a ramen spot, Nudelhaus leans more toward Chinese-style noodles than traditional Japanese ramen. The flavor and presentation reflected that, so it’s good to set expectations accordingly.

That said, I really appreciate that they stay open late, which worked out perfectly for our late dinner. The food did the job and satisfied our hunger, but there was nothing particularly memorable about the meal.

If you’re nearby and need a quick bite late at night, it’s a decent option, but don’t expect a classic ramen experience.

google_reviews.author: David Peter Fernandes
google_reviews.text:
Well as one other reviewer said its not really a ramen bowl its just noodle soup and boy i have never been this disappointed
We ordered the
1: Seafood Soup aka Ramen with seafood (homemade)
traditional Chinese dish with a type of pasta and a degree of spiciness of your choice
2: Ramen with beef (homemade)
traditional Chinese dish with a type of pasta and a degree of spiciness of your choice
3: Bao Zi (2 Stück)
gedämpfte Knödel mit einer Zutat nach Wahl
(We took this for takeaway again a disappointment since the pork stuffing was tasteless and the bao itself was well not baoing

1: The first complaint i have is that it took them more then 35 minutes to get the ramen bowl,
2: The Second Complaint is the seafood ramen well it was good on taste however it has three pieces of prawn and one small piece of fish and an omelette ??? Im not sure but i certainly dont think a ramen bowl contains a full fledged omelette. It also had three pieces of some kinda dumplings (tasted like pork) not sure .However lot of tofu pieces in the soup
3: The Third Problem i had was the noodles as you can see in the last image were like huge chunks of dough and not cut properly and were kinda undercooked in a way .

The Flavours were good i would not deny that and even the quantity is well satisfying and large, however doesn’t mean we can ignore the above factors.
Certainly would give a pass to this place not worth it

google_reviews.author: Nebojsa Simic
google_reviews.text:
Very authentic flavours.

I recently had the pleasure of dining at this hidden gem of a ramen noodles place, and I must say, it was a culinary adventure like no other. The authentic flavors at this establishment are a testament to the artistry and dedication of its chefs.

The Sichuan peppercorn-infused broth adds a delightful numbing warmth to the ramen, creating a unique and unforgettable experience for the taste buds. The homemade noodles, with their perfect chewiness, complement the broth beautifully, showcasing the attention to detail in every bowl.

But the surprises don't end there – the kimchi is a revelation. Bursting with bold and tangy flavors, it serves as an excellent accompaniment to the rich ramen. The gyoza, a side dish that deserves its own spotlight, are perfectly crispy on the outside and packed with savory goodness on the inside.

The ambiance is cozy and inviting, providing the ideal setting to savor each bite. The staff's passion for their craft shines through in every aspect of the dining experience, making it clear that this is more than just a meal – it's a celebration of authentic, mouthwatering flavors.

If you're a ramen enthusiast seeking an unforgettable taste journey, look no further than this establishment. It's a haven for those who appreciate the art of noodle-making and the bold, authentic flavors that make ramen an art form in itself.

google_reviews.author: Mr Schultz
google_reviews.text:
My expectations were sadly much higher.
The food: it was okay, the dumplings were bought premade (frozen) and not handmade in the restaurant… The hand-pulled noodles in the ramen were delicious, but the ramen itself was not good. VERY oily, tasted very bitter (had a weird taste, I ate muuuuch better ramen), beef was ok.
The waitress was literally ignoring me as I stood behind the counter wanting to order something while she was putting the frozen dumplings in the steamer…
Portions are big!

google_reviews.author: thomas butler
google_reviews.text:
Overall a great game!
Not the result I think we are all looking for but that’s just the way things go. Nigeria had a great shot at it but lacked the attacking quality in the final third. Argentina have shown time and time again that they are able to deliver when they need to. And they had the players that could produce something special when needed.
Food was also good

I have also tried the Cordon Bleu, one of the best in Austria. You can seat inside or outside, anywhere will be perfect. The only problem for non smoking people is that all restaurants people are allowed to smoke inside but all of them also have a non smoking area.

google_reviews.author: Jen

google_reviews.text: A long wait for a table not helped by people moving to other tables on the terrace without discussion with staff.  A delicious meal ruined by some very poor service.  Just one example:  a 15-20 minute wait for the wine list is unacceptable.

restaurants.name: Sweet Point

google_reviews.author: Adna
google_reviews.text: Honestly i expected more from Sweet point stand.The ingredients were fresh, I liked the strawberries, but there is too little filling so it was dry- and for kinder bueno they only give one chocolate bar-so 5 little pieces. For the price of 10eu-its not worth it.

google_reviews.author: Chrysi Chalkiadaki
google_reviews.text:
You should totally stop by for a sweet break!!!  We visit this place often in a  week and we are really satisfied with the taste and the quality of the products.
We have tried similar sweets in another places but I really have to say they are the most delicious and worthy their prices.
Their products are always fresh, the girls that work there are very friendly and we always take our orders in a few minutes.
This year we prefer a lot the vegan choices, and the new tastes of ice cream.
I totally recommend it. Don't miss it ,especially if you also have kids !!!

Ceramic dishes instead of one-way throw-away paper plates for eat-in would be appreciated (and would look zillions times better)

restaurants.google_name: Sweet Point Eis - Crêpes - Waffel - Kaiserschmarrn

google_reviews.author: Mike Meyer
google_reviews.text: The staff was very attentive and professional.  Our waiter provided suggestions on food and wine.  He did a pretty job of estimating serving sizes for our group of three.  I'm hoping to be back in Graz on future trips and will definitely make El Gaucho a priority.  Just be aware it is pricey, but I felt it was worth the cost.

google_reviews.author: Evangelos Koutsouradis
google_reviews.text: A nice restaurant for steaks. I took the variety of meat which unfortunately was really cold (I know it has to rest, but that was cold cold). While my wife's steak was better, warmer and more tasty! Service was OK.

google_reviews.author: Camila Rodríguez
google_reviews.text: We had dinner here recently and it was overall really good. The steaks were actually very tasty, much better than we expected. Solid experience.

google_reviews.author: Paul Matencio
google_reviews.text: The rocket salad was very tasty as well as the risoto side dish. The dry aged beef is also very good. The staff is very welcoming and professional.  Really nice ambiance. All in all it is highly recommended for people who like to eat.

google_reviews.author: Адекватный Доктор
google_reviews.text: Absolutely not, no way. The food isn’t good enough to put up with such bad service. I’ve been to this place twice. The first time, I managed to eat, but the waiter clearly didn’t want to serve me. He was pretty rude and aggressive. They brought me a ‘compliment from the chef’ but then charged me 4.5 euros for it. I paid, even though I didn’t order it. The second time, I went back the next day, and a different waiter greeted me at the door saying, “No, we can’t seat you inside, we only serve outside.” She was so unfriendly and unwelcoming that I didn’t want to stay at all. It was super hot outside, and the air conditioning was on inside – why don’t you respect your guests?

google_reviews.author: Wandern Leo

i went there today with a friend for the first time.
she doesn’t eat meat and had her troubles in choosing something.
i ordered a dish with meat, unfortunately, the woman behind the counter assumed that i also wouldn’t eat meat (i ordered a meat dish tho). so now i got a kg of fried platain 🥲 what do i do with that?

unfortunately a fail and i don’t think that i will try again.

the beans are really good tho :)

google_reviews.author: Ivo Marinovic
google_reviews.text:
Very nice and cozy place in the center of Graz. We came in late afternoon. It wasn't too busy The food was really great. If you are looking for home made food, look no more. Sir Demiri, you've earned my recommendation.

google_reviews.author: Luka Vrljić
google_reviews.text:
Nice restaurant close to the downtown, it is quite inside sou you can relax and enjoy your food. We had “Dim Sum” dumplings for appetizer they were fin. Spicy tuna is good, we had better but they are good, californian rolls with few different toppings were really good. Great thing is that rolls are very big, so one portion is really enough for one person, we took noodles on the side and they were average.
I really didn’t liked chopsticks because they are metal, very slippery and it was hard to use them because we are not every day in this kind of restaurant. However we are full and happy, but next time I will try some other restaurant. Thank you.

Me and a couple of friends decided to visit this restaurant. We were happy we had vegan options on the menu and the service was ok.
The waitress was a bit arogant and confused.
The prices are quite big and the portions are small. I would probably never come here again because of the prices.

google_reviews.author: Maja Gajić
google_reviews.text:
I like to come here from time to time. Food can be really good or average. This time I ordered Harumaki, Sweet Kochuchang Chicken (foto) and Jasmine tea. Harumaki weren’t fresh but I like the sauce that comes with it. However Sweet Kochuchang Chicken was really good. Especially the rice was well prepared. Tea is always good choices here.

Staff was polite and helpful.

google_reviews.author: Acisclo Murillo Dominguez
google_reviews.text: Food was very tasty and the bartenders were nice, would repeat

restaurants.google_name: Akakiko Sushi & Asian Fusion, Bibimbap, Kimchi

google_reviews.author: Daniel Herrgesell
google_reviews.text: Really nice staff, delicious "brötchen" and breakfast and little surprises from time to time

google_reviews.author: Bernd Brandstaetter
google_reviews.text: Really nice Service and Perfekt quality

google_reviews.author: Marcel
google_reviews.text: I'm absolutely thrilled! I picked up three small rolls for breakfast and was more than pleasantly surprised: not only are they the most visually appealing rolls in town, but their taste is also unbeatable. The attention to detail and the handcrafted production are evident in every bite – a true hallmark of a artisan bakery, far removed from any mass-produced goods. The sausage spread, the best I've ever tasted, was handmade, hand-sliced, and the quality of the ingredients speaks volumes. The cheese roll, generously topped and garnished with fruity, edible decorations, was a true taste sensation. The ham roll also brought back memories: hearty, delicious, and beautifully presented. A must for anyone who values ​​quality and taste!

google_reviews.author: Karin Boehm
google_reviews.text: These rolls are not only delicious, but also visually wonderful and very creative.

google_reviews.author: Stefan Schwarz
google_reviews.text: PICNIC BASKET - Thank you so much for the incredibly delicious and lovingly prepared picnic basket! You brought immense joy not only to the birthday person but also to me!! The basket contained a wonderful selection of delicious treats; the quality and service were exceptional! Keep up the great work, we'll definitely be back! (Due to the weather, we ate in the trunk of the car :))

google_reviews.author: SCHNELL & SAUBER
google_reviews.text: Reliable!

google_reviews.author: Tyrone01
google_reviews.text:
Disastrous hygiene, dirty place. Unfriendly staff. A person preparing a kebab touches his hair with hands, and then touches the dough???
At the end we didn't get the bill. The horror... Is it possible that this exists in Austria???

google_reviews.author: Martina
google_reviews.text: Kebab is really tasty and perfect balance of sauce!Staff is really friendly...will definitely go there next time I want Kebab!

google_reviews.text: Very tasty, they make fresh bread for every kebap 😋😋😋

google_reviews.author: Alexandru Basarlici
