The food was delicious, with a diverse and well-prepared buffet offering a variety of tasty options. The selection was impressive, catering to different tastes and preferences.

However, I opted for the grill option, and while most of the seafood was fresh and flavorful, I did encounter a few pieces that tasted off—possibly not at their best quality. It wasn’t a major issue, but it did affect my experience slightly.

Another downside is that drinks, including water, are not included in the price. Given the cost, I believe at least basic beverages should be provided. While the price itself is reasonable—not too expensive, but not cheap either—excluding drinks feels like an unnecessary extra charge.

Despite these minor drawbacks, I still think it’s worth trying, especially for the variety and overall quality of the food.

google_reviews.author: MJAlps J
google_reviews.text:
Amazing experience!
Food was delicious and the variety is more than enough.
All in all 10/10 The sushi is by far the best I've seen included in the buffet normally is only the little tiny rolls of rice and nothing inside but this restaurant is a real deal, the sushi is so delicious.
They have the chocolate fondue it's amazing. Our son had birthday and they prepared a cake with candle with a complimentary buffet for the birthday boy ! It was the best experience in a long time. The staff is so nice a friendly. We had a excellent time and we will for sure come back again and again. Thank you you made the perfect birthday for our son. This was simply Amazing.

google_reviews.author: Sash Mattix
google_reviews.text:
Lovely and beautiful restaurant,  staff is professional and friendly for all....
Chefs are amazing and they are very kind.
Food fresh and very nice tastes......

google_reviews.author: Artur Suchanek
google_reviews.text: Great place! You can try everything with the BBQ Buffet. You take the raw things on your plate and give it to the chef, some minutes later the waiter brings your dish to your table, and the best is that it is a All you can eat Buffet.

google_reviews.author: Zvonko Planinc
google_reviews.text: We go to Graz from Slovenija just to eat there , becuse there is the best all you can eat in round 100 km

restaurants.google_name: Cooking WU - Menu - BBQ Buffet - À La C a r t e

google_reviews.author: Ernst-Georg Lorinser
google_reviews.text: Only badminton place in Graz, but very friendly and caring staff - good to play.

google_reviews.author: Ondřej Haněl
google_reviews.text: Old changing rooms, dirty ground. It can be really hard to play for people with eye disabilities due to very bad lighting in the hall. But as far as I know, there is no better option in Graz.

google_reviews.author: Anil Tilanthe
google_reviews.text: Nice place, can check online slots and call for booking. Nice staff, a small cafe is attached. Was not crowded when I went and price are cheaper during day time.

google_reviews.author: Aditya Rana
google_reviews.text: Fast booking, friendly service and they even have good snacks if you are hungry. Would highly recommend this place for playing badminton.

google_reviews.author: Soujanyabc Raagni
google_reviews.text: Good but lighting arrangement is not so good light flash on eyes directly. It should have been with cool day light lights that doesn't flash on eyes directly.

google_reviews.author: Martha Milch
google_reviews.text:
I recently spent an evening at La Tavernetta, tucked away in Graz’s Old Town near Südtirolerplatz. The charming outdoor terrace, complete with umbrella-shaded tables and a backdrop of a baroque church, instantly transported me to an Italian piazza setting.

Inside, the space feels authentically Italian—warm lighting, rustic touches, and cozy seating that makes it feel more trattoria than tourist trap.

I began with a mixed seafood appetizer, followed by a Risotto Asparagi—creamy and fresh with tender asparagus. The Grigliata Mista di Pesce brought a generous platter of grilled sea bass, calamari, and seasonal vegetables, each component highlighting its natural flavors. To finish, I couldn’t resist the Tiramisu, light and classic.

Service was genuine and attentive—friendly without ever rushing me, suggesting wine pairings, and explaining dishes with approachable flair.

google_reviews.author: Tom Maier
google_reviews.text: We chose this authentic Italian restaurant for our special dinner, and it was a wonderful evening full of enjoyment. You can feel the original Italian flair and atmosphere from the friendly welcome. All the menus are fresh (fish), creative and delicious. Together with delicious Prosecco, Vino Blanca & Grappa -tutti perfetto!

google_reviews.author: Der Eisenvater
google_reviews.text: Very tasty Pizza Calabrese with Stracciatella. Really good Italian taste. Service was not too caring but still okay.

google_reviews.author: Marius Matei
google_reviews.text: The waiters are a bunch of SWINDLERS/THIEVES!! Although it's not exactly good manners, but, if you pay with cash, ABSOLUTELY COUNT the money/(cash) you get back (in change/rest) from those "waiters"/THIEVES: if you pay with large bills, they tend to "mince" the change (back) in the lowest bills possible (€5/10), and swindle you of €10-20!!

google_reviews.author: Cookie RFW
google_reviews.text: High-quality, traditional Italian cucina. Fresh seafood and pasta. Special wine selection and attentive and well-trained service team. Went here for celebrations and were very happy from the very start till the tiramisu. The location of La Tavernetta is nice for summer evenings outside as well as inside for the winter time.

google_reviews.author: Matteo T
google_reviews.text: One of the last traditional Cafes of its kind, if you have a chance visit it because it will not be here forever. We found it by chance, on a Sunday afternoon. You step in, and the clock goes at least 40 years back. I can recommend this if you’re interested in a travel in time, which is priceless. Do not expect fancy drinks or any variety in food.

google_reviews.author: Vladimir Khomenok
google_reviews.text:
Don’t fully understand how this place can exist.
It has a great history and a nice vibe inside, but they serve coffee in a cups that weren’t even washed:c

google_reviews.author: Rif Raf
google_reviews.text: The owner was rude and the coffee was the worst I've ever had. The cake also tasted stale.

google_reviews.author: Celil Göde
google_reviews.text: Food is stale and not clean.  Service is bad.  The employee is rude.  Everywhere smells of cigarettes.  Disgusting !!!  don't try it.

google_reviews.author: alice pereira
google_reviews.text: Very unfriendly service, dishes were not clean and people smoking inside. 1st and last visit.

google_reviews.author: Vedran Bajer
google_reviews.text:
Lovely location, very close to the city center, yet far enough to be in a really cool 'Platz' like place with a lot of other bars.

The food menu, whilst limited, was super tasty and a real pleasure to eat 🤤. Great coffee as well, great service and just a nice place to spend time in!

google_reviews.author: Patrick Galster
google_reviews.text: Favourite coffee and brunch place at Lendplatz, friendly staff und great selection of nibbles and drinks.

google_reviews.author: Dora M
google_reviews.text: We stopped here in the morning to have brunch. We loved the atmosphere. Breakfast was just enough and the service was great.

google_reviews.author: Candy Zimmermann
google_reviews.text:
Location: there's a farmer's market until 12 o'clock und lots of people chilling in the sun.

Staff: the jung one is friendly and funny. The old one doesn't seem to like his job.

Menu: they have alcohol and some Snacks

Bathroom: there's a public toilet in a separate container. It was awful

google_reviews.author: Raymond Shen
google_reviews.text: I sent a message by FB to the restaurant and asked if I can reserve for Tuesday night. Tuesday morning I got an answer that reservation is not necessary. 30 minutes later, when I passed by the restaurant, a panel on the door saying that the restaurant is closed for illness. I sent a second message by FB, the restaurant confirmed that it is true. OK. Tuedsay night, when I passed by once again the restaurant, I saw a table full of persons. I leave the judgement to you, simply feel sad not being able to enjoy a good meal. Maybe some misunderstanding, but there would be no next time.

google_reviews.author: Stan
google_reviews.text: Great location by Eggenberg palace main entrance. Nice outside seating area. Expedite service, very polite staff. Tasty food, and refreshing drinks.

google_reviews.author: Tobias Hödl
google_reviews.text: We had our Christmas Party there, food was amazing. The room had bad acoustic treatment so it was very loud. The Staff, while somewhat clumsy from time to time, was always available when you needed them.

google_reviews.author: Adam Pan
google_reviews.text: It's a pity that I visited this place, it looked nice and it's better not to feel this impression from the photos. First they told my wife and children to leave the area where the playground was. It's a pity because the children wanted to talk after a long journey. The food was very expensive, I was counting on quality. The child got to eat twice baked brown strips, very hard. He had a problem chewing them. Don't you see what you serve people at the table? Why ask after that how about food? Should I say no in front of the child, it's bad, take it from under his hands. ? My wife got an exceptionally thin schnitzel with mega head breading, with rice and jam? I don't know what tastes someone has but it was strange. A pancake with goulash and sirloin with pepper sauce were quite ok.

google_reviews.author: Marco Popescu
google_reviews.text: The food was great and the staff very friendly. Thanks

google_reviews.author: nikolaa987
google_reviews.text: Everything was great in Rudolf restaurant but I was amazed by waiter Sinisha. Professional, polite and fantastic server. Can't wait to be back there to be served by him. 👍🏽

google_reviews.author: Bernhard Lang
google_reviews.text:
We brought our daughter for her birthday to brunch and couldn’t have been happier. The owners treated us exceptionally, the brunch options were delicious and the location beautiful. We could not have been happier. Thank you for making this a special birthday!
We can highly recommend this location.

google_reviews.author: Sng Shao Hao
google_reviews.text: Lovely little cafe overseeing parts of the city. Very scenic

google_reviews.author: Tallulah Harvey
google_reviews.text: This was already one of my favourite venues in Graz, which is why it was the perfect choice as our wedding venue. I couldn’t ask for a more beautiful location! The food was incredible! We asked for it to be fully vegan and they did not disappoint (even the meat eaters were happy with it!). The staff were amazing, so kind and helpful. The owners were so helpful and enthusiastic. They really went out their way to give us our perfect day.

google_reviews.author: Johanna Wellems
google_reviews.text:
Super messy, especially the kitchen ( next to the toilet )which i think is  concerning. Asked for a coffee with oat milk. It looked appalling, I think the milk was old as it tasted sour.
Service by a young guy was ok. Nothing friendly or special though.

What a shame that the service doesn't keep up with the location, as the views are stunning and well loved in Graz.

google_reviews.author: Elena McDonnell
google_reviews.text: Attended a wonderful wedding here. Staff were friendly and accomodating. Food was tasty. The location was lovely at the top of a hill

google_reviews.author: Genc Demjaha
google_reviews.text:
Das Promenade in Graz is truly a standout destination and an absolute must-visit. From the moment you arrive, you’re welcomed by an atmosphere of warmth and elegance — thanks in large part to the exceptionally polite and highly professional staff. Their attentiveness, kindness, and genuine care for every guest elevate the entire experience and make you feel truly valued.

The ambiance of the venue beautifully blends style and comfort, creating the perfect setting for both a relaxed visit and a special occasion. Every detail — from the presentation to the service — reflects a high level of quality and dedication.

Visiting Das Promenade isn’t just a stop in Graz; it’s an experience you’ll want to return to again and again. Highly recommended

google_reviews.author: salma saab
google_reviews.text:
Visited for brunch and the food is very high quality. The portions are decent and the price is fair. It has a very nice atmosphere inside and outside. They have a variety of pastries and sweets, coffee with the option of lactose free milk or even plant-based.
I don’t give 5 stars because the service could definitely improve.

google_reviews.author: Volodymyr Dudko
google_reviews.text: The restaurant has a small menu, which you may think is nice, but reality is not so bright. The fish was okay, not dry (thank you), but not salty enough. The tastes within the dish were dull, a hint of acid could make everything more vivid, mushroom taste of porridge was weak. Fancy looking foam, which does not add to the taste. Overall it is okay, but not spectacular, the tartar was good though.

google_reviews.author: eni smolcic
google_reviews.text: Very nice and cozy place, we had a perfect apple strudel and tee.The waiter was very friendly and professional.

google_reviews.author: Rav
google_reviews.text:
The place is perfect for Breakfast, although there is not much variaty in the menu, food was good eggs with bacon and coffee was good.
The location is nice, next to stadtpark.
Would go again.

google_reviews.author: Daniel Ebner
google_reviews.text: I believe this Italian gem in Graz tends to be overlooked due to its location away from the city center, but it's one of the more authentic places you can find. The pizza and pasta options are great, drinks are fine and the service is prompt and friendly. When the weather allows it, you'll also find a generously spacious outdoor area available. Don't miss out!

google_reviews.author: Alina Radulescu
google_reviews.text: Even if we waited like 20 min until they took our order ( they had only 2 waiters and the restaurant was full ) , the waiting was worth it! Calamari misti were amazing! Long time I did not eat somewhere so profesionally cooked calamari ! Drinks are a bit too expensive though..

google_reviews.author: Dr. Dr. Immanuel Fruhmann MSc
google_reviews.text:
The Trattoria Siena is a superb Italian restaurant with an excellent location to soak up the sun. So its sun terrace is really something to keep in mind for the sun-loving folks here in Graz. The slogan of the Siena is therefore a piece of the Tuscany in the heart of Graz. Actually the location itself is in the northern part of Graz-Geidorf to be specific. The pizza here at the Trattoria Siena is very tasty and comes in large portions. Together with a Radler, this Austrian mix drink, half lemonade and half beer, I had the Mini Pizza, but it’s still big anyways. My Salami Pizza tastes delicious and reminds of the great pizzas I had in Italy. But of course they have pasta, seafood, and many other specialities from the Italian cuisine here. The staff is friendly and the WiFi works perfectly, and as mentioned already, the location is perfect for soaking up the sun.

From Graz with Love!
Dr. Dr. Immanuel Fruhmann

google_reviews.author: Phil Gillman
google_reviews.text:
Nice neighborhood spot with attentive service, a solid wine list, and good food.
Enjoyed the Calamari Misti, a pizza and salad.

google_reviews.author: Hammidah Shaari
google_reviews.text: Excellent food, I can say that it's one of the best Italian restaurant in Graz. Friendly staff, nice atmosphere, and very good location.

google_reviews.author: B B
google_reviews.text:
Very interesting place. This place is actually a giant coffee roastery with a cafe.
Very quiet and positive.

google_reviews.author: Abdallah Tawheed
google_reviews.text:
Cozy, comfortable, nice people, amazing coffee, the smell of it all in the air

No better place If you want to just hang out and relax

google_reviews.author: Darjia P
google_reviews.text: Unfortunately, both flat white and cappuccino were quite burnt :( maybe better luck next time.

google_reviews.author: Amine Kouki
google_reviews.text: Nice coffee. Impolite owner who screams at customers who are just trying to park in the dedicated area.

google_reviews.author: Aaron
google_reviews.text: Great coffee and atmosphere

google_reviews.author: Alpenninja
google_reviews.text: Blauschimmelburger = top!

google_reviews.author: Iulian Hordoan
google_reviews.text: Best in town 👍

google_reviews.author: Husein ElIsic
google_reviews.text: Just perfect

google_reviews.author: Martin Napetschnik
google_reviews.text:
A very friendly man took my phone order. About 40 minutes later, my long-awaited pizza arrived, and it was worth the wait. I've usually had black blisters or a blackened base on pizzas I've ordered elsewhere, which fortunately wasn't the case here.
The price-performance ratio was also very good.

google_reviews.author: Christa Maria Rux
google_reviews.text: Quaint inn, very friendly owners. Simple food, open in the evening.

google_reviews.author: Wilfrid Poglits
google_reviews.text: No food, but the beer is cold. Friendly host.

google_reviews.author: Maria Anita Koller
google_reviews.text: Was a really good event with nice line dance music

google_reviews.author: Gery J
google_reviews.text:
Current status as of March 27, 2024.

It was once a very renowned inn with excellent cuisine. Unfortunately, this ended with the death of the senior proprietor.

The establishment was taken over by the youth group and is run by them. It's also not suitable for pensioners looking for a mid-morning pick-me-up, as it doesn't open until between 1:00 and 2:00 PM.

google_reviews.author: Wilfrid Poglits
google_reviews.text: Unfortunately, there's no food at this inn. However, the beer is cold (as it should be). The host is very friendly and accommodating.

google_reviews.author: Jörg Lauritsch
google_reviews.text: Super Service 👍👍👍

google_reviews.author: David Richter
google_reviews.text: We stopped at Ulli's during our pub crawl. Ulli immediately made sure we got a good table for our group of 15. The drinks arrived quickly and were good. A very nice pub! We'll definitely be back on our next trip!

google_reviews.author: Leopold
google_reviews.text: Very lovely owner, good food, beautiful restaurant, highly recommended.

google_reviews.author: Nimra Nibor
google_reviews.text: Ulli is always very accommodating, there are always interesting people there and they also serve my favorite beer😬

google_reviews.author: Hermine Pucher
google_reviews.text: Great place, beautifully decorated. Anyone who knows Ulli knows she's simply fantastic at her job.

google_reviews.author: Juha-Matti Ruutu
google_reviews.text:
Awesome place!!
You can smell the history of this building!
Very nice backyard!
Food is very tasty, beer is cold and nice staff!
Cheers!

google_reviews.author: Boban Arsenijevic
google_reviews.text: Usually very good traditional Austrian cuisine, just once a little bit of a disappointment.

google_reviews.author: Boryana Slavcheva
google_reviews.text: Perfect food, the place is cosy and the staff is very welcoming. Worth trying if you are in town :)

google_reviews.author: Elton Bridges
google_reviews.text: Food is inexpensive here, so you can live quite cheaply.

google_reviews.author: Vincenzo Afferrante
google_reviews.text: Cozy and tipical Austrian restaurant. Recommended

google_reviews.author: Esra Fırat
google_reviews.text:
We enjoyed our pasta  everything was delicious.
Staff were friendly and atmosphere was comfy outdoors.

google_reviews.author: sabri durgut
google_reviews.text: Excellent treatment by the staff and even better food. Everything was exceptional and would recommend everyone to go if you want a pleasent experience.

google_reviews.author: Anisa Haziri
google_reviews.text: I recently dined at Don Camillo and was thoroughly impressed by both the exquisite cuisine and the amazing service. The staff ensured that our meal was an impressive one, providing excellent recommendations and ensuring our satisfaction. Definitely my go-to spot for pizza. Highly recommended!

google_reviews.author: delvina kukaj
google_reviews.text: If you are visiting Graz, I highly recommend eating at Don Camillo as it offers the best food, service and an amazing atmosphere. It checks all the boxes and I can definitely say that their pizza is the best in all of Austria!

google_reviews.author: Orhidea Mamaj
google_reviews.text: I really like this place, is one of my favorite places and one I look forward to eat in, every time I visit Austria. It’s definitely a place you should try, because I can guarantee you won’t be disappointed. The food, service and atmosphere is incredible!

restaurants.name: Sternwirt
restaurants.summary: Unpretentious guesthouse with simple rooms & free breakfast, plus traditional cuisine & local wines.

google_reviews.author: Elif Gokoglu
google_reviews.text:
We had an unforgettable stay at Gasthof zum Sternwirt.
The food was simply outstanding – authentic Styrian dishes prepared to perfection. Chef René Di Gallo is exceptional; he personally came to our table several times, explaining the dishes and making us feel truly welcome.

You can feel it’s a family-run place in the best way possible – warm, genuine, and full of heart.
We stayed overnight and the room was spotless, comfortable, and cozy. We were even traveling with our dog, and the team went out of their way to make her feel at home too.

A perfect mix of delicious local cuisine, heartfelt hospitality, and a relaxing atmosphere. Highly recommended! 🌟🌟🌟🌟🌟

google_reviews.author: Felix Rauch
google_reviews.text: We went there to have lunch. The food has recently increased a lot on quality. However, it still isn’t up to par with other restaurants but the area at the same price level. The atmosphere is traditional and the waiter were polite. I cannot judge the accommodation quality, since I haven’t stayed there

google_reviews.author: Gert Kopera
google_reviews.text: Always extraordinary.. Great, authentic, local fare prepared to perfection

google_reviews.author: Marcus Baker
google_reviews.text: Best food and service in Graz, steaks and salads are fantastic

google_reviews.author: Dejan Ciglar
google_reviews.text: Good food

google_reviews.author: Anna M
google_reviews.text:
Sorry to give one star, because the evening started out well with a nice view and delicious appetizers (for some of us at the table).

Booked a table for 8, they had no idea where to put us when we arrived. Than we had to wait an hour and a half (with little children) for anything other than drinks to arrive, which was again, main course for some a salad for others, the first course for the others never arrived. One person at the table received nothing but drinks after 2 hours of waiting. We ordered both sushi and schnitzel, the food that arrived was absolutely delicious ( 5 starts for the chefs) and the atmosphere and the view are lovely.

The main problem was the way the management handled the situation, telling us it will be done in a minute when it took another half an hour, and saying we actually received the mains all at the same time when we just did not.

One stuff member offered us free drinks to make up for the mess, than they put it on the bill anyway at the end, had to argue another 15minutes on leaving.
We left hungry, angry and disappointed, what a lovely birthday celebration.

Austro-Japanese fusion? More like absolute confusion!

This place needs new management, or any at all...

google_reviews.author: L. H.
google_reviews.text: We are long-term guests at the Landhaus and like it very much. The view is awesome and the waitresses are really nice. The Backhenderl is delicious :) Yes, the prices increased during time while the food on the plate got smaller - but I only noticed this after the Covid Crisis, so people have mercy with restaurants who had a tough time in the last 2 years and just try to survive.

google_reviews.author: Gerlinde Koebel
google_reviews.text: We made a reservation and arrived on time.  We were given a choice of tables and quickly ordered drinks and food.  Drinks arrived quickly,  but the food took 55 minutes.  My Gordon Blue was thin, barely had any ham or cheese in it and was dry. My mother's ribs were very dry.  My nephew's Asparagus and Prosciutto meal had a few spears of asparagus and almost no ham. It was covered in an Hollandaise type sauce. The fried potatoes looked delicious. All in all, disappointing and very untypical. Not sure whether or not we will return.

google_reviews.author: Tara Venditti
google_reviews.text: Excellent,  locally sourced,  delicious food. Fantastic service. Detailed menu options especially for those with allergies. Chef was very helpful to my allergy needs and offered great alternatives. View is amazing.

google_reviews.author: Hendrik Bower
google_reviews.text:
It has alright outdoor seating but that's really where the positive review ends.

€12 for a tiny piece of spinach and ricotta lasagne, barely big enough to be called a starter. It was really bland too.

Oh and a metric F-tonne of Burschenschaftler. I'm not taking points off for that as it's outside of their control (to some extent, anyway), but honestly, it did make the experience even worse.

restaurants.name: Dampflok

google_reviews.author: Thomas .Baumgartner
google_reviews.text: A casual place, great for a hot dog or burger. The prices are reasonable and the portions are very large.

google_reviews.author: Martin 29
google_reviews.text:
Thanks for the last few years 🖤🤍

Where should I go before and after the games now? 😪

google_reviews.author: Tom Cruise
google_reviews.text:
A fantastic, iconic bar that almost knocks you off your feet visually on your first visit. Since I came from the top of the stadium, I only noticed the graffiti on my way out. It's quite something.

The tip about the bar came from my neighbor, who's a regular and spontaneously invited me during a get-together. I love it, absolutely top-notch. I liked it right away. Very cozy, but also very atmospheric and sometimes quite loud, just the way it should be :))

It reminds me a bit of the sadly now-closed "Georgi."

I'll definitely be back soon, best regards.

google_reviews.author: Hubert Petautschnig
google_reviews.text:
This place is a must for football fans!

From the kitchen comes Mom's famous schnitzel sandwich, and the girls behind the counter are fun and friendly.

It's the perfect place to unwind after the action at the stadium!

google_reviews.author: Martin A
google_reviews.text:
Our team has been going to the Dampflok every day for lunch since the end of November. They offer a delicious lunch menu every day, as well as other dishes. It's hard to find a kitchen like this these days. They cook with home-style craftsmanship, with all kinds of dishes, and the friendliness is amazing.

We thank you very much for being there, and we'll keep going as you always do.

Martin and his team.

restaurants.google_name: DAMPFLOK - Café | Bistro | Bar - Der Treffpunkt beim Stadion Graz-Liebenau

google_reviews.author: Hèlios Sanchis Alepuz
google_reviews.text: The waiter is very cool

google_reviews.author: Hubert Schaupp
google_reviews.text: Here you can sit comfortably, meet for a drink, or even something to eat. Friendly service.😀

google_reviews.author: Eveline Zessar
google_reviews.text:
1. Ein besonders liebes Café mit liebevoll zusammengewürfelten Mobiliar und einem Traum von einem Schanigarten - der vermutlich beste in Graz, weil von Bäumen umgeben.
2. Köstlich das Essen, köstlichst die Brownies und die Süßigkeiten, vom Kaffee ganz zu schweigen.
3. Das Service ist ausgezeichnet und weil ich zwischendurch auch ein bisserl Ansprache sehr gerne habe: Immer wieder nett und unterhaltsam: Danke  Veronika, danke Joachim, danke Gernot.
Der langen Rede .... ich gehe schon jahrelang ins Zapo und gedenke das auch weitere Jahre zu tun: BRAVO !

google_reviews.author: Eva Was
google_reviews.text: Good food and friendly staff. Highly recommended.

google_reviews.author: Eva Maria Rath
google_reviews.text: Nice cafe with two lunch specials each, homemade, honest food at reasonable prices! The fruit juices they offer are excellent! The service is always friendly, the decor is charmingly cozy, and the restrooms are well-maintained!

google_reviews.author: Thomas Eibel
google_reviews.text: Good pizza, great atmosphere, fair prices. I'll definitely come back :-)

google_reviews.author: Reza Gulzari
google_reviews.text: The food is good and very fast.

google_reviews.author: David Moseley
google_reviews.text: From the outside this looks like a market stall, but inside you will find extraordinary food! I had the ten course dinner (modest portion sizes, but each entirely delicious .. make sure you book in advance) and would happily go back for the same dinner again. Really super food, presented well and very memorable.

google_reviews.author: Smelly soup
google_reviews.text: We just ordered the breakfast recommended by the lady at the next table! It’s just amazing!!!💝 so I dun actually know what’s the name of it🤣 but it’s like a fancy pizza for me~ The bread is chewy✨ all the ingredients are deliciously coming together!! We had a very good experience here✨

google_reviews.author: Vladimir Khomenok
google_reviews.text: I was pleasantly surprised to find such high-quality cuisine at the local market! Each dish was authentic and had some unique and interesting touches. All the food options are great, and I particularly enjoy Auslese as a drink. Overall, that is a must-go place!

google_reviews.author: Robert Seacord
google_reviews.text: Made a reservation online, but they didn't see it and sent the chef home so we weren't able to eat there.  Did get a recommendation for another place.

google_reviews.author: Benjamin Vis
google_reviews.text: Tiny place right on the market in a fixed stall that's atmospherically decorated. It only seats a small number, so make sure you make a reservation. The two course lunch (two options per course) takes produce bought at the daily market as their point of departure and delivers on flavour in a big and convincing way. The food is simple, but also simply delicious, and you will not be left wanting. I was also served a deliciously dainty moscatel. Certainly worth your visit.

google_reviews.author: Dan J
google_reviews.text:
The restaurant is located in an exceptionally picturesque setting, on the peaceful outskirts of the city of Graz, offering a stunning panoramic view of the entire city. From the restaurant’s garden, you can enjoy a charming panorama of Graz, especially at sunset, creating an unforgettable atmosphere.

The menu offers a unique combination of regional cuisine, traditional Austrian dishes, and selected international specialties, all prepared with the highest quality ingredients. The dishes are not only delicious but also beautifully presented, showing great attention to detail.

The service is polite, courteous, and helpful, gladly offering advice when choosing dishes and wines. It is clear that the staff care about guests’ comfort and strive to make everyone feel special.

I can fully confirm that all the positive reviews I had read about this restaurant are absolutely true. This is a place every gourmet visiting Graz should put on their list. If you are looking for truly great flavors, high-quality cuisine, and beautiful scenery, this restaurant is an excellent choice. I highly recommend it to anyone who appreciates outstanding food in a unique atmosphere.

google_reviews.author: johann mandl
google_reviews.text:
We had a wonderful evening at this lovely restaurant. We sat at a table in the garden which gave us a great outlook over the city. I can absolutely recommend the 5 course menu with the wine pairing. The staff are lovely and very knowledgeable, especially when it comes to their extensive wine list.

We will be definitely coming back!

google_reviews.author: Frank Simon
google_reviews.text: Always a must go in Graz. Wonderful view combined with excellent food, wine and a perfect service.

google_reviews.author: Sophie M.
google_reviews.text: One of our family’s favorite restaurants ❤️. Friendly and professional staff, very accommodating and of course the cuisine is hands down amongst the best there is. The wine selection is excellent and the sommelier and service team members are very knowledgeable. My kiddies (one is very picky) have a wide variety of options that they very much enjoy. They both look forward to the truffle pasta! This is one of the first stops that we bring our out of town guests and every time, they rave about their visit. Highly recommended.

google_reviews.author: Oskar Kubin
google_reviews.text: I arranged an event and asked them to cater one meat, one vegetarian meal. The food was incredible. Delicious cream of spinach soup, goulash or an incredible celery/pasta dish. intense flavours and beautifully presented. The staff couldn't do enough to be helpful and the location is stunning with lovely views over Graz. I couldn't recommend it enough.

google_reviews.author: Julian Zuschnegg
google_reviews.text: Best place to enjoy music, eat toast or fries and just meet nice people. I highly recommend. :D

google_reviews.author: Thomas Moshammer
google_reviews.text: Highly recommended! Cool place to have a good night with good music and drinks!

google_reviews.author: Martin Oisterschek
google_reviews.text: Love this place: friendly owners and staff, fantastic self-made fries and churros, good music and the best Gastgarten in the whole Stadtpark. And Hausbrandt-Coffee to go. Now all that's missing is a bar-dog, then I'll probably move in with them. Big recommendation.

google_reviews.author: markus polly
google_reviews.text: People, music, Drinks and Snacks are top, dont Miss IT because the government of Graz Tries to destroy all free minded cultural places!

google_reviews.author: Kim Jones
google_reviews.text: Good. The quality of the sound amplification sucks though

google_reviews.author: Agata Szabova
google_reviews.text: Honestly the best bar we’ve been to in Graz! The drinks were perfect as well as the atmosphere and the staff! We went back a couple of times during our trip because there’s no place like this in the city. Thank you!

google_reviews.author: Rie Inayoshi
google_reviews.text: This is a cozy and relaxing small cafe surrounded by greenery. The staff were very friendly and welcoming. I only had a cup of coffee and a cake, but that cheesecake was scrumptious! I would say the best cheesecake in the city :)

google_reviews.author: Vladimir Khomenok
google_reviews.text:
A cosy and neutral place: it doesn't add any new vibe, but supports your mood after walking along Stadtpark
The best for some desserts and a glass of wine

google_reviews.author: Hemanth Acharya
google_reviews.text:
Beautiful cafe, but the people serving you would prefer you weren't there so they can smoke outside.

I was here today, it's a wonderful autumn morning. Took a stroll in stadtpark, found this cafe and walked in because the door was open. I sat down. Nobody came. I waited for a minute and went to the counter, I thought perhaps they didn't see me come in. A girl who was smoking outside comes and shows me her phone and says "Its 8:58, we open at 9!" And walks off. I said ok, went outside again and waited. They opened at 9:01. The same girl  came outside, saw me and walked off.

Here are my suggestions :
- Have a smile, you are in the service business
- If you are closed and don't want anyone to come in, close all your doors. Photo shows the open door.
- Your cafe is literally empty, it's not like you were frustrated after a long hard day's work, which I would understand.
- If you really are all about time, you opened 1 entire minute late. You should probably apply leave and continue smoking at home.

It's a beautiful cafe and just a damn shame that it's run by such incompetent folks.

google_reviews.author: Kol2388
google_reviews.text: Great café, and really nice staff working there.

google_reviews.author: Arjuna Galvez
google_reviews.text:
Sweet baby Jesus, that was one of the best kebab I ever ate. Amazing fusion of flavours and textures.
The owner is really kind

google_reviews.author: Theresa Lee
google_reviews.text: This place is a gem. Great for a quick stop, they're open late, and their pide (boat shaped pizza) is delicious. Excellent shawarma and baklavas as well.  Falafel is out of this world. Friendly service. Thanks guys!

google_reviews.author: Jan Slavotínek
google_reviews.text: Perfect!

google_reviews.author: Anita Schiefer
google_reviews.text: Top!

google_reviews.author: Christian Turin
google_reviews.text: Top

google_reviews.author: Martin
google_reviews.text:
Getting to the restaurant is an adventure in itself – the steep, narrow drive up the Plabutsch is definitely not for the faint of heart. But once you arrive, you're rewarded with a stunning panoramic view over Graz that makes it all worth it.

The atmosphere is cozy, and if possible, you should definitely ask for a window seat – the view is the highlight. The staff are friendly and attentive, and the online reservation system works smoothly.

The food is solid and reasonably priced. It’s not gourmet-level, but it’s good and satisfying, especially paired with the view.

Conclusion:
A unique spot above Graz with a breathtaking view, warm service, and decent food – perfect for a relaxed lunch or dinner with a scenic twist.

google_reviews.author: Dejan Jancic
google_reviews.text: Place with very nice view and nicelly made. Due to bad experience would not recomnend to come for lunch if not having reservation made. we came without reservation, personnel placed us at the table (with option even to select place) and after 20 min waiting to be served, waiter kicked us out with excuse table has been reserved. Very unproffesional service that makes you feel bad for coming there at all, for having a lunch and supporting their business. Unfortunatelly, due to bad personnel, restaurant reputation is often ruined. As ex restaurant owner would recommend management to consider this review well.

google_reviews.author: MotoTouring World Wide
google_reviews.text:
The tiny nerve-wracking windy road up made me almost turned back, but when we got here it was worth it. The views are incredible, the restaurant is first class environment with wonderful atmosphere. Our waiter was very kind and made our visit worth coming back.
(Note to the owner, find out who and please remove or move the large Rock boulders on the tiny road in some spots, it serves zero purpose and accomplishes nothing,,, there's no room to move to the side of the road if another car is coming when there's a big huge Boulder there, plus it will severely damaged the car)

google_reviews.author: Gregor Verdovsek
google_reviews.text: The view from the top, especially in the evening, is truly stunning and worth the visit. Unfortunately, the food doesn’t live up to the setting: it’s quite ordinary and overpriced. Service was also underwhelming. Our wine was left sitting at the counter for 15 minutes, and I eventually had to fetch it myself. Overall, not terrible, but far from impressive.

google_reviews.author: Ahmad A
google_reviews.text: Definitely it’s a great place to relax after the hike. However, what I did not like is the prices. It’s pricey. I think prices should be adjusted. Overall, good experience to try.

google_reviews.author: Jyothish Atheendran
google_reviews.text: Went here to try some Authentic Austrian Food. I wasn't disappointed. The food was really nice. The interior of the restaurant looks really vintage and beautiful. The service was awesome and they take payments with card 💳. I can recommend this place.

google_reviews.author: Sandy Pantalon
google_reviews.text: Two of us enjoyed a very comfortable and peaceful night's sleep at this pretty hotel. It was a Sunday night and not possible to try the breakfast they say is included as the restaurant is closed on Mondays, so we had a nice coffee and pastry at a bustling bakery just a few minutes' walk away. Also enjoyed a nice walk along a nearby pedestrian path. Really enjoyed our stay.

google_reviews.author: Armin Hanzl
google_reviews.text:
Amazing service and food.
Reservations on weekends definitely necessary.
The waiters were very friendly and helpful, service was quick, the food was perfect, pricing is bit high but fair for the offered service.
Perfect for larger groups, birthdays, weddings, business meetings.

google_reviews.author: Dr. Dr. Immanuel Fruhmann MSc
google_reviews.text:
The Johanneshof is a great place in the northern parts of Graz. Its roofed patio is ideal to spend hours in and even when the weather is not ideal you can spend your time in fresh air while staying dry due to this roofed patio. So if you can get a table here it’s a wonderful spot for large parts of the year.

The building and its interior is light and bright in country style like an elegant Inn and the staff is friendly and willing to help.

The menu offers typical Austrian/Styrian food and the meals we had were delicious. One had the famous Austrian Tafelspitz, another had the chicken breast (of the corn-fed chicken) with mushroom risotto and another one of our group had the beef tartar with quail-egg in marinated ruccola, butter and white bread.

I had the Styrian Backhendl, a breaded chicken half, you can have with or without skin. I took it without skin, the traditional Styrian way.

From Graz with love!
Dr. Dr. Immanuel Fruhmann

google_reviews.author: Annemarie Karner
google_reviews.text: Beautiful place. You even get a drink shortly before closing hours.

restaurants.name: Stoffbauer
restaurants.summary: Laid-back hotel with country-style rooms offering continental breakfast & a cozy restaurant.

google_reviews.author: ChunkyCherry
google_reviews.text:
Absolutely beautiful location and an absolute perfect meal. Many people from Graz recommended this place so I naturally had to give it a go. The backhandl was apparently what people came for, so that I had to try.

I started with the fleischstrudelsuppe which was very tasty. The broth was nice and meaty with tons of flavor, and the dumplings were equally good. Meat was nice and spiced, and the dough was neither too soft nor chewy. A really nice way to start our meal.

I then had the famous backhandl, and with that I ordered a side of Knödel in gravy sauce. Let’s be completely honest, backhandl is basically fried chicken and that’s definitely not meant in a negative way. I’ve had fried chicken in the US and many other places, but this was without a doubt the best I’ve ever had. The coating was a solid 10/10, cooked perfectly! It’s not an easy thing to coat the chicken perfectly, and cook it equally perfect as well. It takes many tries and iteration until you’ve found the perfect coating for the chicken, and Stoffbauer really nailed that one. Each and every bite was crunchy, with and almost glassy like exterior. Really nice and flavorful.

The Knödel I had was also great. I love Knödel and make sure to try it every time I am in Austria, and this was one of my favorites. The gravy it was served it was rich, meaty and accompanied the Knödel perfectly. I would say it had a kind of demi glaze flavor to it.

Definitely a recommendation from me from now on too!

google_reviews.author: Alex Löffler
google_reviews.text:
Very quiet location in the woods of Graz. Classical austrian heurigen kind of style also reflected in the menu.
Good value for the price!

google_reviews.author: Muddy Mudganger
google_reviews.text: Hospitality is a virtue and the Stoffbauer Hotel & Restaurant undeniably makes you feel "like home". Unfortunately I could not take advantage of the garden, but the genuine Austrian fried "Händl" (a tender and crunchy chicken dish) is a must try if you just go to the restaurant. I recommend highly whenever you come to Graz. A great place to stay.

google_reviews.author: Thomas Wissmann
google_reviews.text: Great place close to the city but in the country side... Food lovers should try the deep fried chicken liver! A dish seldom available nowadays and very tasty here!

google_reviews.author: Dave Bruton
google_reviews.text: Fantastic local beer, coupled with a unbelievablely good fried chicken. The chicken is an absolute must here! Friendly service and great food, can't go wrong.

google_reviews.author: berke ayrancioglu
google_reviews.text: A restaurant fifty years old. Friendly staff, friendly environment. I ate a schnitzel, the food tastes good.

google_reviews.author: Patricia Schadl
google_reviews.text:
We came here on a whim.

We received a very friendly welcome and were given recommendations throughout the evening.

The food is very good and the portions are large.
They are attentive to their guests and make you feel very valued.
We will definitely come back.

google_reviews.author: Robert Werner Eisl
google_reviews.text: A very friendly owner with his heart in the right place, who also has a heart for paying guests with disabilities. The food is excellent. A little gem in Graz, a hidden gem. Open on Sundays.

google_reviews.author: Vanessa Jähn
google_reviews.text: The restaurant is quite hidden away. But don't let that put you off. The service is incredibly friendly. And the food was absolutely delicious and fantastic value for the price. We had the Wiener Schnitzel and the beef goulash, and it was both amazing. We'd definitely go back.

google_reviews.author: Chris Unterberger
google_reviews.text: We dined here one evening à la carte and once for a wedding buffet. Both times we were completely satisfied. The food is very good and the portions are ample. The service is very friendly and they accommodate special requests.

google_reviews.author: Yuli Restiani
google_reviews.text: Came in the morning and paid 8,50 EUR for a portion of fried fish and potato salad. I liked the fish dough that it's not as salty as most Austrian food I've tried. Dough is fluffy and fresh out of the kitchen.

google_reviews.author: Stefan Klampfer
google_reviews.text: Haven’t had such a tasteless, low quality fish ever. Really disappointing. They claim to have fresh fish from the sea, but if you’ve ever had fresh fish from the sea you know that’s not it.

google_reviews.author: Carlos Murillo
google_reviews.text: Staff is great! Very polite and patient. If you order for a specific fish you want, they try to find it and have it for you. I’ve ordered swordfish twice and they’ve managed to find it for me! Everyone is very kind! The food is also great!

google_reviews.author: Bruno Viti
google_reviews.text: The best of the best. All of the wildest and funniest nights start at Sofisch. Campari spritz + calamari fritti and si vola. Must do. Best place for an aperitivo in Graz

google_reviews.author: Kara Grellmann
google_reviews.text: Worst service ever - took 45 minutes for 2 portions of fries. Never again.

google_reviews.author: Dr. Dr. Immanuel Fruhmann MSc
google_reviews.text:
The Cafe Parkhouse is an institution for many decades in the Grazer Stadtpark (City Park), the main park in Graz, right next to the city walls of Old Town Graz. Here at the Parkhouse the artists and the liberal scene – if you know what I mean – is meeting up for a chat and to hang out. Also live music is performed here. The Parkhouse itself is a pavilion and features also a nice patio under big trees. The coffee is nice and the atmosphere as well with excellent opening hours (every day till 03:00 am). So the Parkhouse is a perfect spot to meet old and new friends, and from here you can enjoy the big Stadtpark as well, as the Parkhouse is located right in the center of the city park. The staff is friendly, the cappuccino and drinks are nice, and the view is great, since you look on the nearby medieval walls and the castle moat, filled with water, which creates a little pond, where you also can sit at.

From Graz with Love!
Dr. Dr. Immanuel Fruhmann

google_reviews.author: Berci Fóris
google_reviews.text:

google_reviews.author: Melanie Tanzer
google_reviews.text:
I celebrated my 30th birthday there on a whim (I'd never eaten there before), and everything was executed very smoothly and nicely, exactly as I had imagined. I was able to bring my own music (laptop and speakers) (Wi-Fi is also available) as well as my own cake. Most importantly, the food was truly delicious, and there was also a great option for the vegetarians among the guests. The chef is very friendly and competent, and he cooks excellently! The server was also very funny. :)
Parking is available, and it's also easy to get there by public transport.
So, I would come back anytime and highly recommend it to everyone!

Absolutely delicious burgers. They have perfected the craftsmanship of an exquisite menu and limited edition items. French fries, while very good, are small portion and slightly over priced. What can I say...there was absolutely not a long waiting time and the staff was extremely welcoming although obviously very busy. The food was AMAZING  - big portions, nicely arranged and extremely delicious- high quality for a very reasonable price. It is an absolute recommendation and we will be back for sure.

google_reviews.author: Christine Praschag
google_reviews.text: I've always ordered online here and have never been disappointed. The pizza is very good. Affordable and delicious. The service is fast, friendly, and, above all, honest. While other delivery services that still offer cash try to rip you off, I've always enjoyed absolute integrity and customer friendliness at Happy Burger. That's worth a lot these days.

google_reviews.author: Hollyfut
google_reviews.text:
"I've been a regular customer for years, but what I recently experienced is simply unbelievable. My neighbor wanted to pay for the order but didn't have any cash. When I called the restaurant to explain the situation, I wasn't even given the option of paying the amount next time – something that's perfectly normal in other restaurants.

Instead, I was told to walk several kilometers to the nearest ATM immediately, and I was seriously threatened with police involvement if I didn't. My neighbor then borrowed the money from another neighbor. I've never experienced anything like it – this audacity and complete lack of customer service are truly unbelievable. Treating regular customers like this is a disgrace.

As for the food: The quality has become simply abysmal. It used to be acceptable, but the burgers are now sickening, and the pizza tastes like frozen pizza. Other customers' experiences confirm that I'm not the only one who feels this way. The delivery driver wasn't responsible for this incident and received a tip from my neighbors, but the management is completely unacceptable to me." Unacceptable.

I hesitated for a long time before writing a review, but after this experience, one thing is clear: I will never order from here again. Such behavior is not only customer-unfriendly, but also damaging to business.

google_reviews.author: Hamdi Hajdini
google_reviews.text: I ordered a cheeseburger here that gave me the worst stomach upset of my life. Shortly after eating, I got severe stomach cramps, followed by diarrhea and vomiting. Obviously, either the meat or one of the ingredients was spoiled. This shouldn't happen! I will never visit a restaurant that handles food like that again. Absolutely not recommended.

We spontaneously visited this little pub today. The interior and exterior have a very nostalgic feel. It brings back fond memories of times gone by 😉.

google_reviews.author: Mario Marbler
google_reviews.text: The fried chicken is exceptionally good; I recommend taking it with you in winter, as the ambiance inside isn't particularly captivating. The outdoor seating area, however, certainly has its charm in summer.

google_reviews.author: Irmgard Feichtinger
google_reviews.text: Equally recommendable cuisine for vegetarians and vegans, with excellent pastries at reasonable prices; salad buffet very good.

google_reviews.author: Kevin Schmid
google_reviews.text:
We had a 30s party in the outdoor area with a set dinning menu. 3 soups, 3 main dishes and some dessert were offered. Some of the guest and the host are vegetarian and some are vegan. The kitchen was informed in advanced so that 1 soup and 1 main course could be prepared to be vegan.

Upon ordering, a lactose intolerant guest received the vegan soup with cream in it. Mistakes happen and the soup was returned. However, the same soup came back and they just spooned out the cream. For such a pricey location, this is just unbelievable. After that, the vegan main dish for the same person arrived topped with Parmesan. Fortunately this dish was redone completely. Other issues included not receiving a couple ordered soups, long waiting times for drink orders and smug comments about non-alcoholic beer orders.

The outdoor seating is nice and the other waiters were more courteous but all in all, our experience was not good, especially for that price. Therefore I can't recommend this restaurant.

google_reviews.author: スズメ Sundown

I spontaneously decided to visit this restaurant, even though I rarely go there. The pizza, however, won me over immediately. The crust was thin, fresh, and perfectly baked in a wood-fired oven – exactly how a good pizza should be. The taste, quality, and preparation were consistently excellent.

If there's one thing I can recommend without reservation, it's definitely the pizza here.

The price was also very reasonable. I will definitely be back and can recommend this place without reservation.

I had the opportunity to try the pasta and pizza; both were fresh, delicious, and beautifully presented. Definitely an underrated place. I highly recommend it 👌

I also ordered sauerkraut which came warm, unexpected but wow, so good! Only thing surprising was that they don't accept cash, so we had to go and get some! Would be nice if there were signs to avoid uncomfortable surprises!

google_reviews.author: Michaela Wallnerová

google_reviews.author: Petar Chernaev
google_reviews.text:
Don't go to this place if you don't speak Deutsch!We had our menus only in Deutsch! The waiter was nervous,not polite especially when he saw that we are strangers ( not locals) We didn't know what we are going to eat ,he told us he is in a hurry ( he had 5 tables to serve) then we got some strange food ,he forgot our salads ,finally after feeling like stray dogs we wanted to pay and we have been told " It s an old restaurant,we accept only cash "
Disgusting place !

google_reviews.author: Mario Presser
google_reviews.text:
The 2nd day we ate there, our baby had to change the diapers. My wife checked it in the corner of the restaurant if the diaper was full and called me to bring the diapers and wipes.

The waiter asked us to go to the toilet which anyway we usually do . There was no changing table for babies and no space in the ladies toiled, but we changed it in the man toilet as the tabke was better and packed everything in the plastic and cleaned the area.

Next day as we came for lunch they asked us if we changed the diapers yesterday and asked us very unfriendly and rude to leave the restaurant as changing diapers is not allowed.

Well done to the management, happy not to support your establishment ever again.

google_reviews.author: Krisztian Mark Bakos

restaurants.summary: Charming inn offering terrace dining, a stone wine cellar & a bakery, plus landscaped gardens.

google_reviews.author: Tom Hofer
google_reviews.text: The location is very nice, the room was very basic and the bed basically a couch with a mattress. Several dishes weren’t available anymore after we ordered and the service was a bit slow especially when our guests arrived it took a long time to get the first drink

google_reviews.author: Lisa Chakrabarti
google_reviews.text: Fairly standard food in quite a pretty spot, surrounded by forests. It feels like they are very sure of their customers....a little brusque when taking the tables order. The waiter suggested that payment could happen at the table but much preferred at the desk. Normally fine, but in this case our host is a ninety year old fairly sprightly gentleman with a walking cane ...the waiter could have been much more considerate (we didn't get even a small smile from him the entire time). He led the way at quite a pace to the desk inside!

google_reviews.author: Emily Vesta
google_reviews.text: Overall good.  Dinner was disappointing.  Nice and quiet, plenty of free parking on site.  Room was comfortable but small.  Went down the road to the Kern Bakery for an excellent breakfast instead of eating at the hotel.

google_reviews.author: Марта Егорова
google_reviews.text:
Friendly staff, good location, great parking! We were offered a huge apartment instead of a regular room! The hotel is located near the tram stop, 13 minutes walk! You can get directly to the city center without transfers! very convenient! forest nearby! I want to come back to this place! I recommend!
Показать перевод

google_reviews.author: Gordon Parkinson
google_reviews.text:
Food was brilliant, but service was a problem. Staff are friendly until your tip is not enough. Also staff are unaware of vouchers purchased as a gift. Security on these needs to be improved much like a concert ticket so it can be scanned and cancelled when used. We are not talking about 10 euro here..  The voucher does have all info necessary (details, code etc) but was still questioned. Shame, spoiled an otherwise enjoyable evening.

190823
We visited again with family, but issues still persist.
Booked table for 4 at 6pm but they booked it down as 6 persons at 4pm. (They did confirm it was 1800 when we booked).
Waiter then pointed at the book?? We didn't write it in!!!
Waiter then forgot a drinks order then seemed arrogant and literally threw the drinks on the table.
The food is good there but the staffs attitude requires attention..
I know they can be busy but that's the business they are in. I'm afraid that will be the last visit for a very long time.

restaurants.name: Gasthof Pfleger

google_reviews.author: Minola Sandor Nagy
google_reviews.text: It is a quaint little place perfect for a quick food break! You have a decent menu to choose from at the counter and then you can sit down either on the table row or in the back at a normal, square table. The food is done very quickly and the price is decent as well!

google_reviews.author: Natan Rosenfeld
google_reviews.text: I come here every time I am in Graz. Great prices, friendly staff,  tasty food and generous portions. If you are looking for cheap Chinese food that always hits the spot then I would recommend this place.

google_reviews.author: M. J.
google_reviews.text: HORRIBLE! The place is super close to my place, so I tried it twice. The first time I got the chicken rice box, which is cheap, tasteless and super oily... The other time I really felt like hot and sour soup and got two of them to go -  when I got home and took them out of the bag I saw that both cups were not even half full... And the price for the soup (which was not really good anyway) was already higher than the one other Chinese places offer. Never again, I'd rather walk 20 min to another restaurant to get some decent food...

google_reviews.author: Dietrich
google_reviews.text: Excellent food, good selection, and the staff is very helpful in catering to special requests. The noodles are made in a way slightly different to the standard, but they are all the more enjoyable for it.

google_reviews.author: Ramona Irgolic

google_reviews.author: Dorijan Šipuš

google_reviews.author: Ralf Assmann

google_reviews.author: Christian H.
google_reviews.text:
Beautiful Event location combining historic and modern facilities at the foot of a hill.
Excellent food from the kitchen. In the city but all views into the green. Good as well for workshops, seminars.
Easy to find and get there by car or bicycle.

google_reviews.author: Roman Blagsic
google_reviews.text: This is one of the best restaurants in Graz area. The food was very tasty and service was realy friendly. The prices are reasonable and the indoor rooms are spacious also the seating is cozy. Highly recommended! Also for the dog owners, who are very welcome.

google_reviews.author: Matija
google_reviews.text:
The service was very poor. We ordered a bitter lemon, but I received plain water instead, along with a Coca-Cola that was served in a half-liter glass and was already flat. The waiter does not speak English and only came to our table once, when he brought the food. The meat was bad, and the quality is very questionable considering the taste and smell. Overall, I would not recommend this restaurant to anyone.
And yea hahaha there is no bill

google_reviews.author: TomBecX
google_reviews.text: Nice and fast service, very good food!

google_reviews.author: Branislav Pika
google_reviews.text:
I really wanted to enjoy staying in restaurant. Mesl was just fine, not best but acceptable.
I did not enjoy music in restaurant, it was some kind of techno house which made my feeling sad that it is not typical Greek restaurant as described in describtion. I recommend to change music to something similair to greek culture.
Also staff was not very friendly, I would expect more from them.
So 3dtars are fine, not best, not bad just fine.
I recommend chicken soup, that was delicious.

google_reviews.author: nihar ranjan
google_reviews.text:
I recently had the pleasure of dining at Restaurant Dionysos, an authentic Greek Flavours and I must say, it was an exceptional experience from start to finish. From the warm and inviting ambiance to the impeccable service, this restaurant left an indelible impression on me.

Let me start by highlighting the highlight of my meal – the soup! I opted for their signature [SEAFOOD SOUP], and it was nothing short of a gastronomic revelation. The broth was rich, aromatic, and perfectly seasoned. Every sip tantalized my taste buds with a delicate balance of herbs and spices. The ingredients were fresh and sourced with evident care, as each spoonful was bursting with vibrant flavors. The soup was served piping hot, exactly how I prefer it, ensuring every mouthful was an indulgent delight. It was evident that the chef had mastered the art of soup-making, and I couldn't help but savor every spoonful until the very last drop.

Moving on to desserts, I was in for a sweet treat that surpassed my wildest expectations. The menu featured an enticing array of desserts, and after much deliberation, I choose LOUKOUMADES DESSERT. It arrived beautifully presented, with a combination of artistic flair and attention to detail that impressed me. As I dug my spoon into the dessert, I discovered layers of pure bliss. The flavors harmonized flawlessly, and each bite was an explosion of taste and texture.

Thank you DIONYSOS Team,
Surely I will visit next time

google_reviews.author: Anhuar Lopez Rodriguez
google_reviews.text: Amazing place to eat, I went for luch which was cheaper and for the price is a lot of food you can get. The service is very kind and we got a very quiet place to sit, but some of the food (fried things) is quite greasy. Overall is a 18 eur sushi so the quality is as such. I would go there again for lunch no doubt.

google_reviews.author: Janel Zechner-Leonor
google_reviews.text: The concept is really nice, however the quality of the food changed to worse. They are now experimenting quite a bit with the sushi rolls, sauces, spices, which is nice, yet in some cases the use mayo which ruins the taste. Also lately they are making huge sushi rolls which you can’t fit in your mouth.

google_reviews.author: Theresia Scheiflinger
google_reviews.text: They do have vegan food, but it is not clear to all the service ppl. Best would be if it is correctly written on the menue. And they might put non vegan food beside vegan food. So better order separately.

google_reviews.author: Juan Campos
google_reviews.text: Super good experience. The avocado, tuna and salmon are really tasty, not like in other asian restaurants where they are tasteless. I went on Tuesday of last week and on Monday of this one. And I will be back.

google_reviews.author: Linda Wieser
google_reviews.text:
Be aware that the sushi sizes are enormous, never have I ever seen a sushi this giant. The point of eating out should be to enjoy and taste a lot of different food and dishes, but this really was not possible here.
Then they put some strange sauces on top of every single roll, which we really didn’t like at all and unfortunately they were not shown on the pictures so there was no way of avoiding them.
Its really sad because the fish quality itself was actually quiet good to our surprise and also the cooked food was made well!
The service itself and the atmosphere is also really outdated and simply not enough to keep people coming back. The price also, definitely too high for the experience and quality you get.

I love Chris! Never had a waiter who did his job that well!!

google_reviews.author: Dr. Dr. Immanuel Fruhmann MSc
google_reviews.text:
The Posaune is the central meeting point at the campus roundabout between the Karl-Franzens-University and the famous Zinzendorfgasse, the main street for the student's life in Graz. The Bausatz-System is also omnipresent here, since the Posaune is part of the chain of the Grazer student restaurant, where you choose all your toppings on a basis of toast, pizza, spaetzle pan, or salad. So you are in full control over your ingredients as it is super transparent here, what you getting in the end. And the prices are very reasonable, since the target group are the students of Graz.

The seating is nice as long you are in the patio in the Zinzendorfgasse and sunny in the afternoon. Inside the Posaune it is a pub atmosphere and allowed to smoke. So if you are sensitive to smoke it's better to stay in the patio and enjoy your meal in the sunshine.

We had a spaetzle pan with pineapple and blue cheese, another spaetzle pan with feta, corn, beans, and salami, as well as a pizza with feta, olives, corn, and salami and another pizza with prosciutto, corn, mushrooms, and chilli pepper. All these dishes are very delicious and interesting combinations.

From Graz with Love!
Dr. Dr. Immanuel Fruhmann

google_reviews.text: We loved the choices of food and it's pretty good quality too. The staff were friendly even when it was busy and we liked that food is served late. Only downside is that smoking is allowed indoors. Would recommend trying the "pfandl"

restaurants.name: Bauernwirt
restaurants.summary: Modern rooms in a casual lodging with fine dining, a bar & a garden, plus breakfast.

google_reviews.author: Toni J
google_reviews.text: I only visited the restaurant. It has the old and classic Austrian "Gasthaus" interior and feeling. The food was delicious and the service was very friendly as well as attentive. Though the restaurant is rather outside the city center, it is definitely a visit worth.

google_reviews.author: Tom Maier
google_reviews.text: This is a family-run restaurant and hotel. The hotel offers a variety of well-maintained rooms. A rich breakfast is served to hotel guests. Restaurant visitors enjoy a changing lunch menu throughout the week. And in the evening, there's a selection of excellent, regional, seasonal dinner. When the weather's nice, there's a cozy outdoor dining area. The service is particularly charming and attentive. It's perfect for any occasion, enjoying fine dining and staying overnight while visiting Graz (the bus stops right outside).

google_reviews.author: berke ayrancioglu
google_reviews.text: A great cute old and local restaurant/hotel(We didn't stay, we had only dinner). It is located a bit far from the Graz center but if you are interested in local restaurants and food, I would suggest trying Bauernwirt. We ordered the mixed plate(I forgot its name) which was meant to be for 2-3 but it was 3-4. We barely finished although we didn't have any starters and we couldn't try any desserts because we were already too full. Prices were reaso and the employees were friendly.

google_reviews.author: Biagio Pagliarulo
google_reviews.text: A superbly pleasant stay. Floral ambiance adds to the warm country feel and welcomed hospitality. The food is more than tasty and the breakfast will get you up early to not miss it. I will be back.

google_reviews.author: Nick Vos
google_reviews.text: Bad food. They spilled our drinks and we had to pay for it she said "your problem" also very salty fries. Also very expensive

google_reviews.author: Markus
google_reviews.text: Typical local Austrian meal, amazing desserts. Huge portions, what do you need more.

google_reviews.author: Katarzyna Derkacz Sertori
google_reviews.text: On our way back to Italy with our two daughters and our large dog, we chose this spot for a stopover. It was an outstanding experience: excellent food and incredibly thoughtful, friendly service. We recommend it with all our hearts!

google_reviews.author: Martha Milch
google_reviews.text:
I recently dined at Knusperhäuschen, a welcoming Austrian inn tucked into Graz’s Straßgang district. The ambiance hits just the right notes—warm wood, stone accents, and floral curtains that evoke a loved-for local favorite.

I began with a creamy Frittatensuppe (pancake strip soup), followed by a golden Wiener Schnitzel paired with crisp fries—classic, comforting, and just the right balance of crisp and tender. Their daily special—Leberkäse with Rösterdäpfel—delivered a rustic punch of flavor that felt like home in every bite. Portion sizes were generous, and prices felt fair.

Service was genuinely friendly and unhurried. The staff checked in thoughtfully without interrupting the cozy rhythm of the place. It was the kind of experience that makes you linger with a second coffee before leaving.

Knusperhäuschen feels like a living postcard from Austrian tradition—wholesome, warm, and reliably good. It’s a great go-to if you want genuine, hearty comfort food in a homey setting. I’m already planning my return—next time for their Käsespätzle or a relaxed afternoon in that sunny garden.

Let me know what Linz spot you'd like to explore next—or if you'd like a snapshot-style review of another Graz gem!

google_reviews.author: Veneta Petkova
google_reviews.text: Fantastic place, everything was delicious, I strongly recommend the garlic soup, best soup I have ever tried.

google_reviews.author: Rafael Kovacs
google_reviews.text:
Food was good, drinks too. All the personnel were EXTREMELY nice and kind, about over 9000 :D
The only bummer is that they accept only cash - there's an ATM near, in the ÖMV shop.
And: this was the best non-hommade tiramisu I've ever had!

Recommended, but have some cash.

google_reviews.author: Erick Garcia
google_reviews.text: I came to this place as soon as I arrived to the city for the first time. Eat all you can for 10.9 euros + beberage. They have a good variety of chinesse food, also sushi, and deserts. And the personal was very kind...

google_reviews.author: Tom Reksodipuro
google_reviews.text: Very good for groups , owner is very nice , food  are decent , nice parking , one of my favorite layover place in austria for big group

google_reviews.author: Pumamori
google_reviews.text:
I'm a regular, or used to be, and it genuinely hurts to say I just can't recommend this place anymore. Some days it's excellent when the right people are present, but sometimes it's just so, so rough. Raw chicken, horrible stomach ache & sickness afterwards, the drinks are super expensive but also a coin toss on whether they're good on any given day.

I want to love this restaurant like I used to but I don't like gambling for my own well-being.

google_reviews.author: Fabian
google_reviews.text:
Excellent experience to eat there!
I've been a fan of them for many years and some time ago they added a buffet and it's magnificent! So many different choices and everything tastes great! Especially the duck is extremely well done which is not usual at a buffet! They offer a big variety of desserts as well, even a chocolate fountain!
Service is also great, the owners are always very friendly! Prices are okay.
Looking forward to eating there again!

google_reviews.author: True Sunshine
google_reviews.text: Came here twice on our way to Italy and back, during lunch hours and the food is great. Second time we made a reservation because the place is so popular that it's full during the weekend. There is much to choose from, salty or sweet and they even have sushi. They have a large parking and it was easy to find a spot. For me, it's the perfect chinese restaurant.

google_reviews.author: Amela Stankovic
google_reviews.text: The food is excellent, always fresh and tasty. Delivery is fast and everything arrives hot. Highly recommended!

google_reviews.author: Dominik Polák
google_reviews.text:
Absolutly delicious pizza.
Very short waiting time.
Nice place to visit.
Waitress is also English speaking.

google_reviews.author: Mahesh Yalagach

The crust was perfectly crispy, the toppings were flavorful and fresh, and the overall quality of the ingredients was evident. If you're looking for a cozy spot with tasty pizza, this pizzeria is worth a visit.

google_reviews.author: Camille Malsang
google_reviews.text:
Amazing ! Tasty, generous and the pizza dough was delicious (normally I don’t eat the end of pizza but this time I did!).

Thank you Daniele for the warm welcome.

Cam & Alex from France

google_reviews.author: Elena Chirieleison
google_reviews.text: I m Italian and I have to say that the Pizza was really good and the service was super efficient.I ask for potatoes becuase I couldn t find them on the menu and they litterally give me for free because I had already paid,super sweet I really appreciated it.

I’m very happy about it and will definitely return when stopping in Graz. I need to give some compliments to Ms NADINE - she is very sweet, very kind and always gives an excellent customer service. Thank you Nadine. I recommend this place without any reservation - Aida has the best coffee and Punch cake in Austria, you should try it 🌸

google_reviews.author: Aleksandr Kuchinskii
google_reviews.text:
Probably one of my favorite places with noodle.
Tasty and good portion size. The small one like at the picture costs 7,9€ (for me it's enough even if I am very hungry). In my opinion much better and chipper then macdonalds located next to it.
The only thing they have only 3 small tables to sit in, but take away and eat outside always an option :)

google_reviews.author: Cam Red
google_reviews.text: The food was great it tasted really nice and authentic! The portion was a decent size for the price. It’s definitely a good deal for a train station where food is usually overpriced. I recommend! Also the staff seemed lovely :)

"Osmanthus Wine still tastes the same as i remember"

google_reviews.author: Nancy Nyambici
google_reviews.text: Great food. Lovely ambience. Did I say, "really good Iranian food"? The cleared plates should tell the story. Fair pricing. We came mid afternoon so can't really complain service was slow as that is an awkward time.

google_reviews.author: Anastasiya Sergievskaya
google_reviews.text: Good place. But be careful with Persian restaurants: the food is always delicious, the portions are quite big, so skip the breakfast if you are going there for lunch :-)

google_reviews.author: Nina Midlil
google_reviews.text: Antor Paar Sudhaus is a fantastic choice for a refined dining experience. The restaurant boasts a sophisticated ambiance, with elegant decor and a warm, inviting atmosphere. The food was beautifully presented and absolutely delicious, with high-quality ingredients and well-balanced flavors that made every dish enjoyable. We visited for our Jahresauftaktfeier and the setting was perfect for the occasion. The service was outstanding; attentive, friendly, and professional, ensuring that our evening was seamless from start to finish. Overall, Antor Paar Sudhaus delivers both excellent cuisine and top-tier service, making it an ideal spot for special occasions or a high-end dining experience. Highly recommended!

google_reviews.author: Gabriel Gruber
google_reviews.text: Four of us came with a reservation in the evening during the week. It was busy, but there were still a few seats available as the restaurant is very large. We had two different spreads with sourdough bread as a starter, which was excellent. We sampled the in-house beers, with the ‘Das Altsteirische’ being my favourite. For starters, my companions had a pumpkin cream soup, which was good, and a lamb's lettuce salad garnished with fried bacon and eggs (excellent). For the main course we had a pulled pork burger which was ok, gnocchi with pumpkin cream and cress. A Styrian chicken salad and an onion roast with potatoes and bacon beans as a side dish. The roast onion was good. I prefer slightly thicker pieces of meat (can also be smaller and not completely pounded flat) and lots of sauce (little things). The sauce was excellent, the fried onions were good, but not enough for an onion roast. The service was extremely friendly and polite. We would love to come back!

google_reviews.author: Sebastian Nier
google_reviews.text: Outstanding!!! It was recommended to us while traveling, and we regret not being closer! Everything tasted amazing. The chefs know their stuff. Congratulations!

google_reviews.text: There are many Japan cusine in Graz, me and my wife choose this as it close to our hotel, bit dissapointed as the Ramen is instand noodle (or may be something similar, but not Ramen at all), my beef raman more like beef satay instant noodles from Hong Kong. google_reviews.author: Herosanshow Tutorials (Herosan)
google_reviews.text: Nothing like Osaka, a city where we come from. They use instant noodles for their ramen, and tastes nothing like Japanese ramen. The sushi was ok. Pretty expensive compared to another restaurant Haru around the corner with probably better quality!!!

google_reviews.author: Christoph unknown
google_reviews.text:
The quantities are okay but the price is not justified due to the poor quality.
The sushi had little flavour.
The ramen is instant ramen from the Asian supermarket. You don't need to go to a restaurant for that.
The hygiene conditions are in great need of improvement. The chefs handle and cut the meat without gloves. All the chopping boards are made of plastic and provide a habitat for all kinds of bacteria.

google_reviews.author: Valeriia Tishchenko
google_reviews.text:
I want to share my impressions of a wonderful Ukrainian café! 🍽️ The food here is simply fantastic: I ordered the classic borscht with lard spread – very delicious, and I especially enjoyed the "Mimosa" salad. They also made me a pizza, which was excellent as well.

The staff is very friendly and attentive. It's particularly nice that you can pre-order any dish you like – you don't have to choose something from the menu; the staff is happy to prepare exactly what you want.

There's convenient parking, just like you'd expect in a city, so no problems at all. The café is also conveniently located near a transport hub and is easily accessible by bus. The atmosphere is cozy and intimate, almost like being at home, and they offer takeaway – very practical.

I'd especially like to highlight the friendly staff and the convenient payment options – both card and cash are accepted. It's a real pleasure to come here, enjoy the atmosphere, and try the delicious food!
